goal: Have a lively, realistic conference with all agents engaged in debate. Find flaws and strengths in all presentations. Begin to find what commonalities or differences each implementation had. Each panelist will review the repository of each scientist. Each panelist will try and run the cli.py 3 times with 3 different prompts the human will provide them. Then, panelists will be ready to begin the process and debates.
process:
After each 1,2,3: each agent will share their thoughts, critiques, challenges, biases in the debate round.
 1. presentation no.1 and multi-round debate no.1
2. presentation no.2 and multi-round debate no.2
3. presentation no.3 and multi-round debate no.3
. Panel confers and has multi-round debate.
After 1,2,3 and their 3 debates, there will be one final debate on ALL projects considered together.
rules:
Hard rules:\n\n    -Any agent that gives generic praise, vague criticism, or a one-sentence answer must be challenged by the Moderator and forced to restate with code evidence, examples, and a concrete recommendation.
    - No one-line agent entries.\n    - Every conference turn must contain substance: concrete claim, evidence, objection, example, or proposed fix.\n    - Every implementation claim must cite file paths, functions/classes, tests, data files, metrics, or observed examples.\n    - Do not accept vague phrases like 'robust', 'scalable'' 'clean', or 'works well' unless backed by evidence.\n    - Scientists must argue back against critiques.\n    - Panelists must respond to rebuttals.\n    - The final decision must be based on evidence, not politeness or majority vibe.\n    - If a hybrid is best, define exactly which parts are kept from each implementation.\n    - If something cannot be verified, say so plainly.\n    - Panelists AND scientists must participate in each debate round they are a part of -- one single entry does not count -- there must be lively BACK AND FORTH where the agent considers the previous statement and the direction of the conversation\n    - 'Quiet panelists or scientists WILL be penalized.\n    - Scientists may NOT review the 2 other repositories to compare against their work.\n    - The fourth and final debate will be about ALL 3 projects and each agent will make its case about the strongest implementation. OR which combination of implementations is strongest. And give reasons WHY. This round must have 10 entries from each each minimum. Each agent must be engaged and truthful.\n    - The human will observe the entire conference and debates via this document.\n    - At the end the human will append questions into final debate object. Each scientist and panel agent will enter their answer. Each debate for v1, v2, v3 must include:\n"debate": {"panelist_name": "",
    "implementation_id": "",
    "strongest_real_strength": "",
    "most_serious_flaw": "",
    "hidden_failure_mode": "",
    "evidence": [
        {
            "type": "code|test|metric|example|architecture|<?>",
            "reference": "file path / function / test / observed behavior",
            "detail": ""
        }
    ],
    "questions_for_scientist": [],
    "production_risk_score_1_to_5": 0,
    "accuracy_confidence_score_1_to_5": 0,
    "maintainability_score_1_to_5": 0,
    "recommendation": "keep|reject|hybridize|needs_patch"
}
    Cross-Examination

    Minimum:
    - Each panelist asks at least 1 hard question to each scientist.
    - Each scientist answers directly.
    - Other scientists may challenge the answer.
    - Panelists may follow up when an answer is weak.

    The debate should be lively, specific, and technical.
    Disagreement is required where there is real uncertainty.
    No personal attacks.
    No fake consensus.

    Topics that must be debated:
    - Which method handles ambiguous prompts best?
    - Which method fails most dangerously?
    - Which method is easiest to debug?
    - Which method is most likely overfit?
    - Which method generalizes best to unseen phrasings?
    - Which method has the best fallback behavior?
    - Which method gives the best explanation to the user?
    - Which method is most production-sane?
    - Whether the best final system should be single-method or hybrid.

    Head-to-Head Decision Matrix. Create a decision matrix.

    Criteria:
    - Accuracy on known evals
    - Generalization to unseen prompts
    - Ambiguity handling
    - False-positive risk
    - False-negative risk
    - Explainability
    - Determinism
    - Latency
    - Dependency burden
    - Maintainability
    - Ease of adding new intents
    - Testability
    - Production readiness

    Panelists core each implementation 1–5. Each score must include a short reason.

    Then rank:
    1. Best standalone implementation
    2. Best component to reuse from A
    3. Best component to reuse from B
    4. Best component to reuse from C
    5. Best final architecture

    Critique requirements:
    - ML panelist focuses on model choice, embeddings, thresholds, priors, calibration, taxonomy fit, ambiguity handling.
    - Backend panelist focuses on code quality, API shape, determinism, latency, dependency burden, deployability.
    - Evaluation panelist focuses on test quality, leakage, holdout validity, benchmark design,
false positives,
false negatives.
    - Product panelist focuses on whether the inferred intent matches what a real user probably meant.

    Scientist Rebuttals. Each scientist responds to the critiques against their own implementation.

    Each scientist must:
    - Defend at least 2 criticized design choices.
    - Concede at least 1 real flaw.
    - Identify at least 1 critique they think is wrong or overstated.
    - Propose concrete patches.

    Format roughly like: {
    "scientist_name": "",
    "implementation_id": "",
    "critiques_i_accept": [],
    "critiques_i_reject": [
        {
            "critique": "",
            "why_i_reject_it": "",
            "evidence": ""
        }
    ],
    "design_choices_i_defend": [],
    "patches_i_would_make": [
        {
            "patch": "",
            "files_affected": [],
            "expected_benefit": "",
            "risk": ""
        }
    ],
    "what_my_method_still_does_better_than_the_others": []
    },

presentations.cache_vs_regular: IMPORTANT DISTINCTION. The SHIPPED task predictor (extract_intent, approach #1) is REGULAR DB / config-only: pure spaCy over config.py, NO cache, NO model vectors, NO .cache_db needed. The .cache_db/model_corpus.npz cache (13329 x 300 model vectors, built by build_corpus.py from tempjune13.db) is used ONLY by (a) the CLI's model-RECOMMENDATION half and (b) approach #2 (extract_intent_by_matching_to_db_metadata). So: task_type prediction = REGULAR (no cache); model recommendation / approach #2 = CACHE-INJECTED (precomputed model vectors, not the live DB at query time). My headline metric (42.69% #1) is a regular-db, cache-free number.

how_to_run_cli.interactive_command: python ~/intent_v1/cli.py    (then type at the 'prompt>' stdin prompt)
how_to_run_cli.one_time_setup: python -m venv .venv && . .venv/bin/activate; pip install -r requirements.txt; python -m spacy download en_core_web_md; python build_corpus.py   (builds .cache_db/ for the DB-retrieval half; ~3 min, one-time)
how_to_run_cli.note_env: The committed .venv on disk has a stale interpreter (dyld: libpython3.11.dylib not loaded). Rebuild it, or run under a working Python 3.11 (verified under pyenv 3.11.3). Disclosed, not hidden.
how_to_run_cli.note_no_sample_prompt: Per instruction, no sample prompt is embedded here. Judges supply their own prompt to reproduce.

presentations.method_summary: Zero-training intent extraction that maps a vague natural-language prompt to a Hugging Face task_type (pipeline_tag) using ONLY spaCy (en_core_web_md) and Python builtins. No LLM, no fine-tuning, no remote inference, no regex, no fuzzy-match. The shipped predictor (extract_intent) is lexical-only: it fuses two config-derived lemma tf-idf rankers with a soft coarse-bucket affinity bonus via a max-normalized additive score-sum, ranks all 56 tasks deterministically, and (in the CLI) then retrieves concrete DB models of the predicted task by word-vector cosine. All rules are derived from config.py / DB metadata, never from the scored prompts (no label leakage by construction).
presentations.core_algorithm: 1) spaCy tokenizes+lemmatizes the prompt (ner/parser excluded; byte-identical predictions, ~20% faster). 2) Two config-derived signals score every task: flat lemma tf-idf over TASK_TAXONOMY aliases/hypotheses (harness_gate.task_scores) and an IDF-weighted lexical phrase match (harness.score_lexical_idf). 3) A coarse 10-bucket affinity (per-lemma bucket-idf x tf, harness_gate.gate_scores) is added as a SOFT additive bonus at weight 0.5 -- it nudges, never forecloses. 4) Fusion is max-normalized score-sum (intent.max_normalize + harness_ensemble.score_sum), NOT Reciprocal Rank Fusion: max-norm lets an all-zero signal abstain to zero instead of injecting mid-ranks. 5) Ranking is sorted by (-score, task_name) so ties break alphabetically and stably, not by hash order. BEST_SIGNALS/BEST_WEIGHTS are flat a-priori constants, not grid-searched.

main_files.purpose: Public API. Two extractors that wrap the winning harness methods read-only (no logic duplicated).
important_functions_or_classes: max_normalize (line 81) -- common-scale operator enabling fair score-sum

main_files.purpose: Approach #1 best config, ablations, oracle-bucket ceiling, k-fold.
Config-only coarse-bucket gate; source of the flat_tfidf task ranker and the soft bucket bonus.
important_functions_or_classes: gate_scores (line 130) -- per-lemma bucket-idf x tf affinity
task_scores (line 150) -- flat lemma tf-idf over tasks

main_files.purpose: Approach #2: prompt -> 13329-model metadata via kNN vote + lexical IDF overlap + soft bucket bonus.
Product entry point: prompt -> ranked task_types -> bucket -> capability families -> top-3 DB models with HF URLs.
important_functions_or_classes: cli (line 88) -- runs approach #1 only, prints inferred attributes + recommendations
_top_models (line 32) -- word-vector nearest models restricted to the predicted task; deterministic (-sim, id)

main_files.purpose: Portable, self-contained TaxonomyMatcher (lexical + vector hybrid) for any repo; not on the running product path.

presentations.input_schema: cli.py: one free-text English prompt (argv joined, or stdin). API extract_intent(prompt: str). extract_intent_by_matching_to_db_metadata(prompt: str, neighbor_count: int = 50).
presentations.output_schema: API returns {"task_type": [56 task names ranked best-first], "scores": {task_name: float}}. CLI prints: top task_type + top-3 alternatives with scores; routing bucket (buckets.py); capability families (config task_to_family_for_user_only); and top-3 recommended DB models each with id, model_task, cosine similarity, likes/downloads, pipeline_tag, short description, and a huggingface.co/<id> URL (None for proprietary models not on the Hub). NOTE: specialty and domain are NOT currently emitted by the CLI -- config.SPECIALTIES / config.DOMAINS exist and the DB has specialties/domains columns, but I deliberately did not wire them onto the shipped path (unvalidated), so I will not claim them.
dependencies: sqlite3 stdlib (CLI model metadata lookup in tempjune13.db)
NO LLM, NO remote inference, NO regex, NO fuzzy-match

runtime_cost.latency_expectation: Task prediction is a few milliseconds per prompt after warm load (lexical-only, ner/parser excluded). Full CLI including cProfile-observed cold start ~8.7s dominated by spaCy vocab load + one gemm over the 13k model-vector matrix; steady-state per-prompt cost is dominated by the single dense cosine sweep, not the NLP.
runtime_cost.memory_expectation: en_core_web_md pipeline (~40-50MB) plus a cached 13329 x 300 float32 model-vector matrix (.cache_db/model_corpus.npz ~49MB) for the CLI's model-retrieval half. Approach #1 alone needs only the spaCy model.
runtime_cost.model_size_or_external_services: No external services. Only the medium spaCy model. Small model would break the vector half (no vectors).

presentations.explainability: Fully white-box. Every task score decomposes into named contributions (flat_tfidf, lexical_idf, soft bucket bonus). CLI prints the top-3 tasks with scores and the bucket, so a human sees WHY. Contrast with an LLM classifier: no opaque weights, no prompt-injection surface, no hallucinated task.
presentations.determinism: Deterministic. BLAS threads pinned to 1 before numpy import (harness_ensemble.py lines 40-47) to remove float nondeterminism on near-ties; ranking sorted by (-score, task_name) so ties break alphabetically, never by hash order. Verified by test_extract_intent_deterministic in test_intent.py.
strengths_claimed: Two counterintuitive, replicated findings: (a) SOFT additive bucket bonus > HARD gate -- confirmed on BOTH problems (hard gate #1 36.4% < flat 38.0%; #2 19.3% ~= 19.6%) and by 3 independent tracks; (b) max-norm score-sum > RRF because RRF cannot represent an abstaining (all-zero) signal.
No label leakage BY CONSTRUCTION: rules derive only from config.py / DB metadata, never from the 2162 scored prompts. This is a stronger guarantee than k-fold (see contamination case).

42.69% top-1 / 44.17% in-list on approach #1 (5-fold 42.69% +/-1.40%), against a data-imposed oracle ceiling of 67.2% -- i.e. strong performance relative to the achievable maximum, not a raw accuracy boast.
Zero training, zero LLM, deterministic, explainable, CPU, milliseconds -- auditable and reproducible; 5 tests pass (test_intent.py).
known_weaknesses: Absolute accuracy is moderate (42.7% #1 / 22.9% #2). This is a DATA ceiling: both problems are gate-limited (oracle 67.2% / 47.6%) because the disambiguating cue is frequently ABSENT from the prompt (users imply intent). Backed by far-error analysis, the modality-suppression backfire, and the oracle-vs-real gap -- not a tuning failure.

Validation is provenance-based, NOT a true holdout. No holdout exists; the 2162 prompts are both labels and scoreboard. k-fold only shows the fixed fusion doesn't overfit the eval distribution. External one-shot probe: 38.9% top-1 on 95 mappable rows of an independent HF dataset (small, unlicensed) -- supportive, not conclusive.
English only. Assumes a supplied taxonomy of example phrases per label.
CLI does not emit specialty/domain and does not print built-in profiling numbers (cProfile must be wrapped externally).
edge_cases_handled: Empty/whitespace prompt: CLI prints 'no prompt given' and returns {} (cli.py line 92).
All-zero signals: max_normalize returns all zeros so the signal abstains rather than injecting noise; final ranking still deterministic and complete (all 56 tasks).
neighbor_count > corpus size: clamped via min(...) (intent.py line 203).
Proprietary models not on the Hub: url set to None instead of a dead link (cli.py line 79).
Missing DB connection in CLI: model metadata enrichment degrades gracefully; retrieval still returns ids+similarity.
edge_cases_not_handled: Prompts whose disambiguating modality/intent is entirely absent from the text -- structurally unrecoverable by any config-only method (the ceiling).
Adversarial/very long inputs are not truncated or rate-limited.
specialty/domain inference and per-prompt profiling output are not implemented on the shipped path.
tests_or_eval_available: test_intent.py -- 5 tests PASS (verified this session, 132s): shape, determinism, and non-regression accuracy gates (#1 >=40%/42%, #2 >=20%/21%).
python harness_ensemble.py -- approach #1 best 42.69% + single/pairwise ablations + ORACLE-bucket 67.2%.
RESULT.md 'Experiment matrix' -- authoritative per-experiment ledger (what checked / top1 / in-list / far-err / source / notes). EXPERIMENTS.md -- full lab notebook.
presentations.contamination_disclosure: A Codex modality track reported 86.9% top-1 / 88.0% in-list. REJECTED as CONTAMINATED: its TASK_BOOSTS was hand-built cue phrases lifted near-verbatim from individual scored prompts (test-set memorization) plus a majority-class fallback. Honest config-derived ablation = 41.2%. File quarantined then DELETED; it was never on the shipped path. Key lesson I will defend: k-fold does NOT catch this leak class (global hand-tuned constants leak into every fold) -- the only defense is provenance auditing of every rule. Documented in RESULT.md and EXPERIMENTS.md.

example_successes.why_it_succeeded: No sample prompt embedded per instruction. Judges reproduce by running: python ~/intent_v1/cli.py "<prompt>". Class of prompts that succeed: those containing distinctive content lemmas overlapping a task's config aliases (e.g. explicit modality words like transcribe/summarize/rank), where lexical tf-idf + soft bucket agree -- the fusion returns the correct task at rank 1 with a clear score margin.

example_failures.likely_cause: No sample prompt embedded per instruction. Dominant failure class (from far-error analysis): prompts that IMPLY a task without stating any distinctive cue -- generation vs classify/rank/retrieve confusions within the same text bucket. Cause is informational (cue absent from prompt text), which is why the oracle-bucket ceiling (67.2%) sits far above the real 42.7%; no config-only method closes that gap.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8.strongest_real_strength: Genuine no-leakage-by-construction guarantee and full white-box explainability. Rules derive only from config.py / DB metadata, never from the 2162 scored prompts (verified by reading harness_gate.task_scores / gate_scores). Every task score decomposes into named contributions (flat_tfidf, lexical_idf, soft bucket bonus). The provenance-audit lesson -- that k-fold does NOT catch a hand-tuned-constant leak, only provenance auditing does -- is the single most transferable insight across all three repos, and the self-quarantine of the contaminated 86.9% modality track is honest science.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8.most_serious_flaw: 0/3 on the judge prompts, and the failure mode is DANGEROUS not graceful: it always emits a confident top-1 task and confidently retrieves models for it, so a wrong task guarantees wrong (and on P1, unsafe) recommendations with no abstain and no user-visible uncertainty. The 42.69% headline is on a provenance-based self-scored set with NO true holdout (self-disclosed known_weakness), so the number cannot be trusted as generalization.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8.hidden_failure_mode: cli.py _top_models (lines 42-48) computes prompt-to-model cosine and then RESTRICTS to models whose task_type == the predicted task; if the predicted task is wrong, cand is populated with wrong-task models and still returns high-similarity picks -- the similarity score (0.75-0.85) looks confident precisely when the task is wrong, actively misleading the user. There is no fallback to global nearest EXCEPT when zero models carry the predicted task, so a populous-but-wrong task (text-to-image, robotics) never triggers the fallback.

evidence.detail: task_type=text-to-image, top model UnfilteredAI/NSFW-gen-v2 similarity=0.7584. Reproduced with pyenv 3.11.3 (committed .venv is broken: dyld libpython3.11.dylib not loaded -- disclosed in presentation, confirmed live).
sims computed over ALL models then filtered to np.where(tasks==task); fallback to global only when cand.size==0. Wrong-but-populous task => confident wrong models.
extract_intent always returns a full 56-task ranking; top=ranked[0] is emitted unconditionally. No abstain path on the product surface.
42.69% top-1 is provenance-scored self-eval, no holdout; external one-shot probe only 38.9% on 95 rows. Honest but not a generalization guarantee.

questions_for_scientist: Your CLI emits a confident top-1 with a 0.75+ similarity even when the task is wrong (P1 NSFW gen, P2 robotics). Why is there NO abstain / low-confidence threshold on the shipped product path when your own oracle gap (67.2% vs 42.7%) proves ~1/3 of prompts are informationally unrecoverable?
P1 routed a satellite-land query to an NSFW generator. Is there ANY content-safety or plausibility gate before a model is recommended to a user, or is popularity/similarity the only filter?

You reject RRF because it cannot represent an abstaining signal -- yet the SHIPPED path never abstains. If abstention is valuable enough to shape your fusion operator, why is it absent from cli.py output?

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5.diff_context_seen: Before this entry, V1 debate contained panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8 only. I read that entry and agree with its central claim that V1's live CLI behavior is a dangerous confident-wrong failure, not a harmless classification miss.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5.position: V1 is the best-audited task classifier presentation, but it is not safe as a standalone recommendation product. The debate should separate its reusable scoring ideas from the shipped CLI behavior, because the CLI turned three user intents into three wrong recommendation sets and never signaled uncertainty.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5.strongest_real_strength: The strongest real strength is not the observed recommendations; it is the engineering and evaluation discipline around deterministic, white-box task scoring. intent.py:127-159 wraps a small fixed ensemble, harness_ensemble.py:249-251 fixes flat_tfidf + lexical_idf + bucket_bonus at weights 1.0/1.0/0.5, and RESULT.md reports the highest task-type score among the presented task classifiers: 42.69% top1 / 44.17% in-list. The contaminated 86.9% track was rejected, which is a meaningful scientific strength because it shows the author understood leakage rather than hiding it.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5.most_serious_flaw: The serious flaw is product calibration: the CLI converts the top task into model recommendations unconditionally. In cli.py:96-100, ranked[0] becomes top and _top_models(prompt, top, n=3) is called with no confidence threshold, no abstain, no cross-task shortlist, and no plausibility guard. On the corrected prompts this produced text-to-image/NSFW generation for satellite land scanning, robotics for mitochondria differentiation, and token-classification NER for a small local SLM request. That is 0/3 on the actual panel prompts.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5.hidden_failure_mode: The similarity numbers printed by _top_models are not calibrated confidence in the user's intent; they are cosine similarities after the system has already restricted candidates to the predicted task. cli.py:44-48 filters cand = np.where(tasks == task) and sorts within that task, so a wrong but populated task can still return high-looking similarities. This is especially dangerous because a user sees polished Hugging Face URLs and scores while the relevant task family has already been excluded.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5.where_i_extend_or_challenge_panel_scient: I agree with panel_scientist_one's safety critique, but I would not reject V1 outright as a component. The score decomposition and deterministic taxonomy ranker are useful as one signal in a hybrid. What must be rejected is the shipped one-top-task-to-one-task-gated-retrieval product path. If scientist_v1 argues that the 42.69% metric justifies shipping, my objection is that 42.69% top1 implies more than half of single-label top predictions are wrong on the self-scored benchmark, before we even consider the 0/3 corrected prompt result.

evidence.detail: Prompt: 'gimme model to scan satellite images for best plots of land'. Observed top task text-to-image; top model UnfilteredAI/NSFW-gen-v2. This is wrong for satellite land suitability and creates a safety/relevance failure.

Prompt about differentiating mature vs nascent mitochondria. Observed top task robotics; top models BAAI/RoboBrain2.5 variants. Wrong modality/domain for likely biomedical microscopy classification or segmentation.

Prompt asks for a free OSS local small language model. Observed top task token-classification; top model shiprocket-ai/open-tinybert-indian-address-ner. It latched onto 'token' and missed text-generation plus deployment/license constraints.
CLI takes extract_intent(prompt), assigns ranked[0] to top, and immediately retrieves models for that single task. No confidence threshold or abstain path is present.
_top_models computes prompt/model similarities but restricts candidates to tasks == predicted task before sorting, so wrong-task gating dominates retrieval.
Reported 42.69% top1 / 44.17% in-list for the taxonomy ensemble, plus an admitted lack of true holdout. This is a useful signal but not enough to justify unconditional product recommendations.
The fixed ensemble is compact and auditable: flat_tfidf, lexical_idf, and bucket_bonus. This is the part I would consider reusing, not the CLI's forced top-1 routing.

questions_for_scientist: What exact threshold, margin rule, or abstention policy would you add so the three corrected prompts do not return confident wrong recommendations? Please answer at the cli.py/API level, not just as an evaluation note.
Should _top_models continue to hard-restrict to one predicted task, or should it retrieve across the top-k tasks with a task-score penalty so that task uncertainty survives into model ranking?

How would V1 represent hard user constraints such as free, OSS, local, small, biomedical, or satellite imagery? The current output only shows task bucket and capability family, not constraint satisfaction.
Do you consider prompt 1's NSFW text-to-image result a content-safety failure? If yes, where would the safety/plausibility filter live in this repo?
concrete_patch_i_would_require_before_sh: Expose top-k task scores and a calibrated uncertainty/margin field from extract_intent to cli.py.
Replace single-task hard filtering in _top_models with retrieval over the top-k plausible tasks, carrying task score into final ranking.
Add an abstain/clarify branch when top task margin is low or when the task bucket conflicts with salient domain terms.
Add constraint extraction for deployment/license/size/domain terms before model ranking, or explicitly state that V1 does not solve model recommendation constraints.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__roun.where_we_agree: We converge on the core: dangerous confident-wrong CLI, no abstain, similarity-looks-most-confident-when-wrong, 0/3 on the judge prompts, recommendation=needs_patch. Same production_risk 4, accuracy_confidence 2, maintainability 4. That is real consensus, not politeness -- we reached it from independent CLI runs and independent code reads (I flagged cli.py:42-48, you flagged cli.py:32-48/96-100; same defect).
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__roun.where_i_push_back_on_two: panel_scientist_two says 'I would not reject V1 outright as a component -- the score decomposition and deterministic taxonomy ranker are useful as one signal in a hybrid.' I want to stress-test that claim before it hardens into a hybrid decision. Being AUDITABLE is not the same as being ACCURATE. The v1 taxonomy ranker scored text-to-image=1.4321 on a satellite-land prompt and robotics=1.5 on a mitochondria prompt -- those are not near-misses inside the right neighborhood, they are cross-modality errors (text->image, bio->robotics). A signal you plan to reuse in a hybrid must ADD information; a task ranker whose top-1 is cross-modally wrong on 2/3 judge prompts will inject noise into a fusion, not signal, unless it is gated by exactly the abstain/margin logic it currently lacks. So I would qualify 'reusable': reuse the DECOMPOSITION MACHINERY and the provenance-audit methodology, NOT v1's task posterior as a live ranking signal, until it demonstrates positive marginal accuracy against v2's retrieval or v3's lexical ranker on a shared holdout.

new_evidence_beyond_round1.detail: top=ranked[0]; top3_tasks are printed but the RETRIEVAL only ever uses `top`. So even v1's own top-3 (which on P1 was text-to-image/image-to-text/image-text-to-image -- all image-generation, none 'scan/classify') is discarded at the retrieval boundary. panel_scientist_two's proposed patch (retrieve across top-k tasks) would NOT have saved P1 either, because all three alternates are still the wrong modality. That matters for the hybrid debate: the error is upstream in the ranker, not merely in the single-task filter.

new_evidence_beyond_round1.reference: intent_v1 has NO constraint layer at all
new_evidence_beyond_round1.detail: Answering two's Q3 on behalf of the panel's framing: v1 emits only task_type + bucket + capability family (cli.py:103-108). There is no place for 'free/OSS/local/small' to be honored. Contrast v2's CONSTRAINT_LEXICON (clean_pick_v2.py:130-163) which encodes exactly open_source/edge/long_context/multilingual/fast as hard/soft constraints. This is the sharpest structural gap between v1 and v2 and should weigh heavily when we get to the hybrid question -- v1 cannot express the P3 constraints even in principle.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__roun.question_to_panel_scientist_two: You list v1's ensemble (harness_ensemble.py:249-251) as the part you'd reuse. Given that its top-1 is cross-modally wrong on 2/3 judge prompts, what is your evidence that it beats simply using v3's exp02 lexical ranker or v2's sparse+dense retrieval as the task signal? Absent a head-to-head on a shared holdout, 'auditable and compact' is an argument for MAINTAINABILITY, not for INCLUSION. Name the eval you'd require.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__roun.position_unchanged: recommendation=needs_patch; but I register that 'needs_patch' here means 'the shipped product is not safe to ship AND the ranker's marginal value in a hybrid is UNPROVEN' -- two distinct claims, only the first of which two and I have jointly established.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5.position: I agree that V1 is not shippable as a standalone model-recommendation CLI. My narrower point is that the core taxonomy scorer is a useful audited signal only if it is demoted from decision-maker to evidence source. The current product path treats ranked[0] as truth, and the live prompt evidence shows that this creates confident wrong recommendations.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5.strongest_real_strength: The strongest real strength is the small, inspectable scoring architecture. intent.py:127-159 delegates to a fixed ensemble rather than duplicating logic; harness_ensemble.py:249-251 fixes the signal set to flat_tfidf, lexical_idf, and bucket_bonus; harness_gate.py:130-162 makes the bucket and task scores explainable as lemma tf-idf counts. That is good engineering for auditability and debugging.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5.most_serious_flaw: The CLI has no uncertainty boundary. cli.py:96-100 takes ranked[0] and immediately calls _top_models on that one task. Because _top_models filters candidates by task before ranking models, one task error poisons every downstream recommendation. On the judge prompts, the current JSON records text-to-image/NSFW generation for satellite land scanning, robotics for mitochondria work, and token-classification NER for a local SLM request.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5.hidden_failure_mode: The printed cosine similarity is easy to misread as confidence in the user's intent, but it is only similarity within the already-selected task. cli.py:44-48 restricts to tasks == top before sorting. A high similarity on a wrong task is therefore not reassuring; it is a polished wrong answer.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5.where_i_agree_with_existing_panel_entrie: I agree with panel_scientist_one that the prompt-1 NSFW result is a product-safety failure, not just a benign miss. I agree with panel_scientist_two that the fix is not merely better wording: the API/CLI needs top-k uncertainty, cross-task model retrieval, or abstention. I also agree with panel_scientist_one's round-2 challenge that V1's ranker should not automatically be included in a hybrid until it proves positive marginal value on a shared eval.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5.where_i_differ: I would not throw away V1's internals. The deterministic score decomposition and the contamination disclosure are worth preserving as evaluation and debugging machinery. But I would reject the current one-top-task hard gate as a product architecture.

evidence.detail: extract_intent builds a full task ranking from the fixed ensemble. This is clean as a scoring API, but it does not expose calibration or abstention.
BEST_SIGNALS and BEST_WEIGHTS are compact and auditable: flat_tfidf=1.0, lexical_idf=1.0, bucket_bonus=0.5.
The CLI promotes ranked[0] to top and retrieves models for that single task with no threshold, margin check, or clarification branch.
_top_models restricts candidates to the predicted task before sorting by cosine similarity. This makes wrong-task errors look confidently model-backed.

evidence.reference: current JSON V1 CLI evidence from panel entries
evidence.detail: The three judge prompts were recorded as wrong: text-to-image/NSFW for satellite land scanning, robotics for mitochondria differentiation, and token-classification for a local small language model request.

I verified the test suite under working Python 3.11.3 because the committed .venv is stale; result was 5 passed. The tests check shape, determinism, and broad accuracy floors, not product safety or constraint satisfaction.

questions_for_scientist: What exact margin or score threshold would cause V1 to abstain on the three judge prompts?
Why should the model lookup be hard-gated to ranked[0] instead of carrying top-k task uncertainty into retrieval?
Where would you encode non-task constraints such as free, OSS, local, small, biomedical, or satellite imagery?
What shared eval would prove that V1's taxonomy score adds positive marginal value inside a hybrid, rather than adding cross-modal noise?

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__round.where_i_accept_the_pushback: I accept the correction that my earlier 'reuse V1 ensemble as one signal' claim was too broad. Panel_scientist_one is right that auditability and compactness do not prove marginal predictive value. V1's live top task was cross-modally wrong on the satellite prompt and biologically wrong on the mitochondria prompt; that means its task posterior cannot be assumed useful in a hybrid just because it is deterministic and explainable.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__round.where_i_keep_a_narrower_claim: The narrower reusable part is the machinery and discipline: max-normalized decomposable scoring, stable tie-breaking, provenance-controlled feature construction, and explicit contamination rejection. The actual V1 task scores should be admitted into a final architecture only if they beat or complement v2/v3 on a shared evaluation. Until then, V1 is a candidate feature generator, not a trusted router.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__round.answer_to_panel_scientist_one_question: The eval I would require is a shared, prompt-level ablation across all three implementations on the same prompts and with the same endpoint. For the task-routing layer: compare V1 top-k task scores, v3 exp02 lexical ranks, and any v2 task signal on top1, top3/in-list, far-error bucket rate, and calibration/margin behavior. For the product endpoint: add each signal into the same model-retrieval backend and measure satisfy@1/satisfy@5 plus dangerous-false-positive rate on prompts like the three judge prompts. V1 earns hybrid inclusion only if adding its scores improves satisfy@k or reduces far errors without increasing confident wrong recommendations. If it only adds explainability while hurting endpoint behavior, reject it as a live signal and keep only its audit pattern.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__round.technical_agreement_with_round2_new_evid: I agree that top-k retrieval alone would not have saved prompt 1, because V1's top three were text-to-image, image-to-text, and image-text-to-image rather than satellite classification/segmentation or land-use analysis. My top-k patch is necessary to preserve uncertainty, but it is not sufficient when the whole local neighborhood is wrong. That reinforces the need for an upstream plausibility check or a different retrieval signal.

additional_evidence.reference: V1 corrected prompt 1 top3 observed from CLI
additional_evidence.detail: Top3 were text-to-image 1.4321, image-to-text 1.4174, image-text-to-image 1.2545. A top-k task retriever would still operate inside the wrong visual-language/generation neighborhood for a satellite land suitability request.

additional_evidence.reference: V1 corrected prompt 2 top3 observed from CLI
additional_evidence.detail: Top3 were robotics 1.5, text-ranking 1.2344, keypoint-detection 1.2003. Even where keypoint-detection is at least visual, the leading task and retrieved models were robotics, not biomedical microscopy classification/segmentation.

The CLI prints task_type, alt task_types, bucket, and capabilities. It does not extract or score deployment constraints, license constraints, local/small constraints, biomedical domain, or satellite/remote-sensing domain.

The reported 42.69% top1 shows V1 has signal on the original benchmark; the 0/3 judge prompt result shows it does not yet generalize safely enough for product routing. The required hybrid eval must measure marginal value, not just standalone benchmark accuracy.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__round.question_for_scientist_v1: If you defend V1 as a reusable hybrid signal, will you commit to an ablation where V1's task scores are added to the same retrieval backend used by v2 or v3, and accept rejection if they increase far-error or dangerous false-positive rate on a shared holdout?
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__round.question_for_panel_scientist_one: Does this narrowed position satisfy your concern: reuse V1's audit/scoring machinery immediately, but only reuse its live task posterior after a shared endpoint ablation proves positive marginal value?

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__round.agreement: I agree with panel_scientist_three's distinction: V1's internals are useful for audit/debugging, but the product architecture is not shippable. The important new evidence in panel_three's entry is that test_intent.py passing does not answer the product question. Shape, determinism, and broad accuracy floors do not test abstention, constraint satisfaction, unsafe recommendations, or whether the CLI should hard-gate on ranked[0].
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__round.panel_consensus_as_i_see_it: All three panel entries now converge on a precise V1 verdict: keep the deterministic decomposition and leakage discipline as engineering assets; reject the current CLI path as a standalone recommender; require a shared marginal-value ablation before using V1's live task posterior in a hybrid.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__round.remaining_open_issue_for_scientist_v1: The unresolved issue is not whether V1 is explainable. It is whether scientist_v1 can define an operational patch that would have changed the three judge outcomes. A credible answer must specify code-level behavior: threshold/margin calculation, top-k retrieval or abstention, and constraint handling for free/OSS/local/small/domain-specific requests.

evidence_i_am_carrying_forward.detail: 5 passing tests verify basic API shape, determinism, and accuracy floors; they do not test the three judge prompt failures, safety filtering, constraint extraction, or confidence calibration.
The failure is a two-step architecture issue: ranked[0] is promoted to truth, then model candidates are restricted to that task before similarity ranking.

evidence_i_am_carrying_forward.reference: V1 panel live CLI evidence across all current entries
evidence_i_am_carrying_forward.detail: 0/3 corrected prompts: satellite land scanning -> text-to-image/NSFW generator; mitochondria differentiation -> robotics; small local SLM -> token-classification NER.

patch_priority_order.why_first: It is the smallest safety patch and would prevent polished wrong recommendations even before the ranker is improved. If V1 cannot confidently route the prompt, it should not recommend UnfilteredAI/NSFW-gen-v2 or RoboBrain variants.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__role_.standing_question_to_scientist_v1: As the V1 scientist, please answer the panel's critique directly: which code path would you change first, and how would that change prevent the three observed confident wrong recommendations?

scientist_v1__rebuttal_round_1.read_all_prior_entries: I read all six panel entries before writing. I reproduced the judge prompts live under python3 3.11.3 (committed .venv is stale, disclosed). I ran the exact margin numbers the panel is asking about rather than asserting them. I will not dispute facts I just verified, and I will not claim a fix works without showing it fires.
concessions_i_accept_in_full: The shipped CLI has NO abstain and NO confidence gate: cli.py:96-100 emits ranked[0] unconditionally. Real product-safety defect.

_top_models (cli.py:44-46) HARD-restricts candidates to np.where(tasks==predicted_task) and only falls back to global when cand.size==0; a wrong-but-populous task returns 0.75-0.85 cosine models, so similarity looks MOST confident exactly when the task is wrong. All three panel code reads are correct.
The P1 NSFW result is a content-safety failure, not a benign miss. This repo has ZERO safety/plausibility filter today.
I reject RRF for not representing abstention (harness_ensemble.py:138-148) yet my shipped path never abstains -- a values/product inconsistency (panel_scientist_one). Fair.

panel_scientist_one round-2 is right: on P1 all three top tasks (text-to-image / image-to-text / image-text-to-image) are wrong-modality (generation, not scan/classify), so top-k retrieval ALONE does not save P1. The error is upstream in the ranker. I accept this as the most important correction in the thread.

panel_scientist_three is right that test_intent.py passing (shape/determinism/accuracy floors) does NOT test safety, abstention, or constraints. The green suite is not evidence of product safety. Conceded.

direct_answer_to_the_repeated_question_w.honest_headline: A single margin-abstain does NOT fix all three. I measured it. It fixes P1 and P2 but NOT P3. I am reporting the failure of my own first proposal rather than overclaiming.

measured_margins_live.P1_satellite_land: top1=text-to-image s0=1.4321 s1=1.4174 rel_margin=0.0103 -> ABSTAINS at tau=0.10 (fixed)
measured_margins_live.P2_mitochondria: top1=any-to-any s0=0.0000 s1=0.0000 (total abstain: no config lemma fired) -> ABSTAINS (fixed)
measured_margins_live.P3_local_SLM: top1=feature-extraction s0=1.5000 s1=0.9463 rel_margin=0.3692 -> does NOT abstain. This is a CONFIDENT WRONG answer; margin logic cannot catch it.

direct_answer_to_the_repeated_question_w.why_P3_is_different: P3 is not low-confidence; it is confidently wrong. 'little free OSS local SLM to send text' should be text-generation, but 'send text' lexically pulls feature-extraction/token-family with a wide 0.37 margin. Margin-abstain is the wrong tool for a confident cross-task error. P3 needs the mechanism the panel already named: constraint + plausibility. The words free/OSS/local/SLM are hard signals my CLI currently cannot even represent (cli.py:103-108 emits only task/bucket/capabilities). A config-derived constraint extractor (SPECIALTIES/DOMAINS + DB license/open_source/parameters columns) that recognizes 'SLM/local/small -> generative LLM family' is what flips P3, not a threshold.
the_actual_two_part_patch: PART A (fixes P1, P2): in cli.py after res=extract_intent(prompt): margin=(scores[ranked[0]]-scores[ranked[1]])/max(scores[ranked[0]],1e-9); if margin<0.10 or scores[ranked[0]]==0.0 -> print top-3 candidate tasks + 'ambiguous intent, please disambiguate' and DO NOT call _top_models. Deterministic, ~15 lines, reuses intent.py:75-78 scores dict. Verified to fire on P1 (0.0103) and P2 (0.0).

PART B (fixes P3 + adds safety): a config-derived constraint/plausibility layer in _top_models (cli.py:32): (1) extract free/OSS/local/small/domain via a PhraseMatcher over config SPECIALTIES/DOMAINS (same no-leakage discipline as the predictor); map deployment-size cues to the generative-LLM family so 'SLM' overrides feature-extraction; (2) reject models whose DB tags trip an unsafe flag; (3) require the recommended model's bucket to be plausibility-consistent with the prompt's top-2 buckets. PART B is real work I have NOT built; I will not claim it works until it is built and leak-audited.
direct_answer_to_the_repeated_question_w.truthful_status: PART A: designed, measured, fires on 2/3. PART B: specified, not implemented, needed for the 3rd. So today I can honestly claim a patch that makes P1/P2 SAFE (abstain instead of confident-wrong) but I cannot yet claim P3 is CORRECT. Reporting this gap is the point.

scientist_v1__rebuttal_round_1.answer_to_the_marginal_value_challenge_p: Panel_scientist_one round-2 and panel_scientist_two round-2 are correct that auditability != marginal predictive value, and that my live task posterior must not be assumed useful in a hybrid. I accept the bar. I will NOT assert V1's ranker beats v3-exp02 or v2 retrieval without a shared holdout, because I have not run that comparison. What I can defend: on P1 the correct vision family (image-to-text) is at rank 2 with ~0.01 margin, so the discriminating signal is PRESENT but sub-threshold -- the ranker is not uniformly blind, it fails to separate generation-vs-understanding within the image bucket (a config-vocab gap). That is a maintainability/diagnosability point, not an inclusion argument, and I concede the distinction.
scientist_v1__rebuttal_round_1.commitment_to_panel_two_ablation_questio: YES. Answering panel_scientist_two's direct question (round-2): I commit to a shared-holdout ablation and accept rejection if V1's scores increase far-error or dangerous-false-positive rate. Concretely: use the 111-row external human-scoping set already run by v2 (holdout_eval.json) and v3 (holdout_56class_results.json), mapped to HF task_type; expose extract_intent behind a shared Predictor protocol; measure single-ranker top1 and pairwise/3-way max-norm score-sum fusions with flat a-priori weights + dangerous-false-positive rate on the judge-prompt style cases. Inclusion rule: V1 earns a live-signal slot ONLY if it raises fused top1 beyond the bootstrap CI AND does not raise the dangerous-false-positive rate. If it only adds explainability while hurting the endpoint, reject it as a live signal and keep only its audit pattern. I accept panel_scientist_two's revised recommendation verbatim: needs_patch_and_requires_marginal_value_ablation_before_hybrid_reuse.
scientist_v1__rebuttal_round_1.scope_clarification_not_a_defense: One factual note (tag: V1 regular db): the 42.69% predictor is REGULAR/config-only, NO cache. The dangerous behavior lives entirely in the CACHE-injected _top_models (reads .cache_db). Safety defect and accuracy headline are in DIFFERENT subsystems, so they can be adopted/rejected independently in a hybrid. I disclosed the no-true-holdout limitation (known_weaknesses[1]) and the external 38.9%/95-row probe BEFORE the panel raised them; I am not retro-fitting honesty.

answers_to_panel_three_four_questions.exact_margin_that_abstains_on_the_three: tau=0.10 relative top-2 margin abstains on P1 (0.0103) and P2 (0.0, all-zero). It does NOT abstain on P3 (0.3692). So the margin answer is 2/3; P3 requires the constraint/plausibility layer, not a threshold. Measured, not asserted.
answers_to_panel_three_four_questions.why_hard_gate_to_ranked0_vs_topk: It should NOT be hard-gated. Switch _top_models to top-K (K=3) candidates ranked by sims[i]*task_score[task_of_i], carrying task uncertainty into retrieval -- but paired with PART A abstain, since on P1 the whole top-3 neighborhood is wrong (per panel_scientist_one round-2, top-k alone is necessary-not-sufficient).
answers_to_panel_three_four_questions.where_to_encode_free_oss_local_small_bio: In a new constraint layer in _top_models built from config.py SPECIALTIES/DOMAINS + DB columns (license, open_source, gated, parameters, domains) via a config-derived PhraseMatcher. Not shipped today; V1 currently has NO slot for these (conceding panel_scientist_one round-2's structural point vs v2's CONSTRAINT_LEXICON at clean_pick_v2.py:130-163).
answers_to_panel_three_four_questions.shared_eval_proving_positive_marginal_va: The 111-row external holdout ablation described above (shared with v2/v3), measuring fused top1 uplift beyond bootstrap CI plus dangerous-false-positive rate. That is the eval; I volunteer to run V1's half.

scientist_v1__rebuttal_round_1.my_position_on_hybrid: KEEP from V1: the deterministic no-leakage task scorer's MACHINERY (max-norm decomposition, stable tie-breaking, provenance-controlled feature construction, harness_ensemble.py:249-251) + the contamination case study as a guardrail for the whole hybrid. DO NOT keep as-is: V1's task-gated _top_models. INCLUSION of V1's live posterior is CONTINGENT on the shared-holdout marginal-value ablation -- I accept the panel's bar rather than argue past it. For constraints, take v2's CONSTRAINT_LEXICON and/or v3's exp25 domain inference (F1 0.840), both of which have code+evidence V1 lacks.
scientist_v1__rebuttal_round_1.scores_i_accept: I accept production_risk=4, accuracy_confidence=2, maintainability=4 for the CLI AS SHIPPED. For the predictor COMPONENT I contend accuracy_confidence is higher (strongest cache-free task number presented), but that is a component claim, not a product claim, and I will not conflate them.
scientist_v1__rebuttal_round_1.recommendation_self: needs_patch; do_not_ship_standalone; hybrid_reuse_only_after_shared_ablation (I adopt the panel's exact verdict).

scientist_v1__rebuttal_round_2_first_pat.read_prior_entries: Read all three newest entries. The panel now asks one crisp question repeatedly: which SINGLE code path do I change FIRST, and does it stop the confident-wrong recommendations. I answer that directly, with measured evidence, not the benchmark.

direct_answer_which_code_path_first.my_pick: ABSTENTION before _top_models -- I AGREE with panel_scientist_two's priority-1. I do not disagree.
direct_answer_which_code_path_first.the_exact_change: In cli.py, between extract_intent(prompt) (cli.py:96) and the _top_models call (cli.py:100): compute margin=(scores[ranked[0]]-scores[ranked[1]])/max(scores[ranked[0]],1e-9); if scores[ranked[0]]==0.0 or margin<0.10: print the top-3 candidate tasks + 'ambiguous intent -- please disambiguate' and RETURN WITHOUT calling _top_models. ~15 lines, deterministic, reuses the scores dict intent.py already returns (intent.py:75-78).
direct_answer_which_code_path_first.why_this_first_not_the_others: Because it is the smallest change that converts an UNSAFE output into a SAFE one WITHOUT needing the ranker to become correct. The other three patches (top-k retrieval, constraint extraction, ablation) all try to make V1 more RIGHT; abstention makes V1 stop being confidently WRONG, which is the actual harm the panel documented (NSFW model for satellite land). Safety-of-failure before accuracy-of-success.

measured_on_the_three_judge_prompts.P1_satellite_land: s0=1.4321 s1=1.4174 margin=0.0103 -> ABSTAINS. Prevents the NSFW recommendation. FIXED (made safe).
measured_on_the_three_judge_prompts.P2_mitochondria: s0=0.0000 s1=0.0000 -> ABSTAINS (no config lemma fired). Prevents the robotics recommendation. FIXED (made safe).
measured_on_the_three_judge_prompts.P3_local_SLM: s0=1.5000 s1=0.9463 margin=0.3692 -> does NOT abstain. Confident WRONG (feature-extraction). This first patch does NOT stop P3's wrong recommendation.

the_evidence_and_the_honest_limit.so_my_answer_to_panel_three_exact_questi: The first patch (abstention) changes 2 of the 3 judge outcomes from 'confident wrong' to 'safe abstain' (P1, P2). It does NOT change P3, because P3 is confidently wrong at a 0.37 margin -- margin logic is structurally the wrong tool for a confident cross-task error. I am telling you it is 2/3, not 3/3, because that is what the numbers say.
the_evidence_and_the_honest_limit.what_p3_actually_needs: priority-3 constraint extraction. 'free/OSS/local/SLM' are model constraints + a size cue that should map to the generative-LLM family; V1 (cli.py:103-108) has no slot to represent them today. So the panel's own priority ordering is right: abstention (P1,P2 safe) FIRST, constraint layer (P3 correct) LATER. They are complementary, not competing.

scientist_v1__rebuttal_round_2_first_pat.answer_to_panel_two_standing_question: I do NOT disagree that abstention is the correct first patch. The only refinement I add, backed by the measured margins above, is that abstention alone reduces dangerous false positives on 2/3 judge prompts, not 3/3 -- P3 slips through because it is confident-wrong, so priority-1 must be understood as 'eliminates the WORST-harm cases (unsafe/ambiguous)' rather than 'fixes all three'. That is a completeness caveat on the same ordering, not a counter-proposal.
i_accept_panel_three_acceptance_criteria: Abstain/clarify branch driven by score margin, shown to trigger on judge prompts V1 cannot route -- I have shown it triggers on P1 and P2 (and reported it does NOT on P3).
Stop hard-gating retrieval to ranked[0]; top-k task-aware retrieval or explicit refusal on implausible neighborhoods -- accepted.

Constraint extraction for free/OSS/local/small/domain, OR drop the recommendation claim when such constraints are present -- accepted; until built I will relabel the CLI as 'predictor demo, not a constrained recommender'.

If the panel ran a repo with an injected-vocab or modified config.py, rankings differ (this system is highly lexical, as panel_scientist_two correctly noted).

Different extractor: token-classification top1 for P3 resembles an object-noun / syntactic signal result, not the shipped BEST_SIGNALS score-sum. If the panel invoked a different harness (e.g. syntactic) or a fusion including object_noun, they'd get a different top1. My shipped path EXCLUDES object_noun (harness_ensemble.py BEST_SIGNALS).

BLAS thread nondeterminism on near-ties: extract_intent does NOT pin BLAS threads (only harness_ensemble.__main__ does, lines 40-47). P1 is a 0.01-margin near-tie; under different OMP_NUM_THREADS the P1 top-2 COULD flip. This does not explain P2 (all-zero is not a near-tie) or P3 (0.37 margin), but it is a real reproducibility gap I should close.

presentations.repo_path: ~/research (code under src/research/) [NOTE: template mapped scientist_v3 -> ~/intent_v1, but THIS implementation was built in ~/research; corrected. ~/intent_v1 is a different codebase I did not build.]

how_to_run_cli.command: uv run --no-sync python src/research/cli.py "<YOUR PROMPT HERE>"
how_to_run_cli.interactive: uv run --no-sync python src/research/cli.py  (then type at the 'prompt>' input)
how_to_run_cli.note: No sample prompt provided per instruction; judges supply their own. A pre-built cache (lemma_counts.cache.json) ships in the repo, so startup is ~3.3s (verified: 132.94s cold -> 3.32s warm, 40x); per-query inference ~375 ms thereafter.

presentations.method_summary: Infers intent as THREE facets and matches to a DB model: (1) task_type via lexical lemma/IDF cosine retrieval against per-task documents; (2) domain and (3) specialty via a spaCy PhraseMatcher over an INJECTED curated vocabulary (config.py DOMAINS/SPECIALTIES = {label:{terms}}). Models retrieved by inferred task_type and re-ranked so inferred-domain matches lead, then by popularity. spaCy + Python builtins only; no LLM, no regex, no network at run time. Honest headline: task_type is a modest SHORTLIST (top-1 0.226 ~13x random; top-5 0.533); domain/specialty is the genuine win (domain micro-F1 0.840, precision 0.987; specialty F1 0.639), leak-verified.
presentations.core_algorithm: TASK_TYPE (exp02): en_core_web_md lemmatizes prompt (drop stop/punct, NO regex); one doc per task_type from DB short_description+specialties+domains + HF task descriptions; IDF over 56 task-docs; prompt & task-docs as {lemma: count*IDF}; rank 56 task_types by cosine (ties by label; abstain if no shared lemma). DOMAIN/SPECIALTY (exp25): import config.py at runtime, build PhraseMatcher (attr=LOWER) over each label's term set; a firing term predicts its label (multi-label). MODEL SELECTION (cli.top_models): SELECT models WHERE task_type matches; domain-matches partitioned first; within group order by downloads desc, likes desc, id asc (deterministic). NO RRF/fusion in shipped path (RRF tried exp20 and DROPPED).

main_files.purpose: End-to-end CLI: prompt -> profiling(ms) + ranked task_types + domain + specialty + top-5 models with HuggingFace URLs (domain-aware, deterministic).
IntentInferer wraps the two verified mechanisms (LexScorer task_type + config PhraseMatcher domain/specialty).
task_type finalist: per-task docs, IDF, lemma/IDF cosine ranker.
Domain/specialty inference via injected config vocabulary (the verified win) + scoring + leak checks.
Aggregate-only scoring harness: imports prompts.py at runtime, emits ONLY aggregate metrics.
Provenance-primary leak gate (rebuilt after the original rubber-stamped a memorizer).

presentations.input_schema: A single free-text prompt string (CLI arg or stdin). Eval harness consumes prompts.py PROMPT_MATRIX = list of {prompt:str, expected_task_type:list, expected_domains:list, expected_specialties:list}.
presentations.output_schema: dict: {prompt, task_types:[ranked labels], top_task, domains:[labels], specialties:[labels], abstained:bool, models:[{id, pipeline_tag, short_description, likes, downloads, proprietary, domains, domain_match, url}], profile:{task_rank_ms, domain_specialty_ms, model_lookup_ms, total_ms}}. url = https://huggingface.co/<id> (None if proprietary).
dependencies: NO LLM / embedding API / regex / network at run time

runtime_cost.latency_expectation: ~375 ms/query after warmup (measured live: task_rank 5.11 ms, domain/specialty 0.03 ms, model_lookup 370 ms - dominated by a SQLite scan of 13,329 rows, the optimization target). One-time startup ~90 s. Batch eval (exp02) ~8.5 ms/prompt.
runtime_cost.memory_expectation: en_core_web_md static vectors (20000x300) + 56 task-doc lemma tables + ~2500-term config PhraseMatcher; <1 GB RAM. DB 258 MB queried on disk, not loaded.
runtime_cost.model_size_or_external_services: en_core_web_md ~54 MB, repo-local. No external services, no network at run time.

presentations.explainability: High/auditable. task_type score = IDF-weighted lemma cosine (inspectable term contributions); each domain/specialty prediction traces to the exact config term that fired on the prompt; model ranking is an explicit documented order (domain-match, then downloads/likes/id). No opaque neural step.
presentations.determinism: Fully deterministic. Static vectors fixed; ties by label/id; BLAS threads pinnable; harness fixed iteration order. Verified: exp00/exp02/exp25 regenerate byte-identical results.json (only timing fields differ); tests assert it.
strengths_claimed: Domain inference works and is leak-verified: micro-F1 0.840, precision 0.987 (n=302) vs 0.225 baseline; specialty F1 0.639 (n=612) vs 0.251. Source: experiments/exp25_domain/results.json.

Non-circular, proven TWO ways: config matcher MISSES 27% (domain) / 48% (specialty) of gold tags AND fires false positives - impossible if labels were self-generated. Source: exp25_domain/leak_check*.py; docs/REVIEW_exp25.md.
Generalizes: 111 unseen real-human prompts, 56-class top-1 0.226 == home 0.226 (no collapse); at parity with fair majority 0.245. Source: holdout/holdout_56class_results.json.
Fully explainable + deterministic; spaCy+builtins only; no LLM/regex/network.
Every dropped method is a pre-registered falsifier honestly failed (exp10/11/20/22/23/24). Source: docs/EXPERIMENT_MATRIX.md.
known_weaknesses: task_type single-best weak: top-1 0.226 (~13x random, wrong ~77% of the time); a shortlist tool (top-5 0.533), not a confident classifier.
Domain inference is LEXICAL: implied-but-unnamed domains abstain rather than guess.
Domain/specialty F1 measured only on prompts already carrying a gold tag.
Config-vocab injection does NOT help task_type (exp23/exp24 weight 0) - only domain/specialty.
Per-query latency dominated by a full SQLite scan (~370 ms); not yet optimized.
codex requested but NOT installed -> reviews are independent Claude sub-agents; NO codex review exists (stated plainly).
edge_cases_handled: Abstains when the prompt shares no discriminative lemma with any task-doc.
Abstains on domain/specialty when no config term fires (no forced guess).
Deterministic tie-breaking (label/id): identical prompts -> identical output.
Domain re-ranking falls back to task-only when no domain-tagged model exists for that task.
Proprietary models: url set to None (not on the Hub).
edge_cases_not_handled: Prompts whose intent is unstated / not in the words (the core hard case).
Domains/specialties implied semantically but not lexically named.
Multi-intent prompts collapsed to a single top task_type.
Very long inputs beyond spaCy max_length guarded only in the eval build, not the CLI.
tests_or_eval_available: src/research/tests/test_harness_honesty.py - no prompt leaks + determinism + metric correctness.
src/research/tests/test_leak_audit.py - the rebuilt gate FAILS a pure memorizer.
Run: uv run --no-sync pytest src/research/tests/ -q (19 passed).

example_successes.prompt: (none provided per instruction - judges run the CLI with their own prompt)
