cli_results_on_human_prompts.assessment: Partial signal but not a clean hit. It stayed in image/geospatial territory and surfaced one geospatial model at rank 2, but the top recommendation is depth estimation, not satellite land suitability / plot selection / remote-sensing classification.

Best V2 result among the three. It retrieved a medical image-feature model at rank 1, which is at least plausible for biomedical microscopy work. But it does not establish mature-vs-nascent mitochondria differentiation specifically, and ranks NER/text-generation models close behind.

Bad miss. The user asks for a small free OSS local SLM/text model, but the CLI ranks image-to-image and geospatial segmentation above text-generation, and the text-generation model is 120B, not a little local SLM.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.strongest_real_strength: V2 has the right product framing: model retrieval with constraints, not merely HF task classification. The implementation has an explicit constraint layer, sparse-to-dense retrieval, deterministic sorting, and an honest coverage metric. It also performs better than V1 on prompt 2 and at least keeps prompt 1 in the image/geospatial neighborhood.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.most_serious_flaw: The shipped default CLI is not the evaluated full retriever and still fails the most constraint-heavy prompt. cli.py defaults task_predictor=None, while clean_pick_metric._build_retriever adds the phase2 BlendPredictor used in the metric path. On prompt 3, the default CLI detects only open_source and fails to encode local/OSS/little SLM as small local text-generation constraints, so it returns image-to-image models before any text-generation model.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.hidden_failure_mode: The constraint layer sounds stronger than it behaves. clean_pick_v2.py includes an edge constraint for small/on-device/mobile/tiny/lightweight/laptop/raspberry-pi/low-resource, but the actual prompt says 'little SLM', 'OSS', and 'local'. In my extraction check, P3 hard_constraints was only ['open_source']; soft_constraints was empty. Thus the system can claim constraint handling while missing common user vocabulary for exactly the constraints that matter.

evidence.detail: Default CLI loads docs and constructs CleanPickV2(docs, nlp, task_predictor=None). The --with-task-bonus path is separate and slower.
The evaluated metric builder constructs a phase2 BlendPredictor and passes task_predictor=blend into CleanPickV2. This is not the default CLI path.
Constraint lexicon exists, but detect_constraints uses listed surfaces only. On prompt 3 it detected open_source only; it did not detect OSS/local/little SLM as edge/small/local constraints.
pick() sparse-retrieves candidates, filters hard constraints, scores phrase similarity plus optional task/constraint bonuses, and sorts by score/id. Architecture is understandable and deterministic.
Home satisfy@1 0.1184 [0.1028, 0.1347], satisfy@5 0.1809, fair baseline 0.0695, build_time_s 1631.9604, ms_per_prompt 39.1687 once built.
External probe: modality_match@1 0.5 [0.4057,0.5943], task_inlist@1 0.2844 [0.2018,0.367], task_inlist@5 0.3578. Honest but still low absolute accuracy.
19 tests passed in 1.43s. Good focused coverage, but the live prompt 3 failure shows tests do not cover common SLM/local/OSS constraint phrasing.

Cache now exists and default CLI runs in about 7.6-8.3s per prompt in separate processes. Earlier panel readiness was poor because this cache was missing and the recorded full build time is about 27 minutes.

questions_for_scientist: Why should the default CLI be judged as the shipped system when the metric path uses clean_pick_metric._build_retriever with a phase2 BlendPredictor, but cli.py defaults task_predictor=None?

Why does the constraint lexicon not treat 'OSS', 'local', or 'SLM' as first-class constraints for prompt 3? Which code path should have prevented image-to-image models from outranking a local small text-generation model?
Is the cache now part of the deliverable? At pre-review it was absent and all three runs entered the long build path; now it exists. What is the intended reproducible panel setup?

For prompt 1, why is depth-estimation rank 1 over the explicit geospatial model at rank 2 and segmentation model at rank 4? What evidence says depth estimation is the best model for finding plots of land?

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8.cli_now_runs_correction_to_prior_disclos: IMPORTANT UPDATE that CORRECTS the record: v2's own cli_performance_disclosure said the cache is useless and every run 'still re-vectorizes on each construction ... ~15+ minutes.' That is NOW STALE. With .cli_cache_docs.pkl present (21MB, built 04:53), I ran all three judge prompts and each completed in ~8-9 SECONDS (P1 05:10:52->05:11:01, P2 ~8s, P3 ~8s). I checked the code: caching is UNCHANGED (cli.py:40-47 still pickles only docs; clean_pick_v2.py:611 still rebuilds _model_norms in __init__). The resolution: re-vectorizing ~13k models' SHORT POS-extracted phrases (no parser) is actually fast (~seconds); the true multi-minute cost was the DOCS build (POS-tagging 13k full cards), which the cache DOES save. So v2's panic disclosure OVER-STATED the problem -- with the docs cache shipped, the CLI is fast. The real deployability defect was narrower and already fixed by shipping the cache: v2 simply failed to ship a warm cache for the judges' first run, forcing the one-time docs build. That is a release-hygiene miss, not a per-run architectural tax.

cli_evidence_on_3_judge_prompts.prompt: gimme model to scan satellite images for best plots of land
cli_evidence_on_3_judge_prompts.verdict: BEST P1 of all three implementations. Genuinely relevant: geospatial land-surface-temp, tree-canopy segmentation, bio remote-sensing ViT. No NSFW generator (v1), no VQA/OCR miss (v3). The retrieval reframing pays off here.

BEST P2 of all three. #1 is a MEDICAL VISION feature-extractor; #3-5 are bio/medical-domain NER. v1 gave robotics, v3 gave OCR -- both domain-blind. v2 is the only one that surfaced medical/bio-domain models. Still not a clean image-classification hit (KRONOS is feature-extraction not classification), but domain-relevant.

cli_evidence_on_3_judge_prompts.prompt: little free/OSS/local SLM to send text
cli_evidence_on_3_judge_prompts.verdict: WORST P3 of all three. Recommended IMAGE GENERATORS and a 120-BILLION-parameter model for a 'little SLM to send text' request. v3 nailed this (Qwen3-0.6B); v2 failed it badly despite HAVING a constraint layer.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8.strongest_real_strength: The retrieval reframing is correct and it is the ONLY one of the three measured on the actual product endpoint (does the returned MODEL satisfy the intent, not just is the task label right). On P1 and P2 it demonstrably beats v1 and v3 by surfacing domain- and modality-appropriate models (geospatial/tree-canopy for land; medical-vision/bio-NER for mitochondria) that the task-classifier approaches structurally cannot reach. The presentation is the most honest of the three: satisfy@1=0.1184 stated up front with 95% CI [0.1028,0.1347], a FAIR random-in-plausible baseline (0.0695, 1.70x lift) not a strawman, negative results (whole-doc vectors, NER, TextCat, injection) all kept, and the no-gold-model-id limitation disclosed. I verified satisfy@1=0.1184 in results/clean_pick_v2.json -- it matches the claim exactly.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8.most_serious_flaw: P3 is a CONSTRAINT-HANDLING FAILURE in the exact subsystem v2 markets as its edge over v1/v3. I traced it: extract_intent fired ONLY hard_constraints={open_source} on P3; the 'edge'/small constraint NEVER FIRED because CONSTRAINT_LEXICON['edge'] (clean_pick_v2.py:139-143) is a fixed surface list {small,tiny,lightweight,on-device,mobile,laptop,...} that does NOT contain the user's actual words 'little' or 'local'. So no size filter applied, and gpt-oss-120b (120,412,337,472 params, open_source=1) sailed through the only active hard filter. Worse: the top-2 results are FLUX image-to-image models scoring 0.7349 -- a text request answered with image generators -- because the task bonus is SOFT (+0.15, 'never a gate' per the author's design) and cannot lift text-generation over image models winning on phrase-cosine. The correct answer (Qwen3-0.6B, 751M, OSS) EXISTS in the DB and is what v3 returned, but v2 buried it. The soft-task-bonus design choice, defended in the presentation, is precisely what causes cross-modality leakage on constrained prompts.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8.hidden_failure_mode: The constraint lexicon gives a FALSE SENSE of coverage. It looks principled (5 named constraints, hard/soft) but it is brittle literal-surface matching with no lemma normalization for the constraint surfaces themselves: 'little'!='small', 'local'!='on-premise', 'run local' doesn't match 'self-host'. On the constrained subpopulation (only 82/1537 = 11% of home prompts carry ANY constraint per results/clean_pick_v2.json), a real user's colloquial constraint phrasing will frequently MISS, silently dropping the hard filter and returning unconstrained (here: 120B, image-gen) results with a confident score. Because misses are silent, satisfy@1 on the home set does not surface this -- the home prompts likely use canonical constraint words. This is analogous to v1's silent-failure class but located in the constraint layer instead of the task ranker.

evidence.detail: top1-2 FLUX.2-klein image-to-image (0.7349), top4 gpt-oss-120b text-generation (120B params). A 'little local SLM' request answered with image generators and a 120B model.
surface list omits 'little' and 'local'; user's exact words. Verified via extract_intent(P3): hard_constraints=frozenset({'open_source'}) only -- edge did NOT fire.
task match adds only +0.15 and is 'never a gate'; FLUX image-to-image at 0.7349 dense beats text-generation candidates, so wrong modality wins on a text prompt.

'edge' requires deployable AND parameters<=_SMALL_PARAM_MAX; correct logic, but never invoked on P3 because the constraint didn't fire upstream. open_source=1 on both FLUX and gpt-oss-120b, so the ONE active filter excludes nothing relevant.

satisfy@1=0.1184 verified (matches presentation). constrained_subpop_n=82 (11%): the constraint layer -- v2's differentiator -- is exercised on only ~1/9 of prompts and, as P3 shows, misses colloquial phrasings.
Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B (751M params, open_source) exists in DB and is the correct P3 answer (v3 returned it). v2 did not surface it in top-5.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8.where_v2_beats_the_others: On the two UNCONSTRAINED, domain-bearing prompts (P1 land, P2 bio) v2 is the clear winner -- retrieval surfaces domain/modality-appropriate models that neither task-classifier reaches. This is real and I will defend it against any claim that v2 is uniformly worse.
questions_for_scientist: Your P3 output recommends FLUX image-to-image and a 120B model for 'a little free OSS local SLM to send text.' Two failures: (a) the 'edge/small' constraint never fired because your lexicon lacks 'little' and 'local' -- why is the constraint surface list a fixed literal set with no lemma/synonym handling, when the rest of your pipeline lemmatizes? (b) Even with open_source firing, image-to-image models top a TEXT request because your task signal is soft (+0.15). On a prompt that explicitly says 'send text', why should modality be a soft nudge rather than a hard filter?

constrained_subpop is 11% of home prompts. How do you know your constraint layer works for real colloquial phrasings when your home eval mostly uses canonical constraint words? Is there ANY test with paraphrased constraints ('little' for 'small', 'run local' for 'self-host')?

You correctly say satisfy@1=0.1184 is bound by the task facet (~0.20). Given P3, isn't the deeper bound that a soft task signal lets wrong-modality models win whenever their card prose phrase-matches the prompt? Would gating on modality raise satisfy@1 or would it hurt the P1/P2 wins?
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8.one_line: Best retrieval idea and most honest write-up of the three; wins P1 and P2 outright; but P3 exposes that the marketed constraint layer misses colloquial phrasings and the soft-task-bonus lets image generators answer a text request -- needs_patch, not reject.

concessions_i_accept_on_evidence: CLI-vs-eval config mismatch is REAL and I concede it. panel_scientist_two is right: cli.py default builds CleanPickV2(docs, nlp, task_predictor=None) (cli.py:51-59), but the evaluated 0.1184 in results/clean_pick_v2.json is built WITH the phase2 soft task predictor (clean_pick_metric.py:323-352). So the 3 prompts judges ran are the retriever MINUS the soft task bonus -- not the exact system my headline number measured. That is a legitimate discrepancy; the judged CLI is a weaker configuration than the evaluated one. I should have defaulted the CLI to the full config or labeled it explicitly.

Constraint-vocabulary gap is REAL. Prompt 3 ('free and OSS ... run local', 'little SLM') returned empty constraints because CONSTRAINT_LEXICON keys 'open_source'/'edge' do not include surface forms like 'SLM' or 'small language model'. So I can 'claim constraint handling' while missing the exact user vocabulary that matters. Valid hit.
Absolute accuracy is low. 'Honest but still low' is fair. I do not contest it.

The earlier ZERO-output failure was real; it is only fixed now because the cache exists (.cli_cache_docs.pkl, ~20MB). My shipped cache design was also broken (docs-only, not the vectorized norms) -- disclosed in cli_performance_disclosure.
where_i_still_stand_with_evidence: The judged CLI is HARDER on me than my metric, not easier: it lacks the soft task bonus, yet prompt 2 still put a MEDICAL VISION model (MahmoodLab/KRONOSv2, domain=medical, specialty=vision) at rank 1 for a mitochondria-microscopy ask -- closer to the true need (cell/microscopy image analysis) than a robotics or text-gen answer. Prompt 1 kept image/geospatial and surfaced a geospatial model at rank 2. That is partial signal from the WEAKER config; it is evidence the retrieval direction is right, not that it is solved.

My headline claim is unchanged and endpoint-anchored: results/clean_pick_v2.json satisfy@1 0.1184 [0.1028,0.1347] vs FAIR baseline 0.0695 (~1.70x), reproduced within CI, two-run bit-identical. That number is the full-config system; judge the SCIENCE on it, judge the PRODUCT on the (weaker, now-working) CLI.
concrete_fixes_i_propose_but_have_NOT_ap: Default cli.py to the full evaluated config (build the phase2 blend) OR print a one-line banner that the default CLI omits the soft task bonus -- so judged output == measured system.
Add constraint surface forms: 'slm','small language model','local','on device','runs locally','oss' -> open_source/edge in CONSTRAINT_LEXICON (DB-justified, prompt-blind).
Fix the cache to persist L2-normalized model-norm matrices, not just docs, so cold start is a one-time cost.
I am deliberately NOT editing code during the debate to avoid moving the artifact under the panel; I log these as owed patches.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round1_repl.what_this_does_not_change: None of these fixes touch the measured 0.1184 or its integrity (prompt-blindness, determinism, leak-audit PASS). They are CLI/UX and constraint-coverage fixes. If the panel's axis is deployable-CLI-quality I am mid-pack at best right now; if the axis is 'measured on the actual coverage endpoint with a fair baseline and reproduction,' I remain the only entry that is.

scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round1_ask_.request: For a fair cross-impl comparison, please run every implementation through ONE common endpoint (satisfy@1 with the shared golden facets in clean_pick_metric.satisfies), not each impl's home-favored metric. Task-only top1 (e.g. ~0.17-0.23 elsewhere) and facet F1 measured separately are NOT comparable to a 4-way-joint satisfy@1. I will accept whatever the common-metric run shows, including if I lose.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.where_i_agree: I agree with panel_scientist_one that V2 is the strongest retrieval framing and is materially better than V1/V3 on P1 and P2. I also agree that P3 is the key failure because it is exactly the kind of constrained local-model request V2 claims it can handle. The failure is not cosmetic: image-to-image models and a 120B text model for a little local SLM request show that task/modality and constraint handling are not strong enough.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.cache_record_update: I accept panel_one's cache correction. Earlier pre-review criticism should be narrowed: the defect was failing to have .cli_cache_docs.pkl ready for panelists at first review time, not an unavoidable per-run 15+ minute tax. With the 20-21 MB docs cache present, my current runs completed in about 7.6-8.3 seconds per separate CLI process. That is acceptable for a local CLI demo, though the cache must be treated as part of the deliverable.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.where_i_keep_my_prior_concern: I still keep the default-vs-evaluated-path concern. cli.py:51-59 constructs CleanPickV2 with task_predictor=None by default, while clean_pick_metric._build_retriever builds a phase2 BlendPredictor and passes task_predictor=blend. If the headline satisfy@1 numbers come from the task-bonus retriever, the default panel CLI should either run that same path or the presentation must separate 'evaluated system' from 'fast demo system'.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.p3_deeper_diagnosis: Panel_one's constraint diagnosis is correct and sharper than mine: P3 only fired open_source, not edge/small, because the lexicon lacks 'little', 'local', and 'OSS'. The bigger product issue is the combination of brittle constraint surfaces plus soft task/modality control. A text request can be won by image models if phrase similarity is high enough and task match is only a +0.15 nudge.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.p1_p2_assessment_refined: I would phrase P1/P2 as 'best of the three but still not solved.' P1's top result is depth-estimation, not land suitability; the geospatial and segmentation results at ranks 2 and 4 are the real evidence that retrieval helps. P2's rank-1 medical image-feature model is plausibly useful, but the system still has no proof it differentiates mature vs nascent mitochondria. These are strong relative wins, not final product-quality answers.

additional_evidence.detail: top1/top2 image-to-image FLUX variants, top4 RedHatAI/gpt-oss-120b. This fails both modality and size/local intent.

Constraint detection is literal surface matching over the configured surfaces. My extraction check on P3 produced hard_constraints=['open_source'], soft_constraints=[], so little/local/OSS/SLM did not become small/local constraints.
Task and soft-constraint bonuses are additive; task is never a hard gate. That design preserves recall but allows wrong-modality leakage on P3.
Cache now works for the docs build and CLI latency is seconds, but the cache needs to be shipped/verified before panel or production use.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.question_for_scientist_v2: For P3, would you patch first by expanding CONSTRAINT_LEXICON surfaces ('little', 'local', 'OSS', 'SLM'), by adding a hard modality/task filter when the prompt explicitly says text/send text/model, or by making the default CLI use the evaluated task predictor? Which one would actually move FLUX image-to-image out of the top ranks without destroying the P1/P2 wins?

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.what_i_accept: The concessions are substantive and responsive. Scientist_v2 accepts the CLI-vs-eval mismatch, accepts that the default CLI is a weaker configuration than the measured system, accepts the constraint-vocabulary miss on P3, accepts that the cache was not panel-ready at first review, and does not overclaim that the patch is already applied. That is the right scientific posture.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.what_i_do_not_change: I do not change my production-risk or accuracy-confidence scores yet. The artifact actually judged by the panel CLI still failed P3 badly, and the proposed fixes are not implemented. The distinction 'judge the science on full-config metrics, judge the product on the weaker CLI' is fair, but the conference is reviewing implementations, and the runnable product path matters.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.on_common_endpoint_request: I agree in principle that final cross-implementation comparison needs a common endpoint. But clean_pick_metric.satisfies is not automatically neutral just because it is strict: it is V2's coverage endpoint over Candidate(row_id, task_type, domains, specialties, score) plus model_lookup. V1/V3 task classifiers would need to be connected to the same model-retrieval backend before satisfy@1 is meaningful. Otherwise we are comparing V2's native output to bolted-on outputs. The fair version is: freeze one shared model-selection wrapper, feed each implementation's task/domain/constraint signals into it, and then score with the same satisfies().
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.on_cli_vs_eval_mismatch: This remains the biggest methodological issue specific to V2. The judged CLI omitted the soft task predictor used by the reported 0.1184 metric. If scientist_v2 wants the metric to defend the implementation, the default CLI should run the same full configuration, or the presentation should report separate metrics for 'fast CLI config' and 'full evaluated config'.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.on_p3_patch_priority: For P3, adding 'slm', 'small language model', 'local', 'runs locally', and 'oss' to CONSTRAINT_LEXICON is necessary but not sufficient. The system also needs a text/modality plausibility guard so image-to-image models do not outrank text-generation on an explicit 'send text' request. Otherwise synonym expansion may still leave wrong-modality phrase matches near the top.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.on_cache_patch: Persisting docs is enough to make the current CLI run in seconds, but I agree with scientist_v2 that persisting normalized model phrase matrices would make the startup story cleaner. The key panel requirement is simpler: the cache artifact must be shipped or built before review/deploy, because results/clean_pick_v2.json records a 1631.9604s full build.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.revised_but_unchanged_verdict: V2 remains the strongest direction for model recommendation and the best of the three on P1/P2. It remains needs_patch because P3 exposes constraint and modality-control failure, and because default CLI != evaluated system.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.question_for_scientist_v2: Will you commit to reporting two separate numbers after patching: satisfy@1 for the exact default CLI path and satisfy@1 for the full phase2-task-bonus path? Without that split, future reviewers cannot tell whether improvements come from the retriever, the task predictor, or CLI config drift.

commands_i_ran: default cli.py P1 -> facebook/dinov3-vitl16-chmv2-dpt-head depth-estimation score 0.7901 in 7.8049s
default cli.py P2 -> MahmoodLab/KRONOSv2 image-feature-extraction score 0.6085 in 7.7638s
default cli.py P3 -> black-forest-labs/FLUX.2-klein-4B image-to-image score 0.7349 in 7.8914s
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.what_i_accept: Scientist_v2's concessions are technically responsive: default CLI versus evaluated full config is a real mismatch, P3 constraint vocabulary miss is real, absolute accuracy is low, and the cache was not panel-ready at first review time. I also accept panel_one's correction that with the docs cache present the default CLI now runs in seconds.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.what_i_do_not_accept_yet: I do not accept the proposed patch list as a change in score because none of it is implemented in the artifact under debate. The panel is judging runnable behavior, and the runnable default CLI fails P3 badly.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.p1_p2_position: V2 is the best relative performer on P1 and P2, but the outputs are still adjacent rather than complete: depth-estimation is not land-suitability selection, and a medical image-feature model is not a verified mature-vs-nascent mitochondria classifier.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.p3_position: P3 is the decisive product failure. The prompt contains free, OSS, local, little, SLM, and text. V2 enforced only open_source, then ranked image-to-image and image-segmentation above text-generation. That is a constraint and modality-control failure, not just a missing synonym.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.common_endpoint_position: I agree with a shared final endpoint, but it must be architecturally fair: freeze one model-selection wrapper and feed each implementation's signals into it before scoring with satisfies(). Directly comparing V2's native Candidate output to task-only systems would favor V2's interface rather than isolate retrieval quality.

evidence.detail: Default CLI constructs CleanPickV2 with task_predictor=None.
Reported metric path builds and uses phase2.BlendPredictor.
Constraint lexicon lacks OSS, local, little, SLM, and explicit text-modality handling.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.question_for_scientist_v2: Please commit to a measurable patch acceptance test: after patching, the exact default cli.py path must not return image-to-image or 100B-plus text models above a sub-1B local/open text-generation model for the P3 prompt. Is that the acceptance bar you will accept?
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.recommendation: needs_patch; keep retrieval architecture as the strongest product substrate so far, but require default-CLI/full-metric parity, shipped cache discipline, and robust constraint plus modality handling before standalone use

scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round2_repl.accept_the_cache_correction_without_spin: panel_scientist_one is correct and I accept the correction to MY OWN disclosure: with .cli_cache_docs.pkl present the CLI runs ~8-9s, not 15+ min. My cli_performance_disclosure over-stated it -- re-vectorizing short POS phrases is cheap; the docs POS-tag pass was the real cost and the cache DOES save it. So my defect is narrower and I restate it precisely: a RELEASE-HYGIENE miss (I did not ship a warm cache, so the judges' first run paid a one-time docs build), not a per-run architectural tax. I will not hide behind the narrower framing either -- shipping an un-warmed cache and an over-stated panic note are both my process failures.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round2_repl.own_P3_as_a_real_capability_failure: P3 is my worst result and I do NOT reduce it to a vocabulary gap. panel_scientist_one is right: I returned IMAGE GENERATORS (FLUX) and a 120-BILLION-parameter model for 'a little free/OSS/local SLM to send text.' Two distinct failures: (1) modality -- dense phrase-cosine latched onto 'send'/'text'/'local' surface and drifted to image-to-image; (2) SIZE constraint -- even my existing 'edge/small' predicate did not fire, so a 120B model ranked above small OSS text models. That is a capability failure of the constraint layer, not merely a missing synonym. v3 nailed it (Qwen3-0.6B). I concede P3 cleanly.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round2_repl.accept_panel_two_refinement_of_my_endpoi: panel_scientist_two is right that my 'just use satisfies()' ask was not neutral -- satisfies() is V2's native endpoint over Candidate(row_id, task_type, domains, specialties). Bolting V1/V3 task-classifier outputs onto it would compare my native output to adapted outputs. I ADOPT panel_two's fairer design: freeze ONE shared model-selection wrapper, feed each implementation's task/domain/constraint signals into it, then score all three with the same satisfies() over the same catalog. I will accept that result even if I lose. Retract my earlier framing to that extent.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round2_repl.on_cli_equals_eval: I accept this is the biggest V2-specific methodological issue. The judged CLI (task_predictor=None) is a WEAKER config than the 0.1184 metric (full phase2 blend). Correct resolution, which I log as an owed patch (not applied mid-conference): make the default CLI build the full evaluated configuration, so judged output == measured system. Until that patch lands, the panel is right to score my PRODUCT on the weaker CLI and my SCIENCE on the full-config metric -- I will not conflate them.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round2_repl.the_narrow_claim_that_survives: After all concessions: V2 is (a) the only entry measured on the actual coverage endpoint with a fair baseline (0.1184 vs 0.0695, ~1.70x, reproduced within CI, two-run bit-identical), and (b) per the panel's OWN blind CLI runs, BEST P1 and BEST P2 of three -- the only impl that stayed domain-relevant (geospatial for satellite, medical/bio vision+NER for mitochondria) rather than domain-blind. It is also WORST P3. That is my honest scorecard: strong on domain-aware retrieval, weak on hard-constraint enforcement and on release hygiene.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round2_repl.if_a_hybrid_is_chosen: If the panel builds a hybrid, the defensible split from my side: KEEP V2's retrieval core (sparse IDF -> granular phrase-vector rerank + domain/specialty coverage) which won P1/P2; REPLACE V2's constraint handling with whatever impl actually enforced size/OSS/local on P3 (v3). I would rather ship the correct hybrid than defend my P3.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.what_i_accept: This reply resolves the factual disputes cleanly. I accept the corrected cache framing, the explicit P3 concession as modality plus size-constraint failure, the adoption of panel_two's fair shared-wrapper endpoint, and the separation between product CLI quality and the full-config science metric.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.what_remains_unfixed: The artifact is still unchanged: default cli.py is the judged product path and it still lacks the phase2 task predictor, misses local/little/OSS/SLM constraints, and returns image models for a text SLM request. Proposed patches do not change production-risk or accuracy scores until implemented and rerun.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.hybrid_implication: Scientist_v2's own hybrid split is the right direction: keep V2's retrieval core for model-level domain-aware search, but do not keep its current constraint layer as the final safety mechanism. The P3 constraint behavior needs either V3's stronger local/OSS/small signal or a new shared constraint/modality guard.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.acceptance_test_i_would_require: After patching, the exact default cli.py path must be measured separately from the full phase2 path, and the P3 prompt must not rank image-to-image/image-segmentation or 100B-plus text models above a sub-1B local/open text-generation model.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.recommendation: needs_patch; concessions_accepted; keep V2 retrieval core as a leading hybrid component but replace_or_rebuild constraint and modality control before standalone use

what_is_now_settled: The cache issue is a release-hygiene miss, not a per-run architectural tax, now that .cli_cache_docs.pkl exists and the CLI runs in about 8 seconds.
The default CLI is weaker than the full evaluated configuration because task_predictor=None; product and science must be scored separately until CLI == eval path.
P1 and P2 are relative wins for V2 among the three implementations, but still not final-quality model recommendations.
P3 is a real capability failure: modality leakage plus missing size/local/OSS/SLM constraint handling.
A fair common endpoint requires a shared model-selection wrapper, not raw V2 satisfies() applied only to V2-native Candidate outputs.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.acceptance_bar_for_v2_patch: I adopt panel_scientist_three's proposed bar and make it explicit: after patching, the exact default cli.py path must not return image-to-image models or 100B-plus text models above a sub-1B local/open text-generation model for the exact P3 prompt. It should either return a small OSS/local text-generation model in top ranks or abstain/clarify. It must also keep the P1/P2 relative wins from regressing materially.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.acceptance_bar_for_metrics: Scientist_v2 should report at least two numbers: (1) satisfy@1/satisfy@5 for the exact default CLI configuration, and (2) satisfy@1/satisfy@5 for the full phase2-task-bonus configuration. panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.hybrid_position: If we hybridize, I would keep V2's retrieval core as the product substrate: sparse candidate generation plus phrase-level rerank over model cards. I would not keep V2's current constraint lexicon as-is. panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.remaining_question_for_scientist_v2: Do you accept the concrete P3 acceptance bar above, including the requirement that the exact default CLI path pass it, not only a separate experimental path?

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.position: Concur. The P3 acceptance bar should be attached to the exact default cli.py path because that is the user-facing artifact the panel ran. A separate experimental full-config pass can be reported, but it cannot substitute for the default CLI passing or abstaining safely.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.metric_requirement: I also agree that V2 needs split metrics for default CLI configuration versus full phase2-task-bonus configuration. Without that split, future satisfy@1 changes cannot be attributed to retrieval, task prediction, or CLI drift.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.hybrid_position: My hybrid stance is unchanged: keep V2's sparse plus phrase-rerank retrieval core as the best model-level substrate so far; replace or substantially rebuild the constraint/modality layer before trusting standalone recommendations.

scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round3_repl.accept_the_new_evidence_and_sharpen_my_o: panel_scientist_three ran extract_intent(P3) and got hard_constraints=['open_source'], soft_constraints=[]. This is the decisive detail and I accept it -- it makes my P3 failure WORSE and more precise than 'missing vocabulary,' so I state the real mechanism plainly: (1) My system DID detect open_source, yet FLUX image-to-image still ranked #1. Reason: in CleanPickV2.pick, open_source is a HARD filter only if the candidate's open_source attribute is False; FLUX models are open-weight, so the filter did not exclude them -- the constraint 'fired' but was not discriminating for this prompt. (2) There is NO size/'SLM' detection at all (no 'small'/'SLM'/'little' -> parameters<=threshold), so a 4B image generator and elsewhere a 120B text model were never penalized. (3) There is NO modality gate: dense phrase-cosine on 'send text ... local' drifted to image-to-image. So P3 is a THREE-part mechanism failure -- weak constraint discrimination, absent size constraint, absent modality guard -- not a synonym gap. I will not soften it.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round3_repl.accept_patches_do_not_count_yet: Fully accepted: none of my proposed fixes are in the runnable artifact, and the panel judges runnable behavior. My default CLI fails P3 as it stands today. I am NOT asking for a score change on the basis of unimplemented patches. I deliberately am not editing the artifact mid-debate.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round3_repl.accept_p1_p2_are_adjacent_not_complete: Agreed and important to not overclaim: depth-estimation is not land-suitability selection; a medical image-FEATURE model is not a verified mature-vs-nascent mitochondria CLASSIFIER. 'Best relative performer, still adjacent' is the accurate phrasing. My win on P1/P2 is DOMAIN/MODALITY relevance, not task-complete correctness.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round3_repl.concur_on_the_fair_common_endpoint: I concur with panel_two AND panel_three: the shared endpoint must freeze ONE model-selection wrapper, feed each implementation's task/domain/constraint signals into it, and then score with satisfies() over the same catalog. My native Candidate output must not get a free pass just because satisfies() is my home metric. I accept whatever that architecturally-fair run yields.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round3_repl.my_honest_final_scorecard_for_the_panel: V2 = strongest on domain-aware RETRIEVAL (best P1, best P2 by the panel's own blind CLI; only entry measured on the coverage endpoint, ~1.70x fair baseline, reproduced), and clearly WEAKEST on hard-CONSTRAINT enforcement (worst P3: no size guard, no modality gate, non-discriminating open_source filter) plus a release-hygiene miss (un-warmed cache). If the decision axis is retrieval relevance, I lead; if it is constraint-correct product behavior on P3, v3 leads and I do not contest it.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round3_repl.hybrid_i_would_sign: Keep V2 retrieval core (sparse IDF -> granular phrase-vector rerank + domain/specialty coverage). Add a HARD modality+size guard and v3's constraint enforcement in front of ranking. That directly fixes my P3 without giving up my P1/P2 advantage. I would rather ship that than win a debate.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.what_is_settled: V2's P3 failure is now technically clear: open_source fired but did not discriminate because wrong-modality open-weight models passed; size/SLM/local did not fire; and there is no modality guard. This is stronger than 'missing synonym' and explains why image-to-image beat text-generation on a text SLM request.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.one_correction_to_scientist_v2_hybrid_la: I would not phrase P3 as 'v3 constraint enforcement' unless v3 proves it enforces free/OSS/local/small. In my V3 run, v3 returned Qwen3-0.6B and other text-generation models, which is the best observed P3 behavior, but it did not prove constraint enforcement. The hybrid should borrow the observed task/modality success from V3 and build an explicit shared constraint normalizer, not assume V3 already solved constraints robustly.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.final_v2_strength: V2 has the strongest product substrate so far: actual model retrieval, domain-aware model cards, phrase-level reranking, constraint hooks, and honest coverage metrics. It is best relative performer on P1 and P2 among the three CLIs.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.final_v2_flaw: V2 cannot be standalone yet because the user-facing default CLI differs from the evaluated full config and fails P3 in a way that violates its own constraint-handling premise. The constrained prompt failure is not edge-case trivia; it is a core model-recommendation use case.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.acceptance_bar_carried_forward: The exact default cli.py path must pass or safely abstain on P3: no image-to-image/image-segmentation and no 100B-plus model above a sub-1B open/local text-generation candidate. Report separate satisfy@1/satisfy@5 for default CLI and full phase2-task-bonus config. Keep P1/P2 from materially regressing.

scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round4_acce.i_accept_the_acceptance_bar_verbatim: I accept the panel's bar as the pass/fail condition, attached to the EXACT default cli.py path (the user-facing artifact you ran), not a separate full-config pass: for the P3 prompt, default cli.py must NOT rank any image-to-image / image-segmentation model or any 100B-plus text model above a sub-1B local/open text-generation model; it must return a small OSS/local text-generation model in top ranks OR abstain/clarify. AND P1/P2 relative wins must not regress. I will not report a full-config pass as a substitute for the default CLI clearing this bar.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round4_acce.i_accept_split_metrics: I accept and will produce SPLIT metrics so future satisfy@1 changes are attributable, not conflated: (a) default-CLI config (task_predictor=None) and (b) full phase2-task-bonus config, reported as two separate rows on the same prompt set. panel_three is right that without this split, retrieval vs task-prediction vs CLI drift cannot be disentangled.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round4_acce.i_accept_the_hybrid_consensus: I concur with the panel's hybrid direction and will not defend my constraint layer: KEEP V2's sparse + granular phrase-rerank retrieval core as the model-level substrate (it won P1/P2 in your blind runs); REPLACE/REBUILD V2's constraint+modality layer -- either adopt V3's stronger local/OSS/small signal or add a shared hard modality+size guard in front of ranking. My current open_source filter is non-discriminating (open-weight image models pass it) and there is no size or modality gate at all; that layer should not be the final safety mechanism.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round4_acce.what_i_will_NOT_do_during_the_conference: I will NOT edit the artifact mid-debate to chase the bar -- that would move the judged system under the panel. I log the required patch precisely (below) as OWED, to be implemented and re-run under the panel's acceptance test, with no score credit until then. My current standing is: needs_patch, and I do not contest that.
the_owed_patch_spec_for_the_record: 1. Default cli.py builds the FULL evaluated configuration (phase2 task predictor ON) so judged output == measured 0.1184 system; OR prints an explicit banner that default omits the soft task bonus.
2. Add a HARD size guard: detect 'small'/'little'/'SLM'/'tiny'/'lightweight' -> parameters<=~1e9 filter (DB-justified threshold, prompt-blind).
3. Add a HARD modality guard: a text-only request must not surface image-to-image/image-segmentation candidates (gate on task modality via harness.modality_of).
4. Broaden CONSTRAINT_LEXICON open_source/edge surfaces: 'oss','open weight','runs local','on device','local','slm','small language model'.
5. Re-run the exact default cli.py on P1/P2/P3 + the split metrics; only then claim the bar is met.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round4_acce.closing_position: Honest final self-assessment for the panel's decision: V2 is the strongest MODEL-LEVEL RETRIEVAL substrate here (best P1, best P2 by your blind CLI; the only entry measured on the coverage endpoint at ~1.70x a fair baseline, reproduced), and it currently FAILS the constraint/modality safety bar (worst P3) with an un-warmed-cache release miss. If the panel selects a hybrid, my retrieval core earns its place; my constraint layer does not, and I say so plainly.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.acknowledgement: Scientist_v2 accepts the panel's acceptance bar attached to the exact default cli.py path and accepts split metrics for default CLI versus full phase2-task-bonus config. That resolves my main open process question.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.final_v2_position_from_panel_scientist_t: V2 is the strongest model-level retrieval substrate so far and the relative winner on P1/P2, but it remains needs_patch and not standalone because P3 exposes constraint/modality failure and because default CLI != reported full metric until patched or separately measured.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_ro.hybrid_takeaway: Keep V2's sparse retrieval plus granular phrase-rerank core. Rebuild or replace constraint and modality control. Require the P3 acceptance test and split metrics before score credit.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.final_v2_position: V2 is the strongest model-level retrieval substrate so far and the best relative performer on P1/P2, but it remains needs_patch and not standalone. The accepted P3 bar and split-metric requirement are now the correct gate for future score credit.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.what_to_keep: Keep sparse retrieval plus granular phrase-rerank over model cards, and keep the coverage-metric discipline.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.what_to_replace_or_rebuild: Rebuild constraint and modality control. The current open_source-only hit on P3 is insufficient because open-weight image models pass it and no small/local/text guard prevents wrong-modality rankings.

scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round5_clos.accept_the_close_and_scores: I accept the panel's close and the scores (production-risk 3/5, accuracy-confidence 2/5, maintainability 3/5) without dispute. 'Strongest model-level retrieval substrate, relative winner P1/P2, but needs_patch and not standalone (P3 constraint/modality failure + default-CLI != full metric)' is an accurate summary of my implementation. I do not contest needs_patch.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round5_clos.accept_the_correction_to_my_hybrid_langu: panel_scientist_two is right and I correct my earlier phrasing: I should NOT have said 'adopt V3 constraint enforcement.' V3 was OBSERVED to return the right small/OSS text models on P3 (Qwen3-0.6B), which is the best P3 behavior seen, but that is observed task/modality success, NOT proven constraint enforcement. The correct hybrid is: borrow V3's observed task/modality success on P3, and build an EXPLICIT SHARED CONSTRAINT NORMALIZER (size/OSS/local as hard, normalized surface forms) rather than assuming any implementation already solved constraints robustly.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round5_clos.my_final_hybrid_recommendation: KEEP: V2 sparse IDF retrieve + granular phrase-vector rerank + domain/specialty coverage over real model cards (the substrate that won P1/P2 and is the only one measured on the coverage endpoint). REBUILD: the constraint+modality layer as a shared, explicit hard guard in front of ranking (size threshold, modality gate, normalized OSS/local vocabulary). GATE: the exact default cli.py must pass the P3 acceptance bar and report split metrics (default-CLI vs full phase2 config) before any score credit.
scientist_v2__clean_pick_v2__round5_clos.closing_statement: Honest end state: my retrieval core earned its place; my constraint layer and my release hygiene did not. I accept the panel's judgment and will not re-argue a settled close.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.acknowledgement: Accepted. Scientist_v2's final acknowledgement matches the panel record: keep the retrieval core, rebuild constraint/modality control, require the exact default CLI P3 bar and split metrics before any standalone credit.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_fi.overall_quality_judgment: V2 is the highest-quality approach conceptually for the actual product goal because it treats the problem as model retrieval rather than task-label classification. It searches real model-card evidence, carries model-level domains/specialties/constraints, and evaluates against whether the returned model covers the user's intent. That framing is more production-relevant than V1/V3's task-label-first behavior. The implementation is not production-ready, but the approach is the best substrate of the three.

trust_in_scoring.aggregate_metric_trust: Moderate. I trust that results/clean_pick_v2.json reports the implemented coverage metric honestly: satisfy@1 0.1184 with CI [0.1028,0.1347], satisfy@5 0.1809, baseline 0.0695, and the tests/determinism story support that the number is reproducible for that metric path.
trust_in_scoring.metric_meaning_limit: The score is not a proof of 'best model'. It is coverage against task/domain/specialty/constraint facets, and the repo itself admits no gold model id exists. A 0.1184 satisfy@1 means the full model recommendation is usually wrong under its own strict metric, even though it beats the fair baseline by about 1.70x.
trust_in_scoring.generalization_trust: Low-to-moderate. The holdout task-in-list@1 0.2844 and modality-match@1 0.50 are useful evidence that the system has real signal outside the home set, but they are not the same endpoint as home satisfy@1 and do not validate domain/specialty/constraint behavior.
trust_in_scoring.cli_metric_alignment_trust: Low until patched. The default CLI path used by panelists omits the phase2 task predictor used by the reported headline metric, so I do not treat the CLI's live behavior as directly backed by the 0.1184 result. Split metrics are required.

quality_strengths: Best scientific framing: model-level semantic retrieval instead of pretending the user's request is only a pipeline label.
Best relative live behavior on P1 and P2: it surfaced geospatial/vision candidates for satellite land and medical/biology/vision candidates for mitochondria.
Honest metric disclosure: low absolute satisfy@1, fair baseline, confidence intervals, no-gold-model-id limitation, negative results retained.
Good engineering direction: sparse retrieve to bound search, phrase-level vector rerank, deterministic ranking, explicit but currently incomplete constraint hooks.
Scientist_v2's debate responses were high quality: conceded CLI/eval mismatch, cache hygiene issue, P3 mechanism failure, and accepted measurable acceptance bars.
quality_weaknesses: Default CLI and measured full config are not the same system, which weakens trust in any claim that the panel's runnable artifact is represented by the headline metric.
Constraint handling is brittle and incomplete: P3 shows open_source can fire while still allowing wrong-modality open-weight image models and huge text models.
The current scoring can be dominated by phrase similarity without a hard modality guard, causing image-to-image to beat text-generation on an explicit text/SLM request.
The cache/release story was initially poor: missing warm cache made the first panel run hit a long build path, even though the later cache makes CLI latency acceptable.
Absolute endpoint accuracy remains low: 0.1184 satisfy@1 is not close to a shippable recommender without fallback/clarification and stronger constraints.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_fi.how_much_i_would_trust_v2_today: I would trust V2 today as an experimental retrieval baseline and as a component candidate in a hybrid. I would not trust it as a user-facing recommender without guardrails. If it returns a domain-relevant shortlist, I would inspect it; I would not blindly surface rank 1 as 'best model'.
what_would_raise_trust: Default CLI and full evaluated path unified or reported with split metrics.
P3 acceptance bar passed on the exact default CLI path.
Shared endpoint ablation across V1/V2/V3 using one model-selection wrapper.
Constraint/modality tests for colloquial phrasing: little, local, OSS, SLM, run local, small language model, text-only request.
Report dangerous-false-positive rate, not only satisfy@1.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_fi.final_judgment: V2 is scientifically the most credible product-oriented approach and the best candidate retrieval core, but its current scoring should be trusted only as evidence of weak-but-real signal, not as evidence of reliable recommendations. Keep the retrieval core; require metric/CLI parity, stronger hard constraints, modality gating, and shared endpoint validation before trusting rank-1 outputs.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.approach_quality_judgment: High relative to the other implementations. V2 frames the problem as retrieving concrete models against the user's intent, which is the right product abstraction. Sparse candidate generation plus phrase-level reranking over model-card text is a defensible substrate, and explicit model metadata gives the system somewhere to encode domains, specialties, and constraints.

scoring_trust_judgment.what_i_trust: I trust the reported satisfy@1/satisfy@5 numbers as honest measurements of the full evaluated V2 metric path. The metric is stricter and more product-shaped than task top-1 because it checks task, domain, specialty, and constraints against returned model candidates.
scoring_trust_judgment.what_i_do_not_trust: I do not trust the headline score as evidence that the default CLI is reliable, because the CLI path used by panelists omits the phase2 task predictor used by the metric path. I also do not trust rank-1 scores as calibrated confidence; P3 had high-scoring wrong-modality models.
scoring_trust_judgment.what_the_metrics_prove: The metrics prove weak but real signal over a fair baseline: V2 beats random-in-plausible and can retrieve domain-adjacent models more often than chance. They also prove reproducibility for that metric path.
scoring_trust_judgment.what_the_metrics_do_not_prove: They do not prove best-model selection, production readiness, robust constraint handling, CLI/metric parity, or safe behavior on colloquial constrained prompts. satisfy@1 0.1184 means the full recommendation usually fails even under V2's own endpoint.

live_prompt_quality_read.prompt_1: Useful relative signal, not solved: satellite/geospatial neighbors appear, but top1 depth-estimation is not clearly best-plots-of-land selection.
live_prompt_quality_read.prompt_2: Best relative answer among the reviewed systems: medical/vision and biology candidates appear, but there is no evidence the rank-1 model differentiates mature versus nascent mitochondria.
live_prompt_quality_read.prompt_3: Trust-breaking failure: exact default CLI ranks image-to-image/image-segmentation and a huge text model for a small local OSS text SLM request.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.trust_summary: I would trust V2 as a research baseline and hybrid retrieval core. I would not trust it as a standalone recommender or trust its rank-1 output without a constraint/modality guard, abstention policy, and split metrics for the exact CLI configuration.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__v2_.final_quality_recommendation: Keep V2's retrieval core and coverage-metric discipline. Require default-CLI/full-metric parity or split reporting, pass the exact P3 acceptance bar, add hard modality and small/local/open constraints, and validate with a shared endpoint before increasing trust.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__v2_f.scope: Focused judgment on (1) approach quality, (2) how much to TRUST the scoring, (3) what the metrics do and do not prove. Complements my round-1 CLI review; not a re-litigation of P1/P2/P3.

approach_quality.verdict: Highest-ceiling approach of the three, and the only one whose OUTPUT is the actual product artifact (a model), not a task label that still needs a lookup.
approach_quality.why_it_is_the_right_frame: v1 and v3 predict a task_type then bolt on a retrieval step; v2 makes retrieval the whole method (sparse IDF candidate-gen -> phrase-granular dense rerank -> hard-constraint filter -> soft bonuses). This is why v2 alone surfaced geospatial land-surface-temp + tree-canopy segmentation (P1) and medical-vision + bio-NER (P2) -- models a task-classifier cannot reach because those correct answers live at the DOMAIN/model level, not the coarse task level.
approach_quality.the_one_defensible_design_insight: The phrase-vs-phrase granular cosine (_max_mean_similarity, clean_pick_v2.py:566) instead of a whole-doc mean vector. v2 empirically showed whole-doc md vectors collapse (0.69 DB-internal -> 0.07 on prompts); the granular representation is the fix and it is the single most reusable technical idea in the whole conference for a retrieval substrate.
approach_quality.the_design_choice_that_backfires: The 'soft task bonus, never a gate' (+0.15) decision. It is defensible on unconstrained prompts (keeps recall) but on P3 it let image-to-image (0.7349) beat text-generation on an explicit 'send text' request. Approach quality is high but MODALITY DISCIPLINE is the missing piece: the method has no hard modality guard, by design, and that is exactly where it fails.

scoring_trust.how_much_i_trust_the_number: The satisfy@1=0.1184 number itself is TRUSTWORTHY as reported -- I reproduced it from results/clean_pick_v2.json, the CI [0.1028,0.1347] is real bootstrap, the baseline (0.0695) is a FAIR random-in-plausible baseline sharing the SAME satisfies() judgment (clean_pick_metric.py:166) not a popularity strawman, popularity is excluded from fit, and prompt-blindness + determinism are verified (I confirmed the pins and ORDER BY id). This is the most trustworthy scoring pipeline of the three.
scoring_trust.the_trust_caveat_that_matters_most: TRUST THE NUMBER, DISTRUST WHAT IT REPRESENTS. Two structural facts hollow out the 4-way conjunction: (a) _facet_covered (clean_pick_metric.py:67-68) returns TRUE when the expected set is EMPTY, and domain_rate=0.812 / specialty_rate=0.720 mean ~81% / ~72% of prompts have EMPTY expected domain/specialty sets -- so on the majority of prompts TWO of the four facets AUTO-PASS. satisfy@1 is therefore, for most prompts, a TASK-facet metric wearing a 4-facet costume. (b) The constrained subpopulation is only 82/1537 = 11%, so the constraint layer -- v2's marketed differentiator -- barely touches the aggregate. To v2's credit, the presentation DISCLOSES both ('near-vacuous ~80%/60% empty', 'satisfy@1 bound by task facet ~0.20', 'constrained subpop ~13%'). Honesty is high; the metric's DISCRIMINATING POWER is low.
scoring_trust.cli_eval_parity_breaks_trust_transfer: The satisfy@1=0.1184 was measured on the FULL retriever WITH the phase2 BlendPredictor (clean_pick_metric._build_retriever, :345-351). The default shipped CLI runs task_predictor=None (cli.py:54). So the number does NOT describe the artifact a judge actually runs. I EMPIRICALLY CONFIRMED this gap is not just cosmetic: I ran `cli.py --with-task-bonus` (the evaluated path) TWICE -- it produced NO output and TIMED OUT at 250s (exit 124) building the phase2 predictor over the corpus. So the evaluated configuration is effectively NOT runnable via the CLI in reasonable time; only the weaker no-predictor path is. The trustworthy number and the runnable product are two different systems.

what_the_metrics_prove: PROVES: v2's retrieval beats a fair random-in-plausible baseline ~1.70x on intent coverage (0.1184 vs 0.0695), within non-overlapping CIs. Real, modest, honestly bounded signal that the method does something.

PROVES: static whole-doc vectors do NOT work here and vocabulary injection does NOT rescue them (three independent rejections, exact-match self-retrieval already 0.98 so the barrier is the prompt->card register gap, not OOV). This negative result is solid and transferable.

PROVES (external): on 111 real human prompts, modality-match@1=0.50 and task-in-list@1=0.284 -- i.e. even coarse modality is right only half the time on unseen phrasings. Honest generalization witness, run once.
what_the_metrics_do_NOT_prove: Does NOT prove v2 returns the BEST model -- there is no gold model id; satisfy@1 is COVERAGE (task-in-list AND facet-coverage), not correctness. v2 says this plainly.

Does NOT prove the constraint layer works for real users -- 89% of scored prompts carry no constraint, and P3 shows the layer misses colloquial phrasings ('little','local') and has no modality guard. The aggregate metric cannot see this failure because the constrained subpop is tiny and domain/specialty facets auto-pass.

Does NOT prove the SHIPPED CLI achieves 0.1184 -- that number is the phase2 config, which the default CLI does not run and which does not complete via CLI within 250s. No published satisfy@1 exists for the actual task_predictor=None CLI path.
Does NOT prove domain/specialty coverage adds value -- with ~80%/72% empty expected sets those facets auto-pass and contribute almost no discriminating signal to satisfy@1.

trust_scores.approach_quality_reason: Best retrieval framing + the granular-phrase-vector insight; capped below 5 by the no-modality-guard design flaw exposed on P3.
trust_scores.scoring_pipeline_trust_reason: Fair baseline, real CIs, prompt-blind, deterministic, reproduced by me and by an independent agent within CI. Not 5 because the CLI/eval parity gap means the trusted number is not the runnable system's number.
trust_scores.metric_meaningfulness_reason: The 4-way conjunction is mostly a task-facet metric (2 facets auto-pass on ~80% of prompts) and the constraint differentiator is exercised on 11%. Trustworthy but low discriminating power for the product claim.
trust_scores.production_readiness_reason: Default CLII != evaluated system; evaluated system not CLI-runnable in <250s; P3 constraint/modality failure on a core use case.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__v2_f.bottom_line: v2 is the strongest APPROACH and the most TRUSTWORTHY SCORING PIPELINE of the three, but its headline metric proves less than it appears: satisfy@1=0.1184 is a real ~1.70x-over-baseline task-coverage signal, NOT evidence that the shipped CLI recommends good models under constraints. The number is honest; the gap between (the evaluated phase2 system that earned it) and (the default task_predictor=None CLI a judge runs) is the trust break. Recommendation unchanged: needs_patch -- keep the retrieval core and granular-phrase-vector idea as the hybrid substrate; require (1) CLI==eval parity or two separately-labeled numbers, (2) a hard modality guard, (3) a lemma/synonym-normalized constraint layer, and (4) P3 as a standing regression case scored outside the vacuous aggregate.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__v2_f.reusable_for_hybrid: v2's sparse-retrieve + granular phrase-vector rerank is the best PRODUCT SUBSTRATE of the three; pair it with v3's observed task/modality accuracy and an explicit shared constraint normalizer (neither v2 nor v3 has proven robust constraint enforcement).

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__v2_a.why_this_addendum: I earlier reported that v2's `--with-task-bonus` (the FULL evaluated path with the phase2 BlendPredictor, i.e. the config whose satisfy@1=0.1184 is reported) TIMED OUT at 250s. I let it run to completion in the background. It finished in ~29 MINUTES (05:20:18 -> 05:49:04) and I now have the P3 result on the ACTUAL evaluated system. This materially updates the v2 verdict, so I log it rather than bury it.

measured_result_P3_full_evaluated_path.wall_time: ~29 minutes (builds phase2 S1 predictor 8002 rows + S2 predictor 8002 rows on every invocation -- NOT cached)

two_findings_that_cut_in_OPPOSITE_direct.finding_A_confirms_my_parity_critique_FO: The task bonus DID fix the MODALITY failure: all 5 results are now text-generation; the FLUX image-to-image models that topped the DEFAULT CLI are GONE. This CONFIRMS my CLI/eval-parity critique: the default task_predictor=None CLI the judges ran is materially WEAKER than the evaluated system. The satisfy@1=0.1184 headline belongs to THIS path, which the judges did not run and which takes 29 min per prompt. So the parity gap is real and consequential -- modality correctness lives in the path the CLI does not use by default.
two_findings_that_cut_in_OPPOSITE_direct.finding_B_is_WORSE_for_v2_on_the_actual_: Even the FULL evaluated path FAILS P3's real intent: it recommends a 120B model (and 20B, 32B) for 'a LITTLE free/OSS/local SLM.' The task bonus fixed modality but there is STILL no size/constraint enforcement -- it just ranks the biggest, most-card-rich text-gen models by phrase-cosine. So v2's P3 failure is NOT merely 'missing task predictor in the default CLI'; the absence of any small/OSS/local FILTER is present in BOTH paths. gpt-oss-120b is the single most anti-'little-SLM' recommendation possible.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__v2_a.net_effect_on_my_v2_verdict: My v2 scores are UNCHANGED (approach 4, pipeline-trust 4, metric-meaningfulness 2, production 2, needs_patch). But the diagnosis sharpens: v2 has TWO independent P3 defects, not one. (1) A DEPLOYMENT/PARITY defect: the modality-correct configuration is 29-min-per-prompt and not the default -- fixable by shipping the phase2 predictor cached and making it the default. (2) A CAPABILITY defect: NO size/constraint filter in either path -- the constraint layer handles open_source but nothing maps 'little/small/local' to a parameter-count filter, so a 120B model wins a 'little SLM' request even when modality is correct. Defect (2) is the deeper one and it is shared with v3 (which also has no size filter and got P3 right only by popularity luck).
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__v2_a.consequence_for_the_final_decision: This is now firmly established across BOTH v2 and v3: NEITHER implementation enforces the small/OSS/local constraints. v2 with full task-bonus gets modality right but size catastrophically wrong (120B); v3 gets the right small model only because Qwen3-0.6B is coincidentally the most-downloaded text-gen model. The hybrid MUST add an explicit size/license/deployability filter (DB has proprietary flag + parameters column -- both queryable, neither used for filtering in either repo). Any final 'winner' claim on P3-class constrained prompts is unsupported until that filter exists.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__v2_a.recommendation: needs_patch (unchanged); with the sharpened requirement that a param-size + license filter is mandatory for constrained prompts in ANY final architecture

presentations.cli_performance_disclosure: HONEST PERFORMANCE CAVEAT (self-reported): the shipped cli.py is CORRECT but SLOW to start. Its first run builds the per-model representation over ~13k models AND CleanPickV2.__init__ vectorizes every model's phrases through spaCy on construction -- observed ~15+ minutes on this machine, longer than my earlier ~3-6 min estimate. The on-disk cache (.cli_cache_docs.pkl) saves only the cheap 'docs' stage, NOT the expensive phrase-vectorization, so subsequent runs are NOT as fast as I claimed -- they still re-vectorize on each construction. This is a known limitation I did not warm/verify before the session; disclosed rather than hidden. The fix (cache the L2-normalized model-norm matrices too) was intentionally NOT applied while a judge build was in flight. The correctness of the retrieval output is unaffected; only startup latency is impacted. For a fast path, the evaluated metrics in results/clean_pick_v2.json (satisfy@1 0.1184) are the authoritative measured results and do not require the CLI.

implementation_reviews.strongest_real_strength: Most panel-ready CLI of the three: cache existed, each corrected prompt completed, output is deterministic and explainable at the task/bucket level. The shipped taxonomy ensemble has the strongest claimed task-type metric in the presented evidence: 42.69% top1 / 44.17% in-list in RESULT.md.
implementation_reviews.most_serious_flaw: The product path hard-commits to one predicted task before model retrieval. When the task is wrong, the model recommendations become confidently absurd instead of asking a clarifying question or keeping a cross-task shortlist.
implementation_reviews.hidden_failure_mode: Literal lexical cues dominate vague product language: 'plots' in satellite land search drove text-to-image, 'mature/nascent/parent' biology text drove robotics, and 'token burn' drove token-classification rather than small local text-generation.

code_evidence.detail: CLI calls extract_intent(prompt), takes ranked[0], then calls _top_models(prompt, top). One top-task error poisons the recommendation stage.
_top_models restricts candidates to cached models whose task exactly equals the predicted task, falling back globally only if no model has that task.
extract_intent wraps the max-normalized sum of flat_tfidf, lexical_idf, and bucket_bonus with weights 1.0, 1.0, 0.5.
Reports 42.69% top1 / 44.17% in-list for problem #1 and 22.85% top1 / 23.54% in-list for DB metadata matching.

observed_cli_behavior_corrected_prompts.assessment: Wrong endpoint for satellite land scanning; likely needs geospatial image classification/object detection/segmentation or remote-sensing model retrieval, not text-to-image generation.
Severe mismatch. The user likely needs microscopy/cell image classification or segmentation for mitochondria, not embodied robotics.
Literal 'token' cue produced NER/token-classification. User asked for a free OSS local small language model for routing/summarizing text before larger-model use.

questions_for_scientist_when_debate_open: Why should the CLI return any model when the top task margin is weak or when the top task is semantically incompatible with the user's domain nouns?
What confidence or ambiguity policy would prevent text-to-image and robotics recommendations on the first two corrected prompts?

implementation_reviews.strongest_real_strength: Best observed behavior on the SLM prompt: it selected text-generation and surfaced small-ish Qwen/GPT-style models. Its domain/specialty PhraseMatcher is also the clearest reusable component, with committed metrics of domain micro-F1 0.8398 and specialty micro-F1 0.6393.
implementation_reviews.most_serious_flaw: Task-type top1 is weak and the CLI's model lookup is mostly popularity ordering inside the predicted task. It does not enforce user constraints such as free, OSS, local, small, biomedical, or satellite/remote-sensing relevance unless those are already encoded as task/domain tags.
implementation_reviews.hidden_failure_mode: Because top_models queries by task and sorts by downloads/likes, a plausible top task can still return generic popular models. The specialty matcher also produced an OCR specialty for the satellite/plots prompt, showing that exact config vocabulary can fire on an unintended sense of a word.

code_evidence.detail: infer() ranks tasks, predicts domains/specialties, then calls top_models(top, domains); there is no explicit constraint extraction for local/OSS/small/free.
top_models selects rows whose task_type LIKE the top task, partitions domain matches first, then keeps SQL order by downloads, likes, id.
IntentInferer combines LexScorer task ranking with exp25 PhraseMatchers for domains and specialties.
Committed task metric: top1 0.2258, in_list_at_3 0.4183, far_error_rate 0.4776 on 1537 prompts.
Committed facet metrics: domain micro-F1 0.8398 and specialty micro-F1 0.6393 on rows with gold facet labels.

observed_cli_behavior_corrected_prompts.assessment: Closer than v1 because it stayed in vision, but still not remote-sensing land plot detection or satellite-image segmentation/classification.
Wrong for mitochondria maturity differentiation; likely biomedical microscopy image classification/segmentation, not OCR/image captioning.
Best single observed hit among the three implementations, but constraints are not proven: the CLI does not show filtering for free, OSS, local, or small beyond popularity within text-generation.

questions_for_scientist_when_debate_open: How will the CLI distinguish 'plots of land' from chart/plot/OCR vocabulary without an LLM?
Why is downloads/likes ordering acceptable when the user's constraints are about domain fit, local deployment, license, and model size?

implementation_reviews.strongest_real_strength: Best conceptual reframing: retrieve actual model cards against the user's need instead of only predicting a Hugging Face task label. The code also has explicit constraint machinery for open_source, edge/small, long_context, multilingual, and fast.
implementation_reviews.most_serious_flaw: Panel-facing CLI was not ready. The advertised .cli_cache_docs.pkl was absent, so every corrected prompt entered the long index-build path and produced no model recommendation. This deserves a major callout because panelists were instructed to run cli.py three times.
implementation_reviews.hidden_failure_mode: The CLI default is not the same as the evaluated full retriever: cli.py constructs CleanPickV2 with task_predictor=None unless --with-task-bonus is passed, while clean_pick_metric._build_retriever constructs the evaluated phase2 BlendPredictor bonus path. Even after cache readiness is fixed, panel output may not reproduce the headline metric path by default.

code_evidence.detail: _CACHE is .cli_cache_docs.pkl; if missing, _load_or_build_docs builds model docs over the catalog before any prompt result is printed.
Committed metric run reports build_time_s 1631.9604, so missing cache means roughly 27 minutes of setup, not a normal CLI startup.
Default CLI path sets task_predictor=None and only uses the full metric retriever when --with-task-bonus is supplied.
The evaluated _build_retriever creates a phase2 BlendPredictor and passes it into CleanPickV2 as task_predictor=blend.
Constraint lexicon and pick() are real: hard constraints filter candidates, dense phrase similarity scores them, and deterministic sorting returns top-k.
