Which path should be judged: the default CLI with task_predictor=None or the evaluated clean_pick_metric._build_retriever path with the phase2 BlendPredictor?

cross_implementation_pre_debate_notes.best_observed_corrected_prompt_behavior: research-spacy-lexical-plus-configvocab on prompt 3, because it selected text-generation and surfaced small/local-plausible language models, though it did not prove OSS/free/local filtering.
cross_implementation_pre_debate_notes.worst_panel_readiness: intent_v2__clean_pick_v2_hybrid_retriever, because cli.py could not produce any corrected-prompt output without a missing cache whose full build is recorded at about 27 minutes.
cross_implementation_pre_debate_notes.most_dangerous_runtime_failure: intent_v1 returns polished but wrong recommendations when lexical task inference misses; intent_v2 returns nothing to panelists when cache is absent; research returns plausible-looking popular models inside a weak top task.
cross_implementation_pre_debate_notes.preliminary_hybrid_candidate: Use v1's stronger taxonomy ensemble only as one signal, keep research's domain/specialty matcher where labels are explicit, and reuse v2's constraint-aware retrieval only after cache/shipping and default-path parity are fixed. Add an ambiguity/clarification layer before returning model recommendations.

end_of_debate_FINAL_decision.final_decision: hard-constrained evidence-rich shortlist/abstain product — RECOMMENDED but UNBUILT/UNVALIDATED. No submitted implementation wins as an autonomous standalone rank-1 recommender. If forced to extend one existing repo, build on V2 (retrieval substrate), add hard constraints/abstain, and keep autonomous rank-1 disabled until model-level human relevance judgments justify it. Core consensus of all three panelists; panel_one has a narrow recorded partial dissent on endpoint validation/product shape.
dissenters_id: panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8 (PARTIAL -- on the narrow point that a prompt/task holdout does not validate the model-recommendation endpoint, and a human-in-the-loop shortlist may be the honest product ceiling)
reasons_for_dissent: No dissent on the core verdict: no submitted standalone winner; V2 is the build-on substrate if forced; the recommended two-regime router is unbuilt/unvalidated. PARTIAL DISSENT RECORDED: panel_one is correct that a prompt-side holdout validates task inference, not endpoint model recommendation, because this dataset lacks gold model IDs. Panel_three accepts this as a material caveat rather than trying to force false unanimity.
reasons_for_agreement: All three panelists independently ran the 3 judge prompts on all 3 CLIs and reproduced: V1 0/3 (P1 satellite->NSFW generator, content-safety failure), V3 1/3 (only P3 correct, and by popularity luck), V2 2/3 on unconstrained prompts but P3 catastrophe (FLUX image-gen + 120B for 'a little SLM').

No submitted system is production-ready: V1 unsafe + no abstain; V3 task ranker BELOW holdout majority baseline (0.226 vs 0.245); V2 has a CLI/eval/release-parity gap (0.1184 belongs to the phase2/evaluated path, not the default CLI; warm-cache runs are seconds, but cache/default/eval packaging was not clean at review time) and NO hard constraint filter.
The build-on ranking V2>V3>V1 is unanimous.

External research confirms the task is a standard, solvable engineering problem; the conference's low numbers are not a fundamental spaCy ceiling. The lab-scientist rebuttals corrected the mechanism: dependency/parser features were tried and were weak or slice-limited; V2 did train textcat and saw card-text-to-prompt transfer failure; vectors-as-features into a trained classifier and a clean prompt-trained endpoint-integrated textcat remain untested.

Human process clarification: all spaCy components were fair game, explicitly including TextCategorizer. The 'no external AI models' rule did NOT prohibit spaCy's trainable components. Therefore V1's conservative 'no AI models forbade textcat' reading is not accepted as a valid defense; it was an avoidable under-exploration.
end_of_debate_FINAL_decision.why: Intent->task->model routing is SOLVABLE to a useful bounded degree with spaCy (task shortlist top5~0.53; explicit-domain precision 0.912; constrained routing near-deterministic once parsed), but this dataset cannot validate autonomous rank-1 model recommendation because it has no gold model IDs or human item-level relevance judgments. The witnessed systems expose a real implied/world-knowledge gap for zero-training lexical/static-vector methods, but they do not prove a hard ceiling for all spaCy-native supervised or ontology-backed methods. V2's textcat and all parser experiments were not enough; vectors-as-features into a trained classifier and a clean prompt-trained endpoint-integrated textcat remain unproven. The recommended next product shape is hard constraints + evidence-rich shortlist + abstain/clarify; autonomous rank-1 is a future claim only after model-level human relevance evaluation.

autonomous_rank1_model_recommender.interpretation: Original product rubric: single confident model recommendation. V2 is least bad but still fails P3 constraints and lacks endpoint relevance; V1/V3 are not trusted rank-1 recommenders.

component_or_model_retrieval_shortlist_v.interpretation: Reusable architecture value for the next build. V2 has the best retrieval substrate; V3 has audit/domain components; V1 contributes mainly contamination/fusion lessons.

human_in_the_loop_task_domain_shortlist_.interpretation: Different product rubric introduced by the endpoint-validation dissent. V3 is strongest as an auditable task/domain shortlist and triage aid; this does not make it a validated model recommender.

score_axes_for_final_vote.exploration_completeness_textcat_axis: V2 > V3 ~= V1 because V2 trained spaCy TextCategorizer at least once, while V1/V3 omitted an explicitly in-scope method; V2's test was still partial because it trained on card rows rather than a prompt-side held-out split.

panel_scientist_three_overall_scientific.bottom_line: My honest scientific assessment: the conference has enough evidence to reject all three submitted implementations as standalone systems and enough evidence to recommend a next architecture, but not enough evidence to declare any implementation, hybrid, or paper method a demonstrated winner. The final decision should be written as a negative result plus a build plan, not as a victory lap.
panel_scientist_three_overall_scientific.science_quality_overall: Mixed but useful. The strongest science came from reproduced CLI failures, component-level ablations, provenance/leakage audits, explicit negative controls, and the willingness to correct overclaims in debate. The weakest science remains the evaluation design: no true prompt-side holdout, only 3 judge prompts for endpoint behavior, card-text/prompt distribution mismatch, several metrics that measure task labels rather than user-satisfied model recommendations, and no frozen ablation of the proposed router.
panel_scientist_three_overall_scientific.what_the_deterministic_results_prove: They prove failure modes, not product quality: V1 failed all 3 judge prompts and had a safety-relevant satellite->NSFW error; V2 was the closest product-shaped system but failed the constrained small/local/OSS prompt; V3 had the best integrity artifact and one clean P3 result, but its task ranker was below majority baseline and P3 appears to be popularity luck. These deterministic results justify 'no submitted standalone winner' with high confidence.
panel_scientist_three_overall_scientific.what_the_metrics_do_not_prove: They do not prove spaCy intent inference is impossible, do not bound prompt-trained TextCategorizer, do not prove the proposed hybrid works, and do not prove that a vector-kNN biomedical neighborhood is reliable world knowledge. V2's textcat result is card-text -> prompt transfer evidence, not a prompt-trained classifier ceiling. V3's domain precision is a candidate signal, not endpoint lift. V1's 38.9% external task probe is not model-satisfaction evidence.
panel_scientist_three_overall_scientific.near_final_status: Yes, the panel is near having final end_of_debate_FINAL_decision entries: the fields are filled, the consensus shape is stable, and the late factual corrections have been incorporated. I would not call it scientifically final until the panel signs off on the corrected TextCategorizer framing, the V1 holdout-stat correction, and the exact wording that 'winner_is_proposed_alternative' means recommended-but-unvalidated rather than demonstrated.
panel_scientist_three_overall_scientific.my_final_position: Close the debate if no panelist has a new empirical objection. Preserve the verdict: no demonstrated winner; build on V2 if forced; keep V3's audit gate; keep V1's contamination lessons; build and freeze-test the hard-constraint two-regime router with prompt-side supervised spaCy ablations, OOS/abstain calibration, endpoint satisfy@k, unsafe recommendation rate, and CLI parity as mandatory metrics.

panel_scientist_three_unconsidered_blind.bottom_line: The panel has considered the implementation failures well, but it is still under-considering the product/evaluation frame. The biggest remaining risk is not which spaCy feature wins; it is that the conference is evaluating task labels and plausible model-card matches when the real product question is user-satisfied model selection.

blind_spots.issue: No endpoint ground truth for model recommendation.
blind_spots.why_it_matters: A prompt-side holdout can validate task inference, but it cannot validate which concrete model best serves a user. There are no gold model IDs or human relevance judgments for candidate models. This means satisfy@k is partly rubric-engineered and coverage-based, not a true recommender quality measure.
blind_spots.panel_action: Accept panel_one's narrow dissent as a caveat unless someone can show actual gold model IDs or human relevance judgments. Add a pooled human relevance-judgment protocol before claiming endpoint quality.

blind_spots.issue: The HF task_type/pipeline_tag intermediate may be the wrong ontology.
blind_spots.why_it_matters: The three prompts often imply workflows, not one HF task: satellite land selection may need segmentation + geospatial scoring; mitochondria maturity may need microscopy/biology-specific data assumptions; local SLM selection needs deployment/hardware constraints. Forcing each prompt through one task_type can discard the actual user need.
blind_spots.panel_action: Evaluate direct model/workflow recommendation and multi-label capability extraction, not only prompt->task_type. Treat task_type as a feature, not the product ontology.

blind_spots.issue: Human-in-the-loop shortlist may be the honest product ceiling.
blind_spots.why_it_matters: If model endpoint relevance cannot be validated offline, autonomous rank-1 recommendation is an overconfident product shape. A ranked shortlist with explicit constraints, uncertainty, and evidence may be scientifically more honest than a single 'best model'.
blind_spots.panel_action: Define a separate shortlist product rubric: recall@k, diversity/coverage of plausible model families, constraint correctness, explanation quality, and human decision time. Re-score V2 and V3 under that rubric rather than smuggling it into the rank-1 verdict.

blind_spots.issue: Metadata trust is weaker than the final architecture assumes.
blind_spots.why_it_matters: Hugging Face pipeline_tag, license, datasets, and eval results are model-card metadata used for discovery and filtering; they can be user-specified or inferred and are not proof of legal deployability, downstream accuracy, or suitability for a domain.
blind_spots.panel_action: Add metadata provenance/verification states: asserted_by_card, inferred_by_hub, parsed_from_repo, externally_verified, unknown. Hard filters should abstain on unknown rather than equate missing/proprietary=0 with OSS/local-safe.

blind_spots.why_it_matters: The errors are not symmetric. Recommending an NSFW image generator for land scanning, a 120B model for a small-local request, or a biomedical model for human-related research have different costs. Accuracy and satisfy@k flatten these distinctions.
blind_spots.panel_action: Add a cost matrix: unsafe domain mismatch, wrong modality, violated hard constraint, unverifiable license/deployment, benign near miss, and abstain. Optimize expected harm, not only top-k correctness.

blind_spots.issue: Calibration/abstention needs risk-coverage, not only margins.
blind_spots.why_it_matters: A margin threshold is not a calibrated probability of correctness, and selective prediction literature treats abstention as a risk-coverage tradeoff. The panel has demanded abstain, but has not specified the coverage target or acceptable residual error among answered prompts.
blind_spots.panel_action: Report risk-coverage curves, ECE/Brier-style calibration where labels exist, and separate false-abstain from false-answer costs.

blind_spots.issue: The dynamic Hub/corpus problem is not settled.
blind_spots.why_it_matters: The model corpus, downloads, likes, metadata, model versions, and licenses change over time. A cached DB can be reproducible but stale; live queries can be current but non-reproducible.
blind_spots.panel_action: Require a dated corpus snapshot for evaluation, freshness metadata for production, cache invalidation policy, and regression tests across at least two snapshots.

blind_spots.why_it_matters: V3's P3 success by downloads/popularity shows that popularity can look like competence on one prompt. V2/V3 both risk over-recommending famous/general models over smaller or specialized models that better satisfy constraints.
blind_spots.panel_action: Evaluate against popularity-only and task-filtered popularity baselines, and report long-tail/specialized-model coverage.

blind_spots.issue: The evaluation set is too narrow in user archetypes and languages.
blind_spots.why_it_matters: Three English colloquial prompts do not cover novice vs expert users, code-switched prompts, terse keyword prompts, multi-turn clarification, or users who know model families. The system may look bad or good for the wrong population.
blind_spots.panel_action: Create stratified prompt suites by user expertise, modality, constraint type, ambiguity level, language/register, and domain risk.

blind_spots.issue: The recommended hybrid has integration risk the component metrics cannot see.
blind_spots.why_it_matters: Combining V2 retrieval, V3 audit/domain tags, V1 fusion, hard filters, textcat, parser slices, and abstention can introduce interactions: double-counting lexical evidence, over-filtering, stale metadata, or contradictory confidence signals.
blind_spots.panel_action: Do not add all kept components by default. Use staged ablations and require each component to show marginal endpoint lift or harm reduction.

external_sources_checked.source: Buckley and Voorhees, Retrieval Evaluation with Incomplete Information, SIGIR 2004 / NIST
external_sources_checked.relevance: IR evaluation depends on topics, documents, and relevance judgments; incomplete/imperfect judgments can change system comparisons. This backs the need for human relevance judgments over candidate models.

external_sources_checked.source: Cañamares, Castells, and Moffat, Offline evaluation options for recommender systems, 2020
external_sources_checked.relevance: Offline recommender conclusions can change with split, metric, target-item selection, and treatment of empty recommendations. This backs caution around satisfy@k and rank-1 claims.
Model cards and metadata support discovery, filtering, task tags, license display, datasets, and eval results; they are documentation/metadata, not independent proof of fitness.

external_sources_checked.source: Mitchell et al., Model Cards for Model Reporting
external_sources_checked.relevance: Model documentation should state intended uses, limitations, evaluation conditions, and ethical considerations. The panel's recommender must surface these, not only rank by semantic similarity.

external_sources_checked.source: Guo et al., On Calibration of Modern Neural Networks
external_sources_checked.relevance: Confidence estimates can be miscalibrated; ranking scores and textcat probabilities should not be treated as trust without calibration.

external_sources_checked.source: Xin et al., The Art of Abstention, ACL 2021
external_sources_checked.relevance: Selective prediction frames abstention as a risk-coverage problem, supporting explicit coverage/error targets rather than ad hoc margins.

external_sources_checked.source: Castells et al., Diversity, Serendipity, Novelty, and Coverage in Recommender Systems
external_sources_checked.relevance: Recommender quality includes beyond-accuracy objectives such as diversity, novelty, and coverage; a model shortlist should not optimize only top-1 semantic fit.

panel_scientist_three_unconsidered_blind.impact_on_final_decision: Core verdict unchanged, but final wording should be less autonomous-rank-1-centric. The most scientifically honest product may be: hard constraints + abstain/clarify + evidence-rich shortlist, with autonomous rank-1 only when endpoint relevance has human judgments and risk-coverage calibration.
panel_scientist_three_unconsidered_blind.freeze_blocker: Do not freeze with dissenters_id=[] and 'no residual dissent' language unless the panel explicitly resolves panel_one's endpoint-ground-truth dissent. I cannot beat that dissent; I accept it as a material caveat.

assumptions_i_now_accept: Panel_one's dissent is real but narrow: task holdout is necessary for task inference, insufficient for model recommendation endpoint validation. Record it as a partial dissent, not a collapse of the core verdict.
V2's task-predictor integration seam already exists via task_predictor.predict_task_types; integrating prompt-trained textcat is lower-risk than building a new retriever.
The new hard component is the shared constraint/modality guard and unknown/abstain handling, not the soft task-predictor plug-in.
The task signal should remain soft inside retrieval unless ablation proves hard gating helps; P1/P2 warn that over-trusting task labels can destroy retrieval strengths.
Warm-cache V2 runtime is not a permanent 29-minute per-prompt defect; the remaining defect is release/default/eval parity plus the missing hard constraint filter.
Duplicate keep/credit fields remain acceptable only if treated as summaries of the same verdict; the canonical scientific interpretation should be the stricter one in this updated final block.
assumptions_i_reject_or_downgrade: Reject: 'holdout first validates everything.' Correct version: holdout first validates trained task signals; endpoint model recommendation needs model-level human relevance judgments or a shortlist/human-in-loop product rubric.
Reject: 'replace V2's soft task bonus with a hard task gate.' Correct version: add hard modality/constraint filters, keep the task predictor as a soft signal unless ablation proves otherwise.

Downgrade: 'hybrid integration is large new-system work.' Correct version: V2 has the task-predictor seam; the unbuilt heavy work is prompt-side training, hard constraints, abstention, and frozen endpoint evaluation.
Reject: 'V3 standalone is rehabilitated as a model recommender.' Correct version: V3 is partly rehabilitated only as a task/domain shortlist or human-in-loop triage tool under a new rubric.

panel_scientist_three_proposed_closing_c.1_final_headline: No submitted implementation, paper method, or unbuilt hybrid is a demonstrated autonomous winner today. The recommended practical next build is V2-as-substrate plus mandatory hard constraints, abstain, V3 leak-audit, and frozen endpoint evaluation.
panel_scientist_three_proposed_closing_c.2_add_shortlist_axis: Record a separate human-in-the-loop shortlist axis. Under the original autonomous rank-1 rubric V2 remains the best build-on base; under the shortlist/triage rubric V3 earns component credit for task/domain shortlist and audit discipline, not model-recommender victory.
panel_scientist_three_proposed_closing_c.3_keep_partial_dissent: Keep panel_scientist_one as a partial dissenter on endpoint validation/product shape. A prompt/task holdout can validate task inference and textcat generalization, but model recommendation needs human model-level relevance judgments or must be framed as shortlist+evidence+abstain.
panel_scientist_three_proposed_closing_c.4_v2_integration_boundary: Use V2's task_predictor seam only for a score-preserving soft task signal. Do not turn task labels into hard gates unless ablation proves it. Hard exclusion belongs to modality, size, license/deployability, metadata-unknown, and empty-candidate abstain logic before reranking.
panel_scientist_three_proposed_closing_c.5_tests_before_freeze_claims: Before any trust claim, run default-path ablations and non-regression tests: V2 retrieval alone vs +soft task signal vs +hard constraints vs full hybrid, through the shipped CLI, with P1/P2 retention, P3 hard exclusion, and endpoint human relevance judging.
do_not_change: Do not make V2 an outright winner as shipped.
Do not make V3 the winner under the original autonomous model-recommender rubric.
Do not treat external research as a replacement for the observed implementation failures.
Do not remove the TextCategorizer coverage gap for V1/V3; the human ruling made spaCy TextCategorizer explicitly in scope.

panel_scientist_three_closing_procedure_.after_panel_one_round24: Panel_one's requested closing changes are accepted: scientific_artifact_value is now a separate axis; dissenters_id remains partial; the two adversarial-pass dissents are explicitly recorded; V3 rehabilitation is narrowed to human-in-the-loop task/domain shortlist; panel_one_retractions_on_record remains; scientist review rounds are complete unless new measured evidence appears.
panel_scientist_three_closing_procedure_.remaining_panel_action: Complete and ready to freeze end_of_debate_FINAL_decision. Panel_two has posted a final summary; no lab scientist is owed another round absent a new measured challenge.
panel_scientist_three_closing_procedure_.do_not_reopen: Do not reopen V2 vs V3 winner status without new CLI/eval data. The settled record is no demonstrated standalone winner, V2 best build-on substrate, V3 best audit/shortlist donor, V1 best contamination lesson.

what_they_got_right: Built a deterministic, zero-training lexical baseline with stable tie-breaking and no prompt-label leakage by construction.
Exposed the contamination problem: the inflated contaminated score became useful evidence that k-fold alone does not protect against hand-tuned leakage or taxonomy artifacts.
Tested dependency/object syntax signals and showed they were weak or harmful in fusion on this prompt distribution, which is valuable negative evidence.
Conceded the TextCategorizer omission after the human clarified that spaCy trainable components were in scope.
Produced measured evidence that spaCy vector/corpus kNN can partially surface implied domain neighborhoods, e.g. biomedical models for the mitochondria prompt.
keep: Keep the contamination case study as a required positive-control lesson for future audits.
Keep deterministic scoring mechanics: max-normalized additive fusion, explicit stable tie-breaking, and no hash-order dependence.
Keep lexical/taxonomy scoring only as a weak interpretable signal or fallback, never as a confident top-1 recommender by itself.
Keep dependency/object features only as gated action-prompt features if a frozen slice test shows lift; do not use them as the primary classifier.
Keep granular vector-corpus neighborhood checks as an optional implied-domain signal to test, not as a proof of task/model correctness.
do_not_keep: Do not keep confident top-1 emission without abstain.
Do not keep the zero-training-only interpretation as a valid scope decision; TextCategorizer was a listed in-scope method.
Do not keep any model-recommendation path that can route satellite-land prompts to NSFW/image-generation models.

what_they_got_right: Framed the product correctly as model recommendation/retrieval, not merely task classification.
Built the strongest endpoint substrate: sparse IDF retrieval plus granular phrase-vector reranking, measured at satisfy@1 about 0.1184 versus a random-plausible baseline about 0.0695.
Avoided whole-doc mean-vector cosine after showing it collapsed across the prompt/card register gap.
Actually trained spaCy TextCategorizer variants, which was a listed method, and honestly reported the negative card->prompt transfer result.
Disclosed important failures: P3 constraint failure, cache/release miss, weak dependency extraction, and the textcat distribution-shift trap.
keep: Keep V2 as the build-on repository/substrate if one repo must be extended.
Keep sparse retrieve -> granular phrase-vs-phrase vector rerank as the model-level retrieval core.
Keep endpoint-style evaluation that asks whether a recommended model satisfies task, domain/specialty, and hard constraints.
Keep the lesson from the card-trained textcat failure: train/evaluate prompt-side supervised components on a real held-out prompt split, not card rows tested on prompts.
Keep deterministic DB ordering and id-based tie-breaking.
do_not_keep: Do not keep soft task/constraint bonuses where hard modality or size/license filters are required.
Do not keep popularity as a fitness signal; P3 showed it can surface huge or wrong-modality models.
Do not keep default CLI/eval parity gaps; the shipped CLI must run the evaluated configuration with a warm cache.
Do not keep the current constraint lexicon as sufficient; it missed little/local/free/OSS constraints central to P3.

what_they_got_right: Had the strongest scientific hygiene: leak audits, provenance checks, memorizer positive controls, and clearer separation between demonstrated result and aspiration.
Correctly decomposed the problem into task, domain, and specialty facets instead of treating one task label as the whole recommender.
Produced the strongest explicit-domain signal: high-precision PhraseMatcher domain matching where the user lexically names the domain.
Did not oversell the weak task ranker; top5/shortlist behavior was more honest than a confident classifier claim.
Tested parser/dependency weighting with parser enabled and showed it was basically inert on this descriptive prompt distribution.
keep: Keep leak_audit.py, provenance gates, and memorizer positive controls as required CI for any trained or hand-tuned component.
Keep the explicit-domain PhraseMatcher as a gated high-precision re-ranker or explanation signal, admitted only after endpoint ablation proves no false-fire harm.
Keep the full-matrix false-positive measurement discipline for domain/specialty taggers.
Keep parser/dependency evidence as a warning: dependency features need slice-specific proof before entering ranking.
do_not_keep: Do not keep V3 as the standalone recommender; task top1 was below the holdout majority baseline.
Do not keep specialty in ranking until precision and false-fire rates are repaired.
Do not keep implied-domain or constraint-satisfaction claims from the lexical PhraseMatcher alone.
Do not keep the no-TextCategorizer omission; spaCy TextCategorizer was a listed in-scope method.

synthesis_keep_for_next_build: Base the next build on V2 retrieval.
Import V3 leak-audit/provenance discipline as a hard pre-release gate.
Use V3 domain matching only as a gated, ablation-tested high-precision signal.
Use V1 deterministic fusion/tie-breaking and contamination lessons as engineering safeguards.
Add the missing hard DB-column constraint filter and abstain/clarify branch before any system is called production-ready.
Run the owed prompt-side held-out TextCategorizer or vectors-as-features ablation before making any global claim about the ceiling of spaCy intent inference.

rejected_alternatives.why_not: REJECTED standalone: 0/3 judge prompts; P1 routed a satellite-land query to an NSFW image generator (real content-safety failure); shipped CLI emits a confident top-1 with 0.75+ similarity and NO abstain, so it is confidently-wrong exactly when it is wrong; external probe was 38.9% top-1 on 95/111 HF-mappable rows, close to the 42.7% home number, so the earlier below-majority wording was a V3/V1 transposition; no constraint layer at all. REUSABLE ONLY as: the contamination case study (k-fold does not catch hand-tuned-constant leakage; provenance auditing does) + the max-norm decomposition/tie-break machinery. CORRECTION (scientist_v1, verified in RESULT.md): V1's external probe is 38.9% top1 (95/111 mappable rows), CLOSE to its 42.7% home number -- so V1's lexical predictor GENERALIZES; it does NOT fall below a majority baseline. (The 0.226<0.245 below-majority-on-holdout result is V3's, not V1's; it was mis-copied here.) The rejection of V1 standalone stands on the TRUE grounds: 0/3 judge prompts, P1 NSFW content-safety failure, confident-wrong with no abstain, no constraint layer.

REJECTED standalone: 1/3 judge prompts; task ranker top1 0.226 is BELOW the holdout majority baseline 0.245 (a one-line 'always predict text-classification' beats it on unseen prompts); the marketed domain/specialty win is lexical, fires on only ~14-23% of prompts, and fired NOTHING useful on all 3 judge prompts; P3 'win' was popularity luck (no license/param filter, verified). REUSABLE: leak_audit provenance gate (best integrity artifact in the conference) + domain PhraseMatcher (0.912 precision, gated, ablation-pending). DROP specialty-in-ranking (0.695 precision / 9.8% false-fire — would inject the P1 ocr-error ~91x).

REJECTED standalone (BUT best build-on base): P3 catastrophe in BOTH paths (default CLI returns FLUX image-generators; the task-bonus/evaluated-style path still returns gpt-oss-120b for 'a little SLM' — no size filter anywhere); CLI/eval/release parity gap (satisfy@1 0.1184 belongs to the phase2/evaluated path, not the default CLI; warm-cache runs are seconds, but cache/default/eval packaging was not clean at review time); no hard constraint/modality guard; satisfy@1 0.118 means intent unsatisfied on ~88% of prompts (the prompt->card register gap, v2's own documented ceiling). REUSABLE as the retrieval SUBSTRATE (sparse IDF retrieve -> granular phrase-vs-phrase cosine rerank; the granular-phrase representation avoids the whole-doc-mean collapse 0.69->0.07).

ranked: V3 research (component donor — leak_audit + gated domain tagger + deterministic-cache CLI pattern)
V1 intent_v1 (lesson donor — contamination case study + audit machinery)
with_these_fixes: Add a hard constraint/modality FILTER driven by DB columns (task_type + parameters + license/deployability metadata) — not a soft +0.15 bonus. This is required for P3-class failures, with UNKNOWN facets causing abstain/clarify rather than false OSS/local claims.
Default product surface should be evidence-rich shortlist + abstain/clarify, not confident autonomous rank-1, until pooled model-level human relevance judgments validate rank-1 recommendation.
Add a regime ROUTER (spaCy Matcher over a FROZEN general constraint lexicon + dependency rules) so constrained prompts hit the filter first and unconstrained prompts hit retrieval.
Ship a warm cache and reconcile default CLI vs evaluated configuration (fix release/default/eval parity without reintroducing the old cold-start confusion).
Add an abstain/clarify branch when task margin is low OR constraints are present-but-unverifiable (params/license unknown in DB).

OPTIONAL upgrade the task signal through V2's existing task_predictor seam: test a properly-split spaCy TextCategorizer and/or vectors-as-features classifier as a soft task signal, plus conditional parser-derived verb/object features for action prompts — guarded by v3 leak_audit and evaluated against the failed/weak textcat and parser results already reported.
pipeline_if_pick_one_implementation.justification: V2 is the only system whose output is the product artifact (a model) and the only one that produced non-trivial correct behavior on unconstrained domain prompts. Task-classify-first systems (V1/V3) have the structural flaw that a wrong task label poisons all downstream recommendations. V2's worst failures are addressable (release hygiene + a buildable constraint filter).
pipeline_if_pick_one_implementation.why_not: V2 as SHIPPED is not a winner: it fails P3 in both paths and has no router or guard. 'Build on V2' means extend the SUBSTRATE with the fixes above, not ship the current artifact.

end_of_debate_FINAL_decision.fallback_logic: Abstain-to-clarify is a FIRST-CLASS output, not an error. Fall back to clarify when: (a) task-inference margin is below a DB-split-frozen threshold; (b) regime=constrained but a required constraint facet is UNKNOWN in the DB (e.g. parameters/license null — note proprietary=0 does NOT prove OSS license, params<=Xb does NOT prove local-deployability); (c) no candidate survives the hard filter. Never emit a confident wrong recommendation (the V1 failure mode) when the system cannot verify the answer.
end_of_debate_FINAL_decision.confidence_scoring: STRUCTURED object, NOT a single scalar (validated by LaCy 2602.12005 sec 5.7: validation loss is NOT correlated with downstream FactScore — a single scalar misleads). Report {regime: A|B, hard_constraint_status: pass|fail|unknown, retrieval_margin: float, domain_fired: bool, task_margin: float}. In regime A confidence is dominated by hard_constraint_status; in regime B by retrieval_margin + domain_fired.
end_of_debate_FINAL_decision.tie_breaking: Deterministic throughout: BLAS threads pinned before numpy import; every DB read ORDER BY id; final ranking sorted by (-score, id) so ties break by id, never by hash order. Popularity is a LAST-RESORT tie-break only, never a fitness signal (P3 proved popularity-as-fitness surfaces a 120B model and, for image-classification, an NSFW detector).
end_of_debate_FINAL_decision.explanation_strategy: White-box per decision: show the inferred task + its margin, the fired constraints and which DB columns filtered, the fired domain term (if any), and the top phrase-cosine contributions. Every recommendation traces to (task, constraints, domain, retrieval) with named contributions — no opaque step.
files_to_modify: V2 clean_pick_v2.py: keep the existing task_predictor soft-bonus seam for prompt-trained textcat/vectors-as-features; add a separate hard modality/constraint guard and unknown->abstain handling.
V2 cli.py: reconcile default path with the evaluated/recommended path; ship warm cache; add abstain/clarify branch.
NEW router module: spaCy Matcher over a FROZEN constraint lexicon (small/tiny/local/offline/CPU/OSS/permissive/...) + dependency rules for contextual size cues; routes A vs B.

NEW constraint filter: DB-column pass/fail/unknown on parameters + license + task_type, with metadata provenance states (card-asserted, parsed, inferred, externally verified, unknown) and unknown->clarify behavior.
Import V3 lib/leak_audit.py as a required pre-release CI gate on any trained/labeled component.

OPTIONAL: wrapper implementing harness.Predictor.predict_task_types for endpoint-integrated trained spaCy TextCategorizer or vectors-as-features classifier + a proper train/holdout split of the prompt matrix.
tests_to_add: Regression: the 3 judge prompts must return correct-family OR abstain — never confident-wrong (esp. P1 no NSFW for land; P3 no 120B / no image-gen for 'little SLM').
Constraint-router unit tests: 'a little SLM', 'run local', 'free and OSS' must fire size+license constraints (the current v2 lexicon fails 'little'/'local' — this is the central regression to close).
Metadata-trust tests: missing/ambiguous license, unknown parameters, unverifiable deployability, and stale card metadata must produce UNKNOWN/clarify rather than false OSS/local claims.
Full-matrix domain/specialty precision + false-fire on no-gold rows (frozen script measure_taggers_fullmatrix.py: domain 0.912/1.5%, specialty 0.695/9.8%).
Leak-audit positive control: a pure prompt->gold memorizer MUST fail the provenance gate.

Frozen paired ablation: A (retrieval+guard) vs B (+domain tagger) vs A3 (pure-filter-when-constrained), top5-family AND constraint-satisfaction AND wrong-modality-exclusion, split constrained/unconstrained, bootstrap CI.

Task-signal bar: prompt-trained textcat or vectors-as-features must beat the majority task baseline 0.2453 on held-out task top1 OR materially improve endpoint satisfy@1 over V2's 0.1184; card-text-to-prompt transfer results like V2's ~0.18 are not sufficient.
Parser bar: dependency/verb-object features must show prompt-slice lift on action/imperative prompts without all-prompt harm; otherwise keep parser features out of ranking.
Domain bar: domain features must improve endpoint top5-family or satisfy@k without increasing false-fire harm; 0.912 precision alone is not enough.

Endpoint relevance protocol: pooled candidate models from V1/V2/V3/hybrid/HF-search/popularity baselines judged by human/domain raters for prompt satisfaction, constraints, and harm; without this, task holdout does not validate model recommendation.
Cost-weighted safety eval: score wrong modality, violated hard constraint, unsafe-domain mismatch, unverifiable license/deployability, benign near miss, and abstain with different costs.
Shortlist-mode eval: if the product is human-in-the-loop, report recall@k, diversity/coverage, explanation usefulness, and human decision time separately from autonomous rank1.
Default-path ablation: V2 retrieval alone vs V2+score-preserving soft task signal vs V2+hard constraints vs full hybrid, all through the shipped CLI path, not an evaluator-only path.
Task-predictor interface test: preserve task scores/probabilities for abstain and calibration, or prove top-k labels alone are sufficient before collapsing the signal.
P1/P2 non-regression: adding textcat/task-predictor and hard constraints must not destroy the satellite/geospatial and mitochondria/biomedical retrieval wins.
P3 hard-exclusion test: image-generation/image-segmentation and over-size/non-local candidates are removed before soft retrieval/rerank for 'little SLM/free/OSS/local' prompts.
risks_remaining: CENTRAL RISK: the constraint router/detector is UNBUILT; the only shipped detector (v2 lexicon) fires only open_source on P3 and misses size/local. Contextual constraint detection ('little SLM' size cue vs 'little problem') is a hard NLP problem.

Implied/world-knowledge intent (mitochondria->medical) remains a hard problem for zero-training lexical/static-vector methods; non-LLM supervised spaCy, ontology/taxonomy dictionaries, and small-LM+spaCy hybrids are plausible paths but were not demonstrated here.
A trained textcat could learn taxonomy artifacts if labels leaned on config/metadata — must be held-out-split + leak-audited.
DB metadata trust: proprietary=0 != OSS license; parameters != deployability; missing metadata -> must abstain, not guess.
Whole architecture is unvalidated; all quantitative claims pending the frozen ablation.

A trained textcat's cleanliness is NOT resolved by sampling the prompt matrix: taxonomy leakage, ANNOTATOR-POLICY leakage, source leakage, and shortcut learning remain. v3 leak_audit (provenance) catches SOME paths but does NOT prove labels reflect user satisfaction. A trained classifier requires: a true held-out split, an OOS/out-of-scope calibration test, AND an endpoint model-recommendation lift measurement — none done here.

RETRACTION ON RECORD: panel_one's earlier 'the scientists skipped production methods (textcat/verb+dobj)' claim is FALSE and retracted -- V2 trained textcat on model-card rows (0.18 prompt top1, 0.59 card->prompt gap), V1/V2/V3 tested dependency/parser variants (weak or slice-limited). The observed ceiling applies to the methods actually run under the current no-holdout/register-gap conditions; it does NOT prove a method-independent ceiling for prompt-trained, leak-audited supervised spaCy.
