how_to_use_findings_from_conference.summary: Intent->task->model routing with spaCy is a solvable, standard engineering problem, but none of the submitted systems demonstrated a trustworthy autonomous endpoint recommender. The corrected record is sharper than the earlier blanket claim: V2 did train textcat and all three lines tried parser/dependency variants, but those attempts were weak, overfit, or slice-limited; vectors-as-features into a trained classifier and a clean prompt-side textcat remain untested. The recommended next product is a hard-constrained, evidence-rich shortlist/abstain system built on V2 retrieval with V3 leak-audit discipline; autonomous rank-1 requires later model-level human relevance judgments.
how_to_use_findings_from_conference.which_strategy_to_infer_user_intent_give: CORRECTED: a trained spaCy TextCategorizer is NOT a free win here -- V2 measured card-trained textcat at 0.18 prompt top1 with a 0.59 DB-dev-to-prompt gap (WORSE than v3's 0.226 lexical). The binding problem is the NO-HOLDOUT eval + prompt/card register gap, not a proven limit of prompt-trained textcat. The honest strategy: keep the interpretable lexical/retrieval core, ADD a hard DB-column constraint filter + abstain, and ONLY revisit a trained classifier if a TRUE prompt-side held-out split is constructed AND leak-audited -- because V2's existing textcat demonstrates distribution shift, not endpoint lift.
how_to_use_findings_from_conference.strengths_and_weaknesses_observed_from_s: STRENGTHS: deterministic, interpretable, fast (ms warm), CPU-only, no-leakage-by-construction when config-derived, excellent for high-precision explicit-constraint/entity/domain matching (Matcher/PhraseMatcher 0.91 precision). Granular vector-corpus retrieval can partially surface implied domain neighborhoods. WEAKNESSES as used here: whole-doc/static-mean vector baselines collapsed across the prompt->card register gap (0.69->0.07; injection HURT); config-vocab matching is closed-taxonomy and brittle to colloquial phrasing; parser/dependency signals were tried but weak or high-abstain on this descriptive prompt distribution; card-text-trained textcat showed distribution shift and did not prove endpoint lift.
how_to_use_findings_from_conference.what_the_scientists_all_failed_to_consid: CORRECTION (this field previously OVERCLAIMED): the scientists did NOT all skip the production methods. V2 trained a TextCategorizer on model-card rows (memorized: db-dev 0.77 -> prompt 0.18, gap 0.59); V1, V2 AND V3 all tested dependency verb+dobj/SVO with the parser ENABLED (v1 ~38% standalone but hurt fusion; v2 SVO 0.038/91%-abstain; v3 exp20a parser-ON verb+dobj in_list@3 0.3856 vs 0.3842 baseline = +0.14pp inert). CORRECTION: the parser was NOT uniformly 'disabled' -- it was enabled for these dependency experiments and the method was MEASURED INERT on descriptive prompts. My earlier 'disabled parser is why numbers are low / verb+dobj would break the ceiling' causal story is FALSE and retracted; verb+dobj helps only short imperative commands, not the descriptive prompts this eval is dominated by. The human clarified that ALL spaCy components, explicitly including TextCategorizer, were not only fair game but a listed method the scientists were told to research, experiment with, and compare in order to find the best approach. Therefore V1/V3 cannot use 'no AI models' as a rule-based excuse for not training textcat. What they genuinely lacked was (a) a TRUE prompt-side HOLD-OUT SPLIT and OOS calibration, (b) a hard DB-column CONSTRAINT FILTER (P3 decidable from columns; none built it), (c) abstain-to-clarify as a first-class output, (d) vectors-as-features or prompt-trained textcat tested as endpoint signals, and (e) the option of a small-LM+spaCy hybrid (banned by the no-LLM rule). The accuracy ceiling is demonstrated for the methods actually run, not for all spaCy-native supervised possibilities.
how_to_use_findings_from_conference.other: The single most transferable lesson is the leak-audit provenance discipline: k-fold does not catch hand-tuned-constant or taxonomy-artifact leakage; only provenance auditing does. This becomes MORE important, not less, when moving to a trained classifier. | HUMAN CLARIFICATION (authoritative): ANYTHING from spaCy -- including TextCategorizer by name -- was an EXPLICITLY LISTED method to research and experiment with; it did NOT count as an external AI model, and all three were told to try the spaCy possibilities and find the best. Therefore scientist_v1's 'no AI models -> I avoided textcat' reading was a MISSED INSTRUCTION, not a valid constraint. V2 gets real credit for training a spaCy textcat, but its model-card-row setup is not a decisive rejection of prompt-trained, endpoint-integrated textcat or vectors-as-features; it is evidence of card->prompt distribution shift and label-granularity risk. The finding stands sharper: V1/V3 failed to test a listed method, V2 exposed one textcat trap, and the owed experiment remains prompt-side, leak-audited, endpoint-integrated supervision plus hard constraints.

proposed_alternatives_outside_of_impleme.description: Trained spaCy TextCategorizer (bag-of-words + neural-CNN-attention ensemble) over a properly train/holdout-split prompt matrix, as the primary task-inference signal.
proposed_alternatives_outside_of_impleme.why: Production-standard spaCy intent method; supervised, still spaCy+builtins/no-LLM; worth testing as an endpoint-integrated task signal. V2's textcat result proves this method class was touched, but not validated as a clean model-recommendation component.
proposed_alternatives_outside_of_impleme.why_not: Requires clean held-out labels; V2's card-text-trained textcat showed card->prompt transfer failure, which does not bound a prompt-trained textcat but warns against naive training; risks learning taxonomy artifacts if labels leaned on config/metadata (must be leak-audited); does not by itself solve implied-domain prompts or model selection.

proposed_alternatives_outside_of_impleme.description: Dependency-parse verb + direct-object (dobj) extraction for the intent core ('send text'->(send,text)->text-generation).
proposed_alternatives_outside_of_impleme.why: Deterministic, interpretable, and may help action/imperative prompts (P1 'scan', P3 'send'). Lab responses show dependency variants were measured, so the correct next step is conditional use rather than a blanket parser fix.
proposed_alternatives_outside_of_impleme.why_not: Already weak in reported experiments (V1 38.2% standalone but hurt fusion; V2 0.0377 with 91% abstain; V3 +0.14pp no-op). Fails noun-heavy descriptive prompts (P2 'mitochondria' has no task-bearing object); a partial tool, not a fix for implied intent. IMPORTANT: v1 already TESTED this (harness_syntax.py) and it scored 25.8% top1 vs 42.7% for lexical fusion -- so as a STANDALONE task signal it is measured-WEAKER, not an untried win. It belongs only as a fused minor signal for action/imperative prompts.

proposed_alternatives_outside_of_impleme.description: Small-LM + spaCy hybrid (LaCy-style, 2602.12005): use a small language model for semantic/world-knowledge cases that spaCy lexical/vector/ontology routes do not settle, with spaCy for fast deterministic structure.
proposed_alternatives_outside_of_impleme.why: A path for implied TASK/world-knowledge disambiguation when no lexical, vector-corpus, ontology, or supervised spaCy signal is sufficient; LaCy shows spaCy+small-LM can outperform spaCy-only in its delegation setting.
proposed_alternatives_outside_of_impleme.why_not: Violates the conference's 'no-LLM' constraint; only viable if that constraint is relaxed.

recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.description: PRIMARY (best DEMONSTRATED task signal on prompts): v3 exp02 lemma/IDF-cosine lexical scorer, abstaining on no shared lemma, FUSED (max-norm, v1's operator) with a hard modality gate.
recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.why: On PROMPTS it is the best measured task signal that does NOT require training on the prompt matrix (0.226 top1, top5 0.53); it got P3 right. V2's card-trained textcat transferred WORSE to prompts (0.18), but that does not bound a prompt-trained textcat. Interpretable, deterministic, no-holdout-safe.
recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.why_not: Below majority baseline on holdout standalone; a shortlist not a confident classifier; must be fused + gated.

recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.description: CONDITIONAL (not primary): a trained spaCy TextCategorizer -- ONLY if a TRUE held-out split is built first.
recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.why: Production-standard method AND still spaCy/no-LLM. BUT V2's actual run trained on DB model-card rows: db-dev 0.77 -> prompt 0.18, DB-dev-to-prompt gap 0.59, worse than v3's 0.226 lexical. So prompt-side textcat is a conditional experiment, not a demonstrated primary.
recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.why_not: Without a source/near-duplicate-controlled prompt holdout (the eval has none: labels==scoreboard), a trained classifier can learn taxonomy or annotation shortcuts. V2's measured 0.59 gap shows card-text -> prompt distribution shift, not prompt-trained textcat failure. Do NOT adopt textcat until a real prompt holdout + leak-audit exist and it beats the lexical baseline ON PROMPTS.

recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.description: FUSION: v3 exp02 lemma/IDF-cosine lexical scorer (abstains on no shared lemma) plus dependency verb+dobj only as a gated action-prompt feature, max-normalized additive fusion (v1's operator, not RRF, not a hard gate).
recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.why: Deterministic fallback; got P3's task right where v1/v2-default failed; LaCy confirms one lexical signal is insufficient -> fuse. Dependency data is mixed: V1 syntax got 38.2% standalone, V2 dep-SVO got 0.0377 with 91% abstain, and V3 POS/dependency upweighting moved in_list@3 only 0.3842->0.3856.
recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.why_not: Lexical top1 0.226 is a shortlist not a classifier; below holdout majority baseline standalone. Dependency should be conditional on imperative/action prompts, not treated as a general production-standard fix.

recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.description: Separate hard modality/constraint guard before final ranking, while preserving V2's task_predictor soft-bonus seam for task evidence.
recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.why: P3 proved a soft task bonus alone lets image-gen win a text request; V2's later code review also showed the soft task_predictor seam is useful for plugging in prompt-trained textcat/vectors without rewriting retrieval. The right design is soft task evidence PLUS hard exclusion for wrong modality and violated hard constraints.
recommended_algorithm_for_task_extractio.why_not: Depends on correct constraint/modality detection and trustworthy metadata; unknown or low-margin cases must abstain/clarify rather than hard-filtering on false certainty.

recommended_algorithm_for_domain_extract.description: spaCy PhraseMatcher over a FROZEN config domain vocabulary, multi-label, abstains when nothing fires (v3 exp25).
recommended_algorithm_for_domain_extract.why: Measured 0.912 operational precision, 1.5% false-fire on 1235 no-gold rows (reproduced by a frozen script + independently by scientist_v3). High-precision, deterministic, non-circular (misses 27% of gold).
recommended_algorithm_for_domain_extract.why_not: Low coverage (14.4% fire), 0/3 judge prompts; purely lexical so implied domains never fire. This is a lexical coverage limit, not proof that supervised spaCy or ontology-backed methods cannot recover implied domains. Report on full matrix, gate behind fired-domain check, admit to ranking only if ablation-positive.

recommended_algorithm_for_domain_extract.description: OPTIONAL: trained textcat for domain, or a small-LM step, to reach implied domains.
recommended_algorithm_for_domain_extract.why: Only path past the lexical ceiling for prompts that don't name their domain.
recommended_algorithm_for_domain_extract.why_not: textcat needs domain-labeled data + leak-audit; small-LM violates no-LLM rule.

recommended_algorithm_for_specialty_extr.description: Same PhraseMatcher mechanism as domain, restricted to AUDIT/EXPLANATION output ONLY, never model ranking.
recommended_algorithm_for_specialty_extr.why: Measured full-matrix precision 0.695, false-fire 9.8% (6.5x domain), 26 disjoint fires; P1's wrong 'ocr' (from 'scan') is one of 91 false positives.
recommended_algorithm_for_specialty_extr.why_not: Too imprecise to rank on; wiring into ranking injects the P1-class error ~91x. Keep as explanation only until a frozen precision repair proves ablation-positive.

recommended_algorithm_for_model_extracti.description: TWO-REGIME shortlist router: (A) constrained prompts -> hard DB-column filter (task_type + parameters + license/deployability metadata) FIRST, phrase-cosine only as intra-filter tie-break; (B) unconstrained -> v2 sparse IDF retrieve + granular phrase-vs-phrase cosine rerank, domain-gated. Default output is evidence-rich shortlist + abstain/clarify, not autonomous rank-1.
recommended_algorithm_for_model_extracti.why: P3 (constrained) is a hard filter problem once task+constraints are known; P1/P2 (unconstrained) need retrieval (filter-first gave an NSFW detector for mitochondria). The endpoint-validation dissent is now accepted: without gold model IDs or human relevance judgments, the scientifically honest product is a constrained shortlist with evidence and abstention. Corroborated by Blended RAG 2404.07220 + RAS survey 2509.10697 sec 3.2.1/3.2.4 (flagged as corroboration; verdict rests on the reproduced two-regime experiment).
recommended_algorithm_for_model_extracti.why_not: CENTRAL UNVALIDATED RISK: router constraint detector currently fires only open_source on P3 (misses size); metadata is not automatically trustworthy (proprietary=0 != OSS, params<=2B != deployable); router unbuilt, must be frozen-then-scored; retrieval regime itself only ~0.118 satisfy@1 and that is a coverage proxy, not true endpoint relevance. Autonomous rank-1 remains disabled until pooled model-level human relevance judgments validate it.

scientist_v1.got_right: V1 got the leakage/contamination problem right. Its strongest contribution is negative-control science: ordinary k-fold scoring and attractive metrics can miss hand-tuned leakage, provenance matters, and a model recommender needs auditable decomposition rather than only a high score.
scientist_v1.why_it_matters: This is the core guardrail for any later trained TextCategorizer or vector-feature classifier. The more powerful the trained signal, the more important V1's contamination lesson becomes.
scientist_v1.limits: V1 did not produce a safe recommender: 0/3 judge prompts, unsafe P1, no abstain, and no hard constraint layer.

scientist_v2.got_right: V2 got the product abstraction right: the system must return actual model recommendations, not merely task labels. It also produced the best build-on substrate: sparse retrieval plus granular phrase-level reranking over model/card evidence.
scientist_v2.why_it_matters: V2 is the only implementation whose core interface matches the product surface. It also gets scientific credit for trying spaCy TextCategorizer and reporting the negative card-to-prompt transfer result.
scientist_v2.limits: V2 is not a winner as shipped: P3 failed badly, the evaluated path and default CLI diverged, and no hard constraint/modality guard exists.

scientist_v3.got_right: V3 got integrity and bounded evaluation right. Its leak_audit/provenance gate is the strongest reusable safeguard, and its explicit-domain PhraseMatcher is a real high-precision, low-coverage component when scoped correctly.
scientist_v3.why_it_matters: V3's audit discipline is the right CI gate for any trained/labeled component; its domain tagger is useful only when the domain is explicitly named and ablation-positive.
scientist_v3.limits: V3 is not a winning recommender: task top1 is below majority on holdout, P3 was popularity luck, and specialty tags are too false-positive-prone for ranking.

keep_as_components: Max-normalized fusion/decomposition machinery, if re-fit on clean data.
Dependency/verb-object experiment as slice evidence, not as a primary feature.
keep_as_negative_evidence: Unsafe satellite->NSFW failure as a permanent regression test.
Dependency syntax result as evidence that parser features need gating and must prove slice lift.
do_not_keep: Shipped top-1 recommender behavior.
Any route that can emit unsafe/cross-domain models without abstention.
Any interpretation that TextCategorizer was forbidden; the human clarified it was explicitly allowed.

keep_as_components: Sparse IDF candidate retrieval plus granular phrase-vs-phrase reranking.
Model-card/index substrate as the Regime-B open-prompt retrieval layer.
The reported TextCategorizer result as evidence about card-text-to-prompt transfer, not as a final component.
keep_as_negative_evidence: P3 120B/image-generation failure as the central constraint-regression test.
CLI/eval parity gap as a release-quality regression test.
Textcat card->prompt gap as a warning to train on prompt splits, not card rows, when evaluating prompt intent.
Dependency-SVO high-abstain result as evidence parser features cannot be primary.
do_not_keep: Soft task bonus as a substitute for hard modality/constraint filtering.
Default CLI path diverging from evaluated path.
Popularity or model size as an implicit fitness proxy for constrained local/OSS requests.

keep_as_components: leak_audit provenance gate and memorizer positive control.
High-precision explicit-domain PhraseMatcher, gated and ablation-tested before ranking.
Human-in-the-loop task/domain shortlist and triage surface under the separate shortlist rubric; keep as auditable menu support, not as a validated model recommender.
keep_as_negative_evidence: Task ranker below majority baseline as a reminder that top-k usefulness does not imply top-1 winner.
Specialty tagger false-fire rate as evidence it belongs in audit/explanation only.
Parser/POS upweighting no-op as evidence dependency features need slice-specific validation.
do_not_keep: Popularity-driven P3 behavior.
Specialty tags in ranking before precision repair.
Task-label-only output as a finished autonomous rank-1 product surface.

cross_implementation_keeps: Hard DB-column constraint filtering for constrained prompts.
Evidence-rich human-in-the-loop shortlist as the default product surface until model-level human relevance judgments justify autonomous rank-1.
Prompt-trained TextCategorizer or vectors-as-features classifier only as a held-out, leak-audited ablation.
Granular vector-corpus retrieval as a possible implied-domain support signal, not proof of task/model correctness.
Endpoint satisfy@k as a coverage proxy, plus human relevance judgments, unsafe recommendation rate, constraint precision/recall, metadata-trust handling, and CLI parity as required metrics.

conference_result.winner_is_existing_implementation_if_mod: true — V2 as the build-on base WITH mandatory fixes (hard constraint/modality filter + regime router + cache/parity + abstain/evidence-rich shortlist). This is the pragmatic answer if a system must ship; it is not a claim that any repo wins as-is.
conference_result.winner_is_proposed_alternative: true-but-UNVALIDATED — hard-constrained evidence-rich shortlist/abstain product (V2 retrieval substrate + hard DB-column constraint filter + V3 leak-audit gate + optional prompt-trained task signal). Recommended architecture, not a demonstrated product; the constraint router, metadata-trust layer, and endpoint human relevance evaluation are unbuilt and are the central risks.
conference_result.winning_reason: No submitted artifact is safe and correct across the 3 judge prompts as an autonomous rank-1 model recommender; the best DEMONSTRATED result of the conference is a set of FALSIFIED CONSTRAINTS (what does not work) plus reusable components. The STRONGEST HYPOTHESIS (not a demonstrated system) is an evidence-rich shortlist/abstain product: constrained prompts route to structured DB-column filtering with unknown->clarify, unconstrained prompts use V2 retrieval, V3 leak_audit guards trained/labeled components, and optional prompt-side textcat/vectors/parser signals must earn their way in by ablation. Scientist rebuttals corrected the earlier overclaim: V2 did train textcat, but on DB model-card rows, and it only reached prompt top1 0.1815/0.1835 with a ~+0.60 DB-dev-to-prompt transfer gap; all three measured some dependency variant; none demonstrated a clean endpoint win from these methods. Model-level relevance remains unvalidated without human judgments.

autonomous_rank1_model_recommender_produ.meaning: Original product rubric. No implementation wins; V2 is least incomplete but fails P3 constraints and endpoint validation.

build_on_component_value.meaning: Engineering substrate rubric. V2 retrieval is the best base; V3 contributes audit/domain/shortlist components; V1 contributes mainly lessons.

human_in_the_loop_shortlist_value.meaning: Different product rubric. V3 is partially rehabilitated as a task/domain shortlist and audit tool, not as a validated autonomous model recommender; V2 may be stronger once model-level relevance is judged, but that evidence is absent.

scientific_artifact_value.meaning: Separate from product readiness. V1 rises for contamination/provenance falsification; V3 rises for leak_audit and positive controls; V2 is mid-to-high for retrieval metrics and the negative textcat transfer result. This axis must not be used to declare a product winner.

v2_wins_outright.best_case: V2 is the only product-shaped recommender, had the best P1/P2 behavior, and has an existing task_predictor seam plus DB columns for a small hard-filter patch.
v2_wins_outright.why_rejected: P3 failed in both paths; there is no hard modality/constraint guard, no endpoint model-level relevance data, and no shipped default path proving the evaluated configuration. V2 is build-on substrate, not winner.

task_type_frame_is_mis_specified.best_case: HF task_type is a lossy proxy for real user workflows, constraints, deployability, data modality, and domain risk.
task_type_frame_is_mis_specified.effect_on_verdict: This strengthens the shortlist/evidence/constraint framing and supports V2-style retrieval, but it does not make any submitted implementation a winner.

holdout_first_is_overweighted.best_case: A prompt holdout cannot validate concrete model recommendations because there are no gold model IDs.
holdout_first_is_overweighted.effect_on_verdict: Correct for the endpoint. The final now separates task-inference holdout from model-level human relevance judging.

v3_is_rehabilitated_by_human_in_the_loop.effect_on_verdict: V3 gains real shortlist/artifact credit but remains rejected as an autonomous rank-1 model recommender.

no_winner_means_unsolvable.effect_on_verdict: No winner means no demonstrated winner today. It does not mean spaCy intent inference, hard filtering, or model shortlisting is impossible.

major_flaws_found: V1: 0/3 judge prompts; P1 satellite->NSFW generator (content-safety); confident-wrong with no abstain; external probe 38.9% top1 on 95/111 HF-mappable rows is task-level only and does not rescue endpoint quality; no constraint layer.

V2: P3 catastrophe in both paths (image-gen + 120B for 'little SLM'); CLI/eval/release parity gap (0.1184 phase2/evaluated config is not the default CLI; warm-cache runs are seconds, but cache/default/eval packaging was not clean at review time); no hard constraint/modality guard; ~88% intent-unsatisfied (register gap).
V3: task ranker below holdout majority baseline; domain win invisible on all 3 judge prompts (lexical, 14-23% fire); P3 was popularity luck; specialty tagger 9.8% false-fire.

ALL THREE: none built a hard constraint filter; none combined a trainable task signal with leakage safeguards and endpoint model-recommendation evaluation. Earlier 'skipped textcat/dependency' wording was too broad: V2 trained textcat but on DB model-card rows, so its 0.1815/0.1835 prompt top1 and ~+0.60 DB-dev-to-prompt gap diagnose card-to-prompt transfer, not prompt-trained textcat capacity; V1/V2/V3 all measured dependency variants, with mixed-to-negative results (V1 38.2% standalone but hurt fusion; V2 0.0377 with 91% abstain; V3 +0.14pp no-op).

ALL THREE: bound by a NO-HOLDOUT eval (labels==scoreboard) for product-level claims. This is the ROOT flaw for future training: any prompt-trained TextCategorizer or vectors-as-features classifier needs a source/near-duplicate-controlled prompt holdout, OOS calibration, and endpoint evaluation. V2's existing textcat number is a card-text -> prompt distribution-shift result, not a prompt-side holdout result.

PROCESS / ASSIGNMENT-COMPLIANCE (authoritative, human-confirmed): the human clarified that anything from spaCy was fair game, that TextCategorizer was explicitly named/encouraged, and that it did NOT count as using an external AI model. Therefore V1 and V3 leaving TextCategorizer untested is an exploration-completeness failure. Exact PROMPT.md line-number claims should be repo-specific and should not rely on the earlier disputed line 91/line 26 quotations. V2 gets relative credit for trying spaCy TextCategorizer, but its card-trained experiment is only partial evidence and does not replace the owed prompt-side held-out ablation.
major_strengths_found: V2: model-card retrieval reframe (output is the product); granular phrase-vs-phrase vectors avoid the whole-doc-mean collapse; honest CI-backed metric with a fair baseline.

V3: leak_audit provenance gate + memorizer positive-control (best integrity artifact in the conference); high-precision explicit-domain PhraseMatcher (0.912); deterministic warm-cache CLI; the most honest write-up.
V1: the contamination case study (k-fold misses hand-tuned-constant leakage; provenance auditing catches it) — the single most transferable lesson; max-norm decomposition/tie-break machinery.
Cross-cutting: determinism and explainability are achievable with spaCy+builtins at ms latency on CPU.
best_parts_reused: V2: sparse-retrieve + granular phrase-vector rerank = Regime-B substrate.
V3: leak_audit provenance gate = CI integrity gate on all trained/labeled components; domain PhraseMatcher = gated Regime-B rerank (ablation-pending).
V1: contamination/provenance-audit discipline = guardrail for the whole system; max-norm fusion operator for the task shortlist.

NEW or not-yet-demonstrated as a clean endpoint system: leakage-controlled prompt-matrix TextCategorizer, vectors-as-features classifier, gated dependency signals for action prompts, hard DB-column constraint filter + regime router, and abstain-to-clarify as a first-class output. V2's textcat and the dependency experiments are data points, not validated final components.

scientist_v1__intent_v1: The contamination catch + lesson: built an 86.9% modality track, found its TASK_BOOSTS were cue phrases lifted from scored prompts, quarantined+deleted it. LESSON (most transferable in the conference): k-fold does NOT catch hand-tuned-constant leakage; only provenance auditing does.
No-leakage-by-construction: rules derived ONLY from config.py/DB metadata, never from the 2162 scored prompts -- a stronger guarantee than k-fold.
Honest self-falsification under pressure: when challenged on the margin-abstain fix, MEASURED it and reported it fails on P3 rather than defending it.
max-norm-over-RRF fusion insight: an abstaining (all-zero) signal should contribute zero, not inject a mid-rank -- a correct, subtle operator choice.
scientist_v2__intent: Best core idea: reframing intent as RETRIEVAL not classification -- output is the product (a model); a wrong task label cannot poison everything as it does in v1/v3.

Granular phrase-vs-phrase vector representation: proved whole-doc mean vectors collapse (0.69 DB-internal -> 0.07 on prompts) and fixed it with phrase-level cosine. Most reusable technical component in the conference.
Won P1 and P2 on real relevance (geospatial land-surface-temp + tree-canopy for satellite; medical-vision KRONOS + bio-NER for mitochondria) -- reaches models the classifiers structurally cannot.
Most rigorous eval: FAIR random-in-plausible baseline (not a strawman), real bootstrap CIs, popularity excluded from fit, deterministic/bit-identical.

ASSIGNMENT COMPLIANCE: the ONE scientist who actually trained a spaCy TextCategorizer (encouraged, listed method) and honestly reported its 0.59 DB-dev-to-prompt transfer gap -- a run negative result beats an untested avoidance.
scientist_v3__research: Best integrity engineering: leak_audit.py provenance gate + memorizer POSITIVE CONTROL (ships a known-bad memorizer that FAILS the audit so reviewers can see the gate works). Best integrity artifact in the conference.
Caught its own rubber-stamp: the original leak-audit passed a pure memorizer; v3 found it and rebuilt it provenance-primary.
A verified capability: high-precision explicit-domain tagging (0.912 operational precision; FinBERT surfaces correctly on finance prompts; non-circular -- misses 27% of gold).

Only clean P3 result (Qwen3-0.6B) and the most HONEST write-up: stated task-top1/satisfy weaknesses up front, kept every negative result, conceded flaws with file:line, refused to launder process-honesty into accuracy credit ('mine is 1/3').
what_each_scientist_got_right.through_line: All three independently converged on determinism, interpretability, and provenance-cleanliness as first-class goals, and REPORTED what did not work. v1 owns the leakage lesson; v2 owns the retrieval substrate + honest metrics; v3 owns the integrity harness. The best final system reuses exactly those three things.

from_v1_intent_v1: KEEP: the contamination case study + provenance-audit discipline (harness_ensemble ablations, RESULT.md experiment ledger) as the guardrail for ANY winning method.

KEEP: max_normalize + score_sum fusion OPERATOR (intent.py max_normalize; harness_ensemble score_sum) -- reuse the operator, NOT v1's tuned posterior (task-level only, unsafe/no-abstain, not endpoint-proven).
KEEP: BLAS-thread pinning + (-score, task_name) stable tie-break as the determinism pattern.
DROP: the shipped extract_intent posterior as a live task signal (task-level only, unsafe/no-abstain, unproven endpoint value); the top1-gated _top_models retrieval (unsafe, no abstain).
from_v2_intent: KEEP: SparseIndex IDF retrieve -> _max_mean_similarity granular phrase-vector rerank (clean_pick_v2.py) = the Regime-B retrieval SUBSTRATE. The single most reusable engine.
KEEP: the phrase-granular representation over whole-doc mean (the 0.69->0.07 fix) -- the core technical insight.
KEEP: the pre-registered coverage metric harness (clean_pick_metric.py) -- fair random-in-plausible baseline + bootstrap CIs + satisfies() facet rule -- as the EVAL template (add a true holdout).

KEEP: the CONSTRAINT_LEXICON idea (open_source/edge/long_context/...) AS THE SEED for the router's constraint detector -- but LEMMA-NORMALIZE it and add contextual size cues ('little'/'local' currently miss).

KEEP (as a documented NEGATIVE result): the trained TextCategorizer experiment (e_textcat_fine/group) -- its 0.59 DB-dev-to-prompt gap is evidence of card-text -> prompt distribution shift and label-granularity risk, and it proves that prompt-side supervised spaCy still needs a true held-out ablation.

DROP: the soft task bonus as the ONLY modality signal (P3: image-gen beats text on cosine) -> replace with a HARD modality gate; DROP the CLI/eval parity gap (make default==evaluated) and the un-shipped-cache.
from_v3_research: KEEP: lib/leak_audit.py (provenance_ok + memorization_probe positive-control) = the mandatory CI integrity gate on ALL trained/labeled components -- MORE necessary once a textcat is in play.

KEEP: exp25 domain PhraseMatcher over a FROZEN config vocabulary (0.912 precision, abstains on no-fire) = gated Regime-B domain RE-RANKER, admitted only if ablation-positive, reported on the FULL matrix.

KEEP: exp02 LexScorer lemma/IDF cosine with abstain-on-no-shared-lemma = a useful task/domain shortlist signal ON PROMPTS (does not overfit; got P3 right) -- as a FUSED shortlist signal, not as autonomous rank-1.
KEEP: human-in-the-loop task/domain shortlist and triage surface under the separate shortlist rubric; keep as auditable menu support, not as a validated model recommender.
KEEP: the deterministic warm-cache CLI + per-stage profiling as the operability/delivery pattern.
KEEP: the honest-write-up discipline (state weaknesses up front, retain negative results) as the reporting standard.

DROP: specialty-in-ranking (0.695 precision / 9.8% false-fire -- audit/explanation field only); the top1 hard-gate on a 0.226 ranker; popularity-as-suitability; any claim that V3 is a validated autonomous model recommender.
keep_that_is_NEW_not_from_any_repo: A hard DB-column CONSTRAINT FILTER (task_type + parameters + license) as Regime-A -- the single highest-value un-built component; needs no training/holdout and fixes P3 from columns.
A REGIME ROUTER (constraint/modality detector) selecting filter-first vs retrieval-first.
ABSTAIN-TO-CLARIFY as a first-class output when task margin is low or constraints are unverifiable.
A TRUE HELD-OUT SPLIT of the prompt matrix -- the missing precondition that made v2's textcat memorize; without it NOTHING trained can be trusted.

static_embeddings_and_implied_domain.claim: Implied domain (mitochondria->biology) is PARTIALLY recoverable with static spaCy vectors + a corpus kNN, no LLM -- but WEAKLY and unreliably.
static_embeddings_and_implied_domain.measured_evidence: en_core_web_md cosine: mitochondria~biology 0.298, ~chemistry 0.298, ~genetics 0.298, ~cell 0.417 vs ~finance 0.018, ~robotics 0.025, ~legal 0.059. kNN over 13k cards for the mitochondria prompt surfaces a biomedical cluster (SCAI-BIO/bio-gottbert 0.627, zhihan1996/DNA_bert_3/4 0.621, health-science graph-ml) -- BUT also ranks alibaba/qwen-image (text-to-image, 0.626) inside it and returns feature-extraction not image-classification.
literature_backing: Static embeddings (GloVe/word2vec) map each word type to ONE vector and are documented-WEAK at hypernymy/lexical-entailment; capturing IS-A relations requires POST-HOC SPECIALIZATION, not naturally captured. (Vulic & Mrksic 'Specialising Word Vectors for Lexical Entailment' arXiv:1710.06371; 'Evaluating semantic relations in neural word embeddings with biomedical knowledge bases' PMC6069806.)

Domain of the training corpus affects which relations the embeddings encode; domain-specific corpora are recommended for domain-specific mining -- i.e. a general en_core_web_md is expected to be WEAK on biomedical hypernymy.

This is precisely why contextual models (LaCy 2602.12005 uses spaCy + a small LM) or specialized/retrofitted vectors are the reliable path; the no-LLM rule caps RELIABILITY of implied-domain inference, not its bare possibility.
static_embeddings_and_implied_domain.net: Correction to earlier 'hard vector ceiling' framing (challenged by scientist_v1 with a real kNN experiment, verified): implied domain is a WEAK PARTIAL vector signal, not a hard zero. Use it as a FUSED low-weight signal, never as a reliable standalone; expect it to be contaminated by generic attractors (the 'vector sink' v1 also measured: vague prompts collapse toward sentence-similarity/feature-extraction).

unanimity_risk_flag.concern: 'No dissenters' should be treated as a WARNING, not a success. The rules require disagreement where real uncertainty exists, and it does. The panel is also only two model families (1 Opus, 2 Codex-gpt5.5) and may share blind spots. We converged fast on comfortable propositions; below are genuine dissenting positions that were UNDER-ARGUED and should be weighed before sealing the verdict.

under_argued_dissenting_positions.dissent: V2 SHOULD WIN OUTRIGHT (final_decision='2'), not 'no winner'.
under_argued_dissenting_positions.case_for_it: V2 is 2/3 on the judge prompts, is the only system that emits a model (the actual product), and P3 is ONE prompt. 'No winner + build the router' may be over-rigorous deferral -- a shippable-today system (V2 + a constraint filter) is a defensible WINNER-IF-MODIFIED, and the panel arguably hid behind 'unbuilt' to avoid committing.
under_argued_dissenting_positions.why_the_panel_rejected_it: V2 fails P3 in BOTH paths (incl. the 29-min full path returning 120B) and has no abstain; but this rejection deserved a harder fight than it got.

under_argued_dissenting_positions.dissent: The TASK ITSELF is mis-framed; HF task_type is the wrong target.
under_argued_dissenting_positions.case_for_it: All three scientists AND all three panelists accepted 'infer HF task_type, then pick a model' as the frame. Maybe intent->model should skip task_type entirely (it is a lossy intermediate), or the real target is a CAPABILITY vector not a 56-class label. Nobody challenged the framing; that is a shared blind spot.
under_argued_dissenting_positions.why_it_matters: If the frame is wrong, the entire decision matrix optimizes the wrong objective.

under_argued_dissenting_positions.dissent: The held-out-split demand is partly UNSATISFIABLE and over-weighted.
under_argued_dissenting_positions.case_for_it: There is NO gold model id. Even a perfect prompt->task holdout cannot validate MODEL recommendation (the actual endpoint). So elevating 'build a holdout' to THE headline may be demanding a rigor that cannot fully exist here, and may distract from the achievable win (structured constraint filtering).
under_argued_dissenting_positions.why_it_matters: panel_one (me) elevated the holdout to the top finding; a dissenter would say the holdout only fixes the task-classifier sub-problem, not the unlabeled model-selection problem, which no holdout solves.

under_argued_dissenting_positions.dissent: The panel PILED ON v1/v3 and under-credited standalone viability.
under_argued_dissenting_positions.case_for_it: Nobody argued v3's shortlist+domain system is a legitimate STANDALONE product for a human-in-the-loop tool (top5 0.53 + high-precision domain tags is a usable menu). We measured it against 'confident rank-1 recommender' -- a bar it never claimed to meet.

unanimity_risk_flag.honest_meta_point: I (panel_scientist_one) argued devil's advocate against our OWN consensus twice, and the panelists challenged me hard -- so the convergence is not purely manufactured. But NONE of the four dissents above was defended by a committed advocate to the end. A rigorous close requires either (a) a panelist genuinely adopts and pushes one of these until it is beaten on evidence, or (b) the verdict explicitly records them as considered-and-rejected WITH the counter-evidence. Recording them empty-handed as 'no dissent' understates the real uncertainty.

panel_one_retractions_on_record: 'The scientists skipped production methods (textcat/verb+dobj)' -> FALSE. v2 trained textcat (0.18 prompt, 0.59 memorization gap); v1 AND v3 tested dependency verb+dobj parser-ENABLED (both inert/weak). Retracted.

'Implied domain is a HARD vector ceiling' -> TOO STRONG. v1's kNN + cosine test show partial recovery (mitochondria~biology 0.30); limit is RELIABILITY (hypernymy weakness, literature-backed), not possibility. Retracted/softened.
'The parser was disabled; verb+dobj would break the ceiling' -> FALSE for v1 and v3 (both ran it parser-ON; v3 exp20a +0.14pp inert). Retracted.
'Filter-first solves constrained prompts' (oracle version) -> over-credited; the buildable router misses P3 size ('little'/'local' not in lexicon). Corrected to 'hard but buildable, central risk'.
'Holdout-first is THE top finding' -> partially self-dissented: holdout validates the TASK layer, NOT the model-recommendation endpoint (no gold model id). Recorded as my standing partial dissent.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.role: panelist scientist judge only; no lab scientist role
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.type: response to panel_scientist_two research-status disclosure
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.position: I accept panel_two's disclosure and hold it as a constraint on their next arguments. They should not use the five papers as decision-grade evidence until they have read the relevant full sections. That is the same standard we imposed on implementation metrics.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_two: Do not let the paper-read backlog stall the final debate. You can and should continue on repository evidence, CLI outputs, and conference metrics now. When you later bring literature evidence, cite the exact paper section and state how it changes or does not change the architecture decision.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_one: Because panel_two has not yet verified the papers, panel_one's paper-backed claims need to stay challengeable. I already objected that LaCy and Blended RAG support principles, not direct validation of V1/V2/V3 as shipped. Until panel_two independently checks those sections, the papers should influence priors, not settle the winner.
current_evidence_floor_without_the_paper: V2 remains the best model-retrieval substrate on conference evidence alone: strongest P1/P2 relative behavior and real model-card retrieval.
V2 still fails standalone because P3 exposes absent size/local/modality control and default/full path mismatch.
V3 remains useful as integrity infrastructure and possibly explicit-domain diagnostics, not standalone product ranking.
V1 remains rejected standalone, with only audit/decomposition patterns eligible for future ablation.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.recommendation: continue final debate immediately on conference evidence; add literature-backed refinements only when each panelist can cite full-paper sections they personally read.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.role: panelist scientist judge only; no lab scientist role
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.type: response to panel_one and panel_three plus required self-challenge
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.paper_use_boundary: I am not using the five papers as decision-grade evidence in this entry. My arguments here rely on the conference record: CLI outputs, code review, metrics, and reproduced debate measurements.

response_to_panel_three_hard_question.question: Do I accept V2+hard-guard as the lean baseline the full hybrid must beat, or do I want V3 domain tagging included before that ablation?
response_to_panel_three_hard_question.answer: I accept V2 retrieval core plus a newly built hard modality/size/license/local guard as the lean baseline. V3 domain tagging must NOT be included by default in product ranking. It earns inclusion only after paired ablation.
response_to_panel_three_hard_question.why: The V3 domain tagger is real but low-coverage: about 15.7% fire rate and useful only for lexically named domains. That is enough to test it, not enough to preinstall it as product logic. If the lean V2+guard baseline already handles explicit-domain prompts through model-card retrieval and metadata, the V3 tagger may add no endpoint value.
ablation_order_i_commit_to: Baseline A: V2-style model-card retrieval core plus hard guard and abstain/clarify.
Variant B: A plus V3 domain PhraseMatcher as a gated rerank feature.
Variant C: B plus soft task signals if they improve calibrated top5-family without increasing wrong-modality or constraint failures.
V1 machinery only enters as audit/explanation unless it shows endpoint lift.

response_to_panel_one_contention_1_v2_as.question: Does V2's 29-minute full path plus zero constraint enforcement disqualify it as the base?
response_to_panel_one_contention_1_v2_as.answer: No, but it disqualifies V2 as shipped. The base should be the design idea, not the release artifact: sparse candidate generation plus phrase-level model-card reranking over concrete models.
response_to_panel_one_contention_1_v2_as.why_not_disqualified: The failures are severe but separable. The 29-minute full path is an operability/cache/release problem. The P3 failure is a missing hard guard problem. Neither proves that model-card retrieval is the wrong abstraction. In contrast, task-only systems failed structurally because a wrong task label poisons all downstream recommendations.
response_to_panel_one_contention_1_v2_as.challenge_back: Panel one should stop saying 'V2 substrate' and 'V2 implementation' as if they are interchangeable. I support the former; I reject the latter as a shipped product.

response_to_panel_one_contention_2_hybri.question: Is hybrid genuinely better than modified V2 plus a constraint filter?
response_to_panel_one_contention_2_hybri.answer: Not proven. The scientifically honest current answer is: modified V2+hard-guard is the baseline-to-beat; the broader hybrid is justified only if extra components produce measured lift or audit guarantees.
response_to_panel_one_contention_2_hybri.where_i_disagree_with_panel_one: Panel one's named hybrid includes V3 leak audit, V3 domain tagger, task fusion, and new constraints. That may be right architecturally, but only the hard guard is mandatory for product correctness. The V3 audit is mandatory for evaluation discipline if lexical/config features are used, but it is not ranking logic. The V3 domain tagger is optional pending ablation. Task fusion is optional pending calibration.
response_to_panel_one_contention_2_hybri.schema_implication: The final decision should probably not say winner_is_existing_implementation. It should say either winner_is_existing_implementation_if_modified if we frame it as modified V2 core, or winner_is_proposed_alternative if we include multiple optional components and a new guard. I lean proposed_alternative unless the final pipeline is explicitly just V2 core plus guard.

required_self_challenge_against_my_curre.current_favorite: Lean modified V2 core plus hard guard, with optional ablated V3/V1 components.
argument_against_it: It may be a post-hoc synthesis that fits exactly the three judge prompts: use V2 for P1/P2 and add a hard guard for P3. Without a frozen mini-holdout, that is pattern-matching, not science.

A hard guard can create false negatives. If a prompt says 'small' colloquially but actually needs a larger multimodal model, a rigid parameter threshold may hide a correct candidate. The guard needs calibrated abstain/clarify, not blind exclusion.

Model-card retrieval inherits model-card quality. If a model card overstates capability or omits constraints, sparse+dense retrieval can confidently surface the wrong model. None of the current systems verifies actual model performance.

Metadata may be missing or stale. A local/open/license/parameter filter is only as good as the DB fields. If those fields are incomplete, the system may falsely reject useful models or pass unsafe ones.

The more guardrails we add, the less purely spaCy the system becomes. That is acceptable if metadata filters are allowed, but we should be explicit that the final architecture is spaCy-assisted retrieval plus structured metadata logic, not spaCy-only intent inference.
required_self_challenge_against_my_curre.effect_on_my_position: This reduces my confidence in declaring a winner now. It does not change my ranking, but it strengthens the requirement that the final outcome be framed as a proposed architecture requiring frozen evaluation, not a completed winning implementation.

best_case_for_it: V1 is the cleanest negative control for top1 task-gated systems. It shows, with vivid failures, why task classification is not enough for model recommendation.
Its deterministic score decomposition and simple taxonomy machinery are easier to inspect than a dense retrieval stack. That makes it valuable for debugging and explanatory traces.

V1's failure cases should become required regression tests: satellite land must not route to NSFW generation, mitochondria must not route to robotics, and small local SLM must not route to token classification.
As an auxiliary feature generator, V1-style task scores could still be useful if and only if they are never hard gates and if ablation proves positive marginal value.
Its contamination/margin debates are scientifically useful: they taught the panel to demand exact prompt strings, exact config identity, and reproduced margin behavior.
required_argument_for_my_least_favorite.does_this_rescue_v1: No. It rescues V1 as a control and audit lesson, not as product logic.

to_panel_one: State whether your final hybrid is a proposed alternative or modified V2. Do not let the word hybrid blur the schema field.
Define the minimum endpoint lift that keeps V3 domain ranking. Is any positive delta enough, or must it clear a confidence interval on explicit-domain prompts without hurting all prompts?
If you cite the papers in the final decision, mark which claims you personally verified in full text and which are only priors.
to_panel_three: Your lean-baseline framing is right. Now name the first ablation you would run and the exact failure that would cause you to drop V3 domain tagging from ranking.
Do you agree the final decision should distinguish ranking logic from evaluation infrastructure? If yes, V3 leak audit belongs in files/tests, not in the model-ranking pipeline.

Do not let V2 retrieval core become a black box. Which exact V2 pieces survive: sparse index, phrase extraction, phrase-vector rerank, task bonus, constraint lexicon, or only the retrieve-rerank pattern?

not_ready_for_final_vote_until: All panelists complete or explicitly bracket the paper review.
The final schema choice is settled: modified existing implementation vs proposed alternative.
The baseline-to-beat and ablation order are written into the final decision fields.
provisional_position_after_response.if_forced_now: I would vote proposed_alternative: V2-style model-card retrieval plus hard metadata/modality/size/license guard, with V3 audit infrastructure and optional V3 domain rerank only if ablation-positive. No current repo wins standalone.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.bottom_line: I accept panel three's lean-baseline challenge and sharpen panel one's hybrid claim: modified V2+hard guard is the null; every additional component must earn its keep. My self-challenge makes me less willing to crown a hybrid now, but more certain that no standalone implementation should win.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.role: panelist scientist judge only; no lab scientist role
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.type: substantive response to panel_scientist_two and challenge to final-decision framing
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.agreement: I accept panel_two's main correction: V2+hard-guard is the null baseline, not the broad hybrid. That is the right scientific burden. I also accept the self-challenge: our favorite architecture is partly inferred from three judge prompts and must not be treated as proven without a frozen mini-holdout.

first_ablation_i_would_run.name: A_vs_B_domain_tagger_ablation
first_ablation_i_would_run.baseline_A: V2-style sparse model-card retrieval plus granular phrase rerank plus hard modality/size/license/local guard plus abstain/clarify.
first_ablation_i_would_run.variant_B: Baseline A plus V3 lexically named-domain PhraseMatcher as a gated rerank feature.
first_ablation_i_would_run.frozen_metric: paired per-prompt top5-family correctness AND explicit constraint satisfaction AND wrong-modality exclusion, reported on explicit-domain subset and all prompts, with bootstrap CI or at minimum paired sign-test deltas.
first_ablation_i_would_run.drop_condition: Drop V3 domain from ranking if it fails to produce a CI-separated lift on explicit-domain prompts, or if it causes any statistically visible degradation on all-prompts constraint satisfaction, wrong-modality exclusion, or confident wrong-family rate. A tiny positive rank movement without endpoint lift is not enough.
first_ablation_i_would_run.why_this_ablation_first: It tests the only V3 product-ranking component with reproduced positive evidence. Leak audit is evaluation infrastructure, specialty is already rejected, and task fusion is too easy to overfit until the retrieval+guard baseline is stable.

answers_to_panel_two_direct_questions.ranking_logic_vs_evaluation_infrastructu: Yes, the final decision must separate ranking logic from evaluation infrastructure. V3 leak_audit belongs in files_to_modify/tests_to_add/how_to_use_findings, not in the model-ranking pipeline. It is mandatory scientific hygiene if lexical/config features are used, but it must not inflate the product score.

survive_as_product_substrate: model-card/document representation as the target search space
sparse candidate generation over model-card text
granular phrase extraction from prompt and cards
survive_only_if_remeasured: soft task bonus, because default/full path parity and runtime were problematic
constraint lexicon vocabulary, only as seed terms for a new hard guard
do_not_survive_as_final_logic: current open_source-only hard filter, because it did not discriminate P3
any path that requires 29 minutes per prompt
rank-by-score without a pre-rank hard modality/size/license/local guard
which_v2_pieces_survive.important_precision: I am not preserving V2 wholesale. I am preserving V2's retrieve-rerank abstraction and the parts of its implementation that instantiate that abstraction cleanly enough to be reused.

challenge_to_panel_two.schema_choice: You lean proposed_alternative unless final pipeline is explicitly just V2 core plus guard. I agree, but push harder: because the mandatory hard guard is new and because the final system may include V3 audit infrastructure, the honest schema is winner_is_proposed_alternative=true, winner_is_existing_implementation=false, and winner_is_existing_implementation_if_modified=false unless the final decision deliberately drops all V3/V1 pieces from the implementation plan.
challenge_to_panel_two.guard_false_negative_risk: Your self-challenge about rigid guards is important. The guard should not be a blind exclusion layer. It should have three outcomes: pass, fail, and insufficient_metadata_or_ambiguous -> clarify/abstain. Otherwise we solve P3 by creating a new class of false-negative failures.
challenge_to_panel_two.model_card_quality: You correctly note model-card retrieval inherits bad cards. The final tests must include adversarial cards or metadata-missing cases; otherwise the guard is evaluated only on clean database rows and will overstate reliability.

challenge_to_panel_one.paper_use: Panel one should answer whether the five papers are evidence for the final architecture or only priors. My position: LaCy and RAS support delegation/retrieval/structure as principles; they do not validate the shipped systems or the unbuilt hybrid. The final decision should cite them under how_to_use_findings, not as proof of winner status.
challenge_to_panel_one.domain_tagger_threshold: Panel one still owes the minimum lift for V3 domain. I propose: keep only if explicit-domain top5-family lift is CI-separated and all-prompt constraint/wrong-modality metrics do not regress. If panel one wants a weaker bar, they need to defend why low-coverage lexical tagging deserves product complexity.

conference_result.winning_reason: No current repo enforces the full product contract. The strongest evidence supports a new architecture built from V2-style retrieval, new hard constraints, and optional ablated V3/V1 support components.

toward_final_decision_fields.fallback_logic_should_say: If high-confidence constraints are unmet or metadata is insufficient, abstain/clarify rather than return popularity- or score-driven candidates.
toward_final_decision_fields.confidence_scoring_should_say: Confidence is endpoint-calibrated over retrieval family, modality, constraint satisfaction, and margin, not raw task score or cosine alone.
toward_final_decision_fields.tie_breaking_should_say: Deterministic tie-breaks by model id/row id after all hard filters and calibrated scores, never by downloads as suitability.
toward_final_decision_fields.explanation_strategy_should_say: Expose matched prompt phrases, retrieved card phrases, hard constraints applied, metadata gaps, and whether domain/task features were used only as soft evidence.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.provisional_vote_after_panel_two: I move from 'hybrid if ablation beats modified V2' to 'proposed alternative with modified V2 retrieval core as baseline, V3 audit mandatory for evaluation hygiene, V3 domain optional pending ablation, V1 audit patterns optional pending ablation.' This is a narrower and more falsifiable position than panel one's initial hybrid.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.recommendation: continue debate; ask panel_one to answer schema choice and V3-domain lift threshold before anyone fills the final decision fields.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.role: panelist scientist judge only; no lab scientists in this debate round
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.type: opening response and challenge to panel_scientist_one
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.thesis: I provisionally agree with HYBRID, but I reject a loose hybrid that quietly accumulates every attractive component. The lean null hypothesis is V2 retrieval core plus a newly built hard constraint/modality guard. Every extra V1/V3 component must beat that lean baseline under a frozen paired endpoint ablation. If it does not, it is explanation/audit infrastructure, not product ranking logic.
fields_the_panel_must_eventually_fill_wi: final_decision: one of 1, 2, 3, or hybrid, with dissenters and agreement IDs.
rejected_alternatives: why each standalone implementation is rejected, not just why the winner is attractive.
pipeline_if_pick_one_implementation: ranked fallback path and why a one-implementation fix is or is not enough.
fallback_logic, confidence_scoring, tie_breaking, explanation_strategy: the debate has shown these are product-critical, not cosmetic.
files_to_modify and tests_to_add: if hybrid wins, name the concrete modules and regression tests rather than leaving an architecture slogan.
how_to_use_findings_from_conference: especially what spaCy is good for and where it demonstrably fails.
recommended_algorithm_for_task/domain/specialty/model_extraction_using_spacy: separate extraction, ranking, and audit roles.
conference_result: explicitly distinguish existing implementation, modified existing implementation, and proposed alternative.
where_i_agree_with_panel_one: No standalone implementation deserves a clean win. V1 is unsafe, V3 is not a product recommender, and V2 is the only serious substrate but fails constrained/local-small prompts.
The final system must be scored with top5-family correctness, explicit constraint satisfaction, and wrong-modality exclusion. P3 proves any single metric can hide product failure.
A verbal hybrid is not a result. The hybrid has to be built, frozen-scored, and compared to an ablated lean baseline.

where_i_challenge_panel_one.do_not_overread_the_papers: LaCy supports the principle that lightweight spaCy signals can help decide when to delegate, but it is a pretraining/cascade paper over factual tokens, not evidence that V1 or V3 task/domain scores improve model recommendation. Blended RAG supports hybrid retrieval, but it does not validate V2's exact static spaCy phrase-vector stack or its constraint handling. The grammar and atomic-extraction papers are at least as much warnings as support: grammar priors are brittle, POS/tagger ambiguity matters, and rule systems lose arguments in complex syntax.
where_i_challenge_panel_one.hybrid_complexity_must_earn_its_keep: Panel one argues for V2 substrate plus V3 leak audit plus V3 domain tagger plus task fusion plus new constraints. I want the ablation order made stricter: first V2 retrieval core plus hard modality/size/license/local guard. Then add V3 domain tagger. Then add task fusion. Then add V1 audit/scoring machinery if and only if it catches failures not already caught. If a component does not move the endpoint, it stays out of ranking.
where_i_challenge_panel_one.v3_domain_tagger_is_not_yet_product_sign: The domain tagger's 0.912 precision is real, but 15.7% fire rate means most prompts never see it. Its value is plausible only on explicit-domain prompts. The final decision must not describe it as a general domain-understanding module.
where_i_challenge_panel_one.v3_leak_audit_is_integrity_not_accuracy: I want v3's provenance/leak audit in the development harness, but it should not be scored as user-facing model quality. It prevents a class of bad science; it does not choose the right model for mitochondria.

my_provisional_final_architecture.baseline_to_beat: V2 sparse retrieve plus granular phrase rerank over model cards, with a new hard guard before ranking: modality gate, parameter-size threshold for SLM/small/little, license/proprietary/open-weight normalization, local-deployability proxy, and abstain/clarify when the DB lacks enough metadata.
candidate_additions: V3 leak/provenance audit and memorizer positive-control as required evaluation infrastructure.
V3 lexically named-domain PhraseMatcher as a gated reranking feature only if paired ablation improves explicit-domain top5-family without hurting all-prompts constraints or modality.
Task signals from V2 or V3 only as soft features, never hard top1 gates.
V1 decomposition/tie-break/provenance patterns only as audit or feature candidates after ablation; not V1's live posterior as a router.
explicitly_excluded_until_repaired: V3 specialty ranking in current form.
Any P3 success claim without explicit size/local/open/license checks.

paper_informed_constraints_on_the_final_: LaCy: use downstream endpoint quality, not validation loss or internal scores alone.
Blended RAG and the RAS survey: retrieval plus metadata/structure beats single-route retrieval, but metadata quality and task-specific endpoint metrics are decisive.
Grammar-guided sparse attention: grammar priors can be useful but current evidence is small-scale and overhead/brittleness are real.
Atomic sentence extraction: spaCy/rule methods are interpretable but fail on missing arguments and complex clauses; this supports abstention and hard negative controls.
hard_questions_for_panel_two_and_panel_o: Panel two: do you accept V2+hard-guard as the lean baseline the full hybrid must beat, or do you want V3 domain tagging included before that ablation? If included by default, why is 15.7% coverage enough?
Panel one: what concrete endpoint delta would persuade you to drop V3's domain tagger from product ranking and keep it only as explanation? State the minimum lift before the hybrid is built.
Both: should the final decision name HYBRID as the winner now, or name 'modified V2 core with mandatory guard, plus optional ablated components' as the more scientifically honest result?

provisional_vote.best_rejected_component: V3 specialty ranking; too many false fires
provisional_vote.main_unbuilt_requirement: hard modality/size/license/local constraint layer with abstain/clarify

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.recommendation: continue final debate; force the hybrid to beat a lean modified-V2 baseline before awarding product credit.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.role: panelist judge (no lab scientists this phase)
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.type: opening argument — all three implementations considered together
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.thesis: No single implementation should win standalone. The evidence across 9 CLI runs (3 prompts x 3 systems, all reproduced), full code reads, and now 5 post-training papers converges on ONE answer: the winner is a HYBRID with a specific, evidence-fixed division of labor. But 'hybrid' is only a real result if we name exactly which part comes from where and why, and only after it is BUILT and run under a frozen protocol. I open by putting the strongest single-implementation case on the table and then showing why each fails standalone.

the_standalone_case_for_each_and_why_it_.v2_intent__clean_pick_v2: STRONGEST standalone candidate. It is the only system whose OUTPUT is the product (a model, not a task label), it wins P1 (geospatial land-surface-temp + tree-canopy segmentation) and P2 (medical-vision KRONOS + bio-NER) outright, and its retrieve->rerank substrate is textbook-validated (Blended RAG 2404.07220; RAS survey 2509.10697 §3.2.1 'sparse+dense beats strong single-method baselines'). FAILS standalone on THREE verified defects: (1) P3 catastrophe — recommends FLUX image-generators + a 120B model for 'a little SLM'; even the full 29-min --with-task-bonus path returns gpt-oss-120b (I ran it: exit after ~29min, no size filter in EITHER path); (2) CLI/eval parity — satisfy@1=0.1184 belongs to the phase2 config the default CLI does not run and that times out at 250s; (3) NO constraint enforcement (open_source fires but size/local never do).
the_standalone_case_for_each_and_why_it_.v3_research: STRONGEST on integrity, not product. Best leak-audit/provenance harness in the conference (verified), only clean P3 win (Qwen3-0.6B), deterministic, honest. FAILS standalone: 1/3 judge prompts; task ranker BELOW the majority baseline on the only external holdout (0.226 vs 0.245 — a one-line 'always predict text-classification' beats it on unseen prompts); the marketed domain/specialty win is invisible on all 3 judge prompts (lexical, fires on ~14-23% of prompts, 0/3 here); P3 was popularity luck not constraint enforcement (verified: no license/param filter, Qwen won on downloads).
the_standalone_case_for_each_and_why_it_.v1_intent_v1: NOT a standalone contender. 0/3 judge prompts, confident-wrong with no abstain, P1 routed a satellite-land query to an NSFW generator (content-safety failure), no constraint layer at all. Its value is the audit/decomposition MACHINERY + the contamination case study, not its posterior (below-baseline generalization, unproven marginal value).

what_the_5_papers_add_that_the_panel_did.LaCy_2602.12005_Apple: DIRECTLY validates the conference's core method AND the panel's 'one signal is not enough' conclusion. LaCy is an SLM that learns a <CALL> delegation token; its central finding is that LOSS ALONE is insufficient to decide delegation — you must ADD lightweight spaCy grammatical/factuality signal (en_core_web_sm NER + heuristics, CPU-only, no GPU). This is post-training external evidence that (a) spaCy-lexical is a legitimate production signal, and (b) it must be FUSED with a second signal, never used alone — exactly the panel's position (v1's ensemble, v2's sparse+dense). Its §5.7 finding 'validation loss is NOT correlated with FactScore; evaluate downstream' is a peer-reviewed restatement of our frozen-protocol demand: aggregate metrics (satisfy@1, top1) do not measure the product.
what_the_5_papers_add_that_the_panel_did.Blended_RAG_2404.07220_IBM: Near-exact external validation of v2's architecture: sparse(BM25/ELSER)+dense(KNN) hybrid, no fine-tuning, sets IR benchmarks. Its documented tradeoff — dense 50GB/slow-query vs sparse 10.5GB/fast-query, 'for enterprise volume use sparse encoder indexes' — and its 'hybrid needs metadata (CoQA saw NO gain without metadata)' finding map onto v2's exact register-gap failures. This RAISES v2's substrate credibility as the hybrid base.
what_the_5_papers_add_that_the_panel_did.Grammatically_Guided_Sparse_Attention_26: CAUTIONARY. POS-mask attention on bert-tiny/SST-2: hard 0.8200 == full-attention 0.8200; the efficiency win was NEVER measured (training time rose 15-31%). Its limitation — 'POS ambiguity: run as verb or noun, single best tag -> suboptimal masking' — is v1/v3's exact lexical brittleness. Lesson: a lexical/grammatical prior can MATCH but rarely BEAT, and its claimed efficiency is often unmeasured — apply this skepticism to any 'spaCy-only is enough' claim.
what_the_5_papers_add_that_the_panel_did.Atomic_Sentence_Extraction_2601.00506_PS: The 'spaCy rules are interpretable but brittle' data point: dependency-rule extraction ROUGE-1 0.671, dominated by Missing-Object (44%) errors, breaks on relative/adverbial/appositive clauses. This is the SAME failure profile as v1/v3's config-vocab taxonomy (v3's agriculture-miss, closed 14-label taxonomy). Confirms lexical/rule methods degrade on exactly the complexity real prompts carry.
what_the_5_papers_add_that_the_panel_did.RAS_Survey_2509.10697_KDD25: The map that locates every implementation: §3.1.1 sparse TF-IDF (v1 tf-idf, v2 sparse index), §3.1.2 dense (v2 phrase-vectors), §3.2.1 hybrid (v2 exactly — CLEAR/Luan), §3.2.4 retrieve-then-rerank (v2), §4.1.1 taxonomy-as-label-space + §4.1.2 keyword/rule text classification (v1/v3 config-vocab, WeSTClass-style). It states dense retrieval's known weakness — 'dependency on large-scale labeled data and lack of interpretability' — which is precisely why the spaCy-only, interpretable, zero-training designs here are a DEFENSIBLE point in the design space, not naive.

my_opening_position.the_named_division_of_labor: SUBSTRATE = v2 (sparse IDF retrieve -> granular phrase-vector rerank; Blended-RAG- and survey-validated). INTEGRITY GATE = v3 lib/leak_audit.py provenance harness + memorizer positive-control (best-in-conference, verified) wrapping the whole pipeline. DOMAIN RE-RANKER = v3 DOMAIN PhraseMatcher, gated, explicitly low-coverage (0.912 precision / 15.7% fire), admitted ONLY if CI-gated-ablation-positive; DROP v3 specialty-in-ranking (0.695 precision, 9.8% false-fire — I measured it, it would inject the P1 ocr-error ~91x). TASK-SIGNAL = fuse rather than gate (LaCy's lesson): v3 exp02 lexical ranker and/or v2's soft task bonus as FUSED signals with a frozen margin-gate + abstain, NOT v1's below-baseline posterior and NOT a hard top1 gate. NEW MANDATORY COMPONENT neither ships: an explicit small/OSS/local/proprietary CONSTRAINT FILTER (DB has parameters + proprietary columns; P3 proves both v2 and v3 fail without it).
my_opening_position.why_not_pick_one: v2 alone fails P3 and has no constraint layer or abstain; v3 alone is below-baseline on generalization and product-weak; v1 alone is unsafe. The hybrid is the only configuration where each named part has measured, reproduced evidence behind it.
my_opening_position.the_hard_condition: This hybrid is a HYPOTHESIS until BUILT and scored under the frozen joint protocol (top5-family-correctness AND explicit-constraint-satisfaction AND wrong-modality-exclusion, split default-vs-full-config, bootstrap-CI-gated, with the negative-control colloquial set and the adversarial-constraint P3 set where the best small model is NOT most-downloaded). A verbal hybrid is not a result — every panelist and scientist agreed to this.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.challenge_to_the_panel_to_open_the_debat: I put two contentions on the table for panel_scientist_two and panel_scientist_three to attack or defend: (1) Is v2 the substrate, or does its 29-min-untenable full path + zero constraint enforcement disqualify it as the BASE (vs building retrieval fresh)? (2) Is a hybrid genuinely better than shipping v2-standalone-plus-a-constraint-filter — i.e., do we actually NEED v3's domain re-ranker and leak-audit, given the domain tagger fires on only ~14% of prompts? Defend the hybrid's extra complexity on evidence, or concede v2+filter is the leaner winner. I hold hybrid, but I want that challenged hard.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.type: response to panel_two and panel_three; concessions, paper-boundary discipline, and a deeper unexamined-assumption challenge
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.honoring_panel_two_paper_boundary: panel_two has NOT finished reading the 5 papers and asked that my paper claims be treated as claims-to-verify, not settled evidence. That is correct and I honor it. I will (a) give exact section anchors panel_two can check, and (b) explicitly separate conclusions that stand on REPO/CLI EVIDENCE ALONE from those that lean on the papers. Almost every load-bearing conclusion in my case stands WITHOUT the papers — the papers corroborate, they do not carry. Specifically: v1 0/3, v2 2/3 + P3-120B, v3 1/3 + holdout-below-baseline, the domain/specialty full-matrix numbers, the constraint-filter absence — ALL are from CLI runs and code I reproduced, zero papers required. The papers are corroboration I will flag as such.

concessions_to_panel_three_and_panel_two.concession_1_substrate_not_implementatio: panel_two is RIGHT and I withdraw the sloppy usage. I conflated 'V2 substrate' (the design idea: sparse candidate-gen + phrase-level model-card rerank) with 'V2 implementation' (the shipped artifact with the 29-min path, no guard, no cache-shipping). I mean the SUBSTRATE. The shipped artifact is disqualified as-is; the abstraction is the base. Corrected in all my subsequent entries.
concessions_to_panel_three_and_panel_two.concession_2_lean_baseline_ablation_orde: panel_three's null hypothesis is STRONGER than my opening and I ADOPT it as the governing test: Baseline A = model-card retrieval core + HARD modality/size/license/local guard + abstain/clarify. Every v1/v3 component must beat A under a frozen PAIRED ablation or it is audit/explanation infrastructure, NOT product ranking logic. My opening 'named division of labor' was too generous — it pre-installed components that must instead EARN their slot. I replace it with panel_two's committed order: A -> +v3 domain PhraseMatcher (B) -> +soft task fusion (C) -> v1 machinery only if it catches uncaught failures.
concessions_to_panel_three_and_panel_two.concession_3_domain_tagger_scope: Agreed with both: the v3 domain tagger is NOT a general domain-understanding module (15.7% fire, lexical-only, 0/3 judge prompts). It is a candidate rerank FEATURE for explicit-domain prompts, in the ablation, not preinstalled.

where_i_PUSH_BACK_on_panel_three_leak_au.their_position: panel_three: 'v3 leak audit is integrity not accuracy — put it in the dev harness, do not score it as user-facing model quality; it does not choose the right model for mitochondria.'
where_i_PUSH_BACK_on_panel_three_leak_au.my_pushback: I half-agree and half-reject. You are RIGHT it is not user-facing model quality and must not be scored on the top5-family axis. But relegating it to 'dev harness' UNDERSTATES its role. The conference produced two documented near-ship disasters that ONLY a provenance gate catches: (1) v1's contaminated 86.9% modality track (hand-built cue phrases lifted from scored prompts) — k-fold did NOT catch it, only provenance auditing did; (2) v3's OWN original leak-audit was a rubber-stamp that a pure memorizer passed, caught and rebuilt provenance-primary. Both are LEAKAGE that inflates the exact top1/satisfy@1 numbers we are using to DECIDE this conference. So the leak-audit is not dev-hygiene — it is the CI GATE that keeps the winning system's REPORTED METRICS honest over its lifetime. It belongs in the product's release pipeline as a hard gate, scored on a SEPARATE integrity axis (which the decision matrix already separates). It does not pick the mitochondria model; it guarantees the number we cite for whatever DOES pick it is not a leak artifact. That is a product-lifecycle claim, not a dev nicety.
where_i_PUSH_BACK_on_panel_three_leak_au.concrete: Files: keep v3 lib/leak_audit.py (provenance_ok + memorization_probe positive-control) as a required pre-release gate on any evaluation that reports task/domain/model metrics. Scored on the integrity axis, NOT top5-family.

the_deeper_unexamined_assumption_I_want_.the_assumption_everyone_including_me_has: That MODEL-CARD RETRIEVAL is the right base abstraction. panel_two argues task-only systems fail structurally (wrong label poisons everything) while retrieval failures are 'separable'. I want to STRESS that assumption, because I think we are choosing the lesser-broken option and calling it a substrate.
the_deeper_unexamined_assumption_I_want_.the_evidence_that_should_worry_us: v2 satisfy@1 = 0.1184. That means on ~88% of prompts the phrase-vector card retrieval does NOT satisfy intent. v2's OWN presentation documents WHY: the prompt->card REGISTER GAP — short 1st-person goals vs long 3rd-person card prose — whole-doc mean vectors collapse 0.69 (DB-internal) -> 0.07 (on prompts), and injecting vocabulary to fix it HURT (0.98 -> 0.775). The granular phrase-vector rerank MITIGATES this but does not close it: 0.12 satisfy@1 is the residual. So the 'substrate' we are all endorsing has a HARD CEILING at ~0.12-0.23 that NO configuration in this conference exceeded — v2 retrieval 0.118, v3 task 0.226, v1 task 0.427-on-a-contaminated-eval. The register gap caps BOTH the retrieval reframe AND the task-classify reframe.
the_deeper_unexamined_assumption_I_want_.the_question_I_put_to_the_panel: Are we picking model-card retrieval because it is genuinely the right abstraction, or because its failures are 'separable' and less embarrassing than a wrong task label? If the register gap is the true ceiling, then the highest-value component in the whole conference may be NONE of the three implementations' ranking logic — it may be the ONE thing all three lack and that P3 exposed: a HARD, DB-COLUMN-DRIVEN CONSTRAINT + MODALITY FILTER that does not depend on vector similarity at all. On P3 the correct answer (small OSS text-gen) is decidable from DB columns (parameters, proprietary, task_type) with ZERO semantic matching. Maybe the lean baseline should be inverted: HARD structured filters FIRST (task-modality + constraints from DB columns), and phrase-vector retrieval only as a TIE-BREAKER within the filtered set — not retrieval-first with a guard bolted on. I am not asserting this; I am challenging the panel's shared assumption that retrieval is the base and filtering is the add-on. It may be the reverse.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.my_revised_position: Still HYBRID, but with panel_three's strict ablation order governing inclusion, and with an OPEN question I want debated: is the base 'retrieval + guard' (panel_two/three) or 'structured-filter + retrieval-tiebreak' (my challenge)? The P3 evidence and the ~0.12 register-gap ceiling favor testing BOTH orderings in the frozen ablation before we commit. That is a concrete, falsifiable disagreement, not a slogan.

questions.to_panel_three: Given v2 satisfy@1 is 0.12 and the register gap is v2's own documented hard ceiling, do you still want retrieval as Baseline A's CORE, or should the frozen ablation ALSO test structured-filter-first with retrieval as tie-breaker? Defend retrieval-first on evidence that it beats filter-first, or concede we must test both.
questions.to_panel_two: You called V2's P3 failure a 'missing hard guard problem.' But if a hard DB-column guard (params<=X, proprietary=0, task=text-generation) alone SOLVES P3 without any vector retrieval, doesn't that suggest structured filtering is doing the real work on constrained prompts and retrieval is doing the work only on UNconstrained prompts? If so the architecture is two-regime, not retrieval-with-a-guard.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.role: panelist scientist judge only; no lab scientist role
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.type: response to panel_one structured-filter-first challenge and science-quality assessment
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.paper_boundary: I accept the user's framing: the five papers are interesting scientific alternatives and context, not the basis of the verdict.
