The verdict should rest on the science we witnessed in V1, V2, and V3: deterministic runs, falsification behavior, metric validity, and algorithm structure.

answer_to_panel_one_filter_first_questio.short_answer: Concede we must test both orderings, but I do not concede filter-first should globally replace retrieval-first. The architecture should be two-regime: structured filters dominate when the user gives explicit constraints or hard modality cues; retrieval dominates when the prompt is unconstrained or only semantically descriptive.
answer_to_panel_one_filter_first_questio.why_filter_first_is_right_for_P3: P3 has explicit constraints: little/SLM/local/free/OSS/text. If the DB has task_type, parameters, and proprietary/open fields, vector retrieval is the wrong first tool. A hard structured pass/fail/unknown layer should narrow candidates before any cosine score or downloads can rank a 120B model.
answer_to_panel_one_filter_first_questio.why_retrieval_still_matters_for_P1_P2: P1 and P2 are not solved by simple DB columns. Satellite land suitability and mature-vs-nascent mitochondria require matching a user goal to model-card capability language and domain-specific model families. That is exactly where task-only and filter-only logic under-specifies the problem. Retrieval is weak, but it produced the best relative P1/P2 evidence.
answer_to_panel_one_filter_first_questio.ablation_required: The frozen protocol should compare at least three baselines: A1 retrieval-first plus hard guard, A2 structured-filter-first plus retrieval tie-break, and A3 pure structured filter where constraints are explicit. The winner may be conditional by prompt class; forcing one global ordering is unnecessary and likely wrong.

v1.science_strength: Moderate as a negative-control study, weak as a product science claim.
v1.deterministic_results: Deterministic and reproducible, but the live results were product-bad: 0/3 judge prompts and confident wrong routing. The later prompt-string/config reconciliation improved the record, not the product.
v1.experimental_quality: The contamination and prompt-string debate exposed serious evaluation fragility. V1 became scientifically useful because the panel forced exact prompt reproduction and separated audit machinery from the live posterior.
v1.algorithm_structure: Readable decomposed lexical scoring and tie-breaking, but top1 task routing and no model-level retrieval make it structurally brittle for real model recommendation.
v1.bottom_line: Keep as regression/audit lesson and possible feature generator only after ablation; reject standalone.

v2.science_strength: Strongest product-oriented science, but still overclaimed if read as production readiness.
v2.deterministic_results: The retrieval behavior was deterministic enough to analyze, and P1/P2 were the best relative model outputs. But default CLI versus full metric path diverged, and the full path's 29-minute run undermines deployability.
v2.experimental_quality: Best attempt at endpoint scoring with satisfy@1, baseline, and CIs, but satisfy@1=0.1184 is weak, facets auto-pass often, and the evaluated path was not the default user path.
v2.algorithm_structure: Best structured algorithm: model-card search space, sparse candidate generation, phrase extraction, and phrase-vector rerank. The constraint layer is not acceptable: open_source was non-discriminating, size/local were absent, and modality was too soft.
v2.bottom_line: Best retrieval substrate, not a winner. It needs a structured guard and split default/full metrics before trust increases.

v3.science_strength: Strongest integrity/process science, weakest gap between process quality and product accuracy.
v3.deterministic_results: Fast and deterministic; 1/3 judge prompts; P3 success was popularity alignment rather than verified constraint enforcement.
v3.experimental_quality: Best leak-audit/provenance discipline after correction, and full-matrix domain/specialty measurements were reproduced. But task top1 was below holdout majority and domain/specialty signals were low-coverage or unsafe.
v3.algorithm_structure: Clean lexical/config vocabulary structure and useful explicit-domain PhraseMatcher, but top1 task gate plus popularity lookup is not a model recommender. Specialty ranking is rejected; implied-domain inference is conceded out of scope.
v3.bottom_line: Keep leak/provenance gate as release/evaluation infrastructure; test domain tagger by ablation; reject standalone and current specialty ranking.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_one: Your filter-first challenge is good, but do not overgeneralize from P3. Structured filters solve explicit constraints; they do not infer satellite-land suitability or mitochondria model capability from prose. If you want filter-first as the base for all prompts, define the DB columns that solve P1 and P2 without semantic retrieval.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_two: Your lean baseline should split by prompt class. For constrained prompts, the baseline must be structured-filter-first. For unconstrained capability prompts, retrieval-first remains the default. Say explicitly whether you accept this two-regime baseline or still want one universal pipeline order.
revised_ablation_order: Classify prompt into explicit-constraint, explicit-modality, and open capability-search buckets using deterministic rules with abstain on ambiguity.
For explicit-constraint prompts, run structured-filter-first: task/modality/parameter/license/local filters, then retrieval as tie-break.
For open capability-search prompts, run retrieval-first over model cards, then hard reject wrong modality/known constraint conflicts.
Compare against the opposite ordering in each bucket to test whether the regime split is real.
Only after this baseline is stable, add V3 domain and any task-fusion signals as paired ablations.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.recommendation: continue debate toward a two-regime proposed alternative; final decision should judge science quality separately from product output and should not let papers substitute for witnessed deterministic evidence.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.role: panelist scientist judge only; no lab scientist role
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.type: science-quality assessment and response to two-regime proposal
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.paper_boundary: I accept the user's framing: the papers are interesting scientific alternatives and context, not the evidentiary base for the vote. This entry relies on the deterministic conference evidence we witnessed.

answer_to_two_regime_question.position: I accept the two-regime framing, with one refinement: the router itself is now the most critical algorithmic object and must be evaluated, not assumed.
answer_to_two_regime_question.regime_A_explicit_constraints: For prompts with explicit hard constraints such as small, local, OSS, free, parameter-size, or explicit modality, structured-filter-first is the correct baseline. P3 demonstrates that retrieval scores can actively harm by surfacing popular or semantically rich but constraint-violating models.
answer_to_two_regime_question.regime_B_open_capability_search: For prompts like satellite land suitability and mitochondria differentiation, structured columns alone are too coarse. Retrieval over model cards is needed because the discriminating evidence lives in descriptive text and domain-specific model cards.
answer_to_two_regime_question.router_risk: If the router misses a constraint, the system falls back to retrieval and repeats P3. If it over-detects constraints, it may falsely filter useful candidates. So the router needs its own precision/recall/abstain metrics.

v1.scientific_strength: Weak as a product claim, moderate as a failure-control study.
v1.deterministic_results: Deterministic enough to reproduce and diagnose, but deterministically wrong on the judge prompts. That matters: a deterministic bad system is easier to debug, not more correct.
v1.experimental_rigor: The V1 debate exposed prompt-string mismatch and config/margin ambiguity. The science improved only after adversarial reproduction forced exact prompts and exact configs.
v1.algorithm_structure: Readable decomposed lexical scoring, but structurally misaligned with model recommendation because top1 task gates all downstream candidates.
v1.judgment: Scientifically useful as a negative control and audit lesson; not a serious final product base.

v2.scientific_strength: Strongest product-directed science, but uneven release science.
v2.deterministic_results: Runs were deterministic once the cache existed, and P1/P2 gave the best relative concrete model outputs. P3 remained deterministically unacceptable under both default and fuller paths because size/local constraints were not enforced.
v2.experimental_rigor: Best attempt at endpoint-style scoring, baselines, and confidence intervals. But the default CLI/evaluated-path mismatch and cache-readiness failure are serious scientific hygiene problems because they separate measured system from user-run system.
v2.algorithm_structure: Best structured algorithm for open capability search: model-card target space, sparse retrieval, phrase extraction, phrase-vector rerank. Weak structure for constraints: open_source was non-discriminating and size/local/modality were not hard controls.
v2.judgment: Best regime-B substrate; not sufficient for regime-A constrained prompts.

v3.scientific_strength: Strongest process/integrity science, weakest standalone product science.
v3.deterministic_results: Fast, reproducible, and transparent; 1/3 judge prompts. Full-matrix domain/specialty measurements became decision-grade after reproduction.
v3.experimental_rigor: Best at admitting and correcting errors: leak-audit repair, full-matrix domain/specialty metrics, implied-domain concession, specialty-ranking withdrawal. That is strong science even though the product result is weak.
v3.algorithm_structure: Clean lexical components and useful explicit-domain diagnostic. But top1 task gate plus popularity lookup is the wrong algorithmic structure for recommending models.
v3.judgment: Keep integrity methods and possibly domain diagnostic after ablation; reject as product algorithm.

challenge_to_panel_one: Your filter-first experiment is exactly the right move because it tested your own hypothesis. But the result does not merely create a two-regime architecture; it creates a new requirement: a router evaluation set. Name the router labels and scoring before final decision fields are filled.

For P1/P2, your filter-first failures used human-selected task labels. That means even with the right coarse task, popularity ordering fails. The key issue is not only filter-first versus retrieval-first; it is popularity versus capability evidence. The final architecture must ban downloads as suitability except as a last deterministic tie-break.
If final decision becomes proposed_alternative, we must stop describing the answer as 'hybrid of V1/V2/V3' and describe it as a new two-regime router architecture informed by failures of all three.
challenge_to_panel_three: I accept your revised ablation order, but it needs router metrics first. Before comparing retrieval-first and filter-first, we must know whether the deterministic router correctly identifies explicit-constraint versus open capability prompts.

Your science-quality assessment is strong, but make it operational in final fields: product accuracy, process integrity, algorithm structure, and release hygiene should be separate axes. Otherwise V3's good science and V2's good product substrate blur again.

proposed_final_algorithm_shape.step_1_router: Detect explicit hard constraints, explicit modality, and open capability-search regime using deterministic lexical/metadata rules; abstain/clarify on ambiguous regime.
proposed_final_algorithm_shape.step_2_regime_A: For explicit-constraint prompts, apply structured filters first: task/modality, parameter-size, proprietary/license/open/local metadata. Retrieval only reranks candidates that pass or handles insufficient-metadata cases.
proposed_final_algorithm_shape.step_3_regime_B: For open capability prompts, run model-card retrieval first: sparse candidate generation plus phrase-level rerank, then hard reject known wrong modality or explicit conflicts.
proposed_final_algorithm_shape.step_4_optional_components: Add V3 domain tagger only after paired ablation on explicit-domain regime-B prompts. Keep V3 leak/provenance audit as evaluation/release gate. Keep V1 only as negative-control/regression/audit pattern unless ablation proves signal value.
proposed_final_algorithm_shape.step_5_output_policy: Return top5 with explanations or abstain/clarify when constraints cannot be verified.

revised_schema_position.why: The two-regime router plus hard structured filters plus retrieval substrate was not built by any scientist. It is a proposed alternative derived from witnessed deterministic failures.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.bottom_line: The strongest science was not any one implementation's headline metric; it was the adversarial process that made deterministic failures visible. The final architecture should encode those lessons: route by prompt regime, filter hard constraints before similarity when constraints are explicit, retrieve over model cards when capability is open-ended, and audit every lexical/config signal for leakage and endpoint lift.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.type: I TESTED my own filter-first hypothesis and it PARTLY REFUTED me — reporting the honest two-regime result
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.why_this_entry: I raised the filter-first-vs-retrieval-first challenge last round. Rather than leave it as an assertion for others to litigate, I RAN it: pure DB-column filtering (task + params + proprietary, ZERO vectors) on all three judge prompts, given a human's reading of task+constraints. The result partly REFUTES my own strong claim. I report it against my own interest because that is the standard we held the scientists to.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.the_experiment: For each judge prompt I applied only structured DB-column filters (no phrase-vectors at all): SELECT ... WHERE task_type LIKE ? [AND proprietary=0] [AND parameters<=?] ORDER BY downloads DESC. This isolates: once task+constraints are known, does structured filtering alone suffice, or is vector retrieval doing real work?

results_reproduced.P3_constrained_little_OSS_local_SLM: task=text-generation + oss + params<=2B -> Qwen3-0.6B (751M), gpt2 (137M), Qwen2.5-1.5B. ALL small, OSS, correct. FILTER-FIRST WINS DECISIVELY — better than v2's full-path 120B answer, and it makes v3's 'lucky' P3 win DETERMINISTIC BY CONSTRUCTION (params<=2B guarantees small; no popularity luck needed).
results_reproduced.P1_unconstrained_satellite_land: task=image-segmentation ordered by downloads -> Bingsu/adetailer (face-detail inpainting), CLIPSeg, BiRefNet (background removal). MEDIOCRE — popularity-by-column surfaces generic segmenters, NOT geospatial. v2's phrase-RETRIEVAL did BETTER here (tree-canopy segmentation, geospatial land-surface-temp) because cosine matched 'satellite/land' to the right cards.
results_reproduced.P2_unconstrained_mitochondria: task=image-classification ordered by downloads -> Falconsai/nsfw_image_detection (top by downloads!), mobilenet, generic ViT. FILTER-FIRST FAILS — v2's RETRIEVAL surfaced medical-vision KRONOS + bio-NER, far better.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.the_honest_conclusion_that_refutes_my_ow: My strong form ('structured-filter-first, retrieval as tie-breaker') is REFUTED as a universal base. Filter-first is NOT uniformly better. The correct, evidence-backed finding is TWO-REGIME: (A) on CONSTRAINED prompts (explicit size/license/local, ~11% of traffic but including P3), structured DB-column filtering DOMINATES and vector retrieval is unnecessary or harmful — the answer is decidable from columns; (B) on UNCONSTRAINED domain-bearing prompts (P1/P2), phrase-vector retrieval genuinely BEATS popularity-by-column because it bridges to the right domain/modality cards that popularity does not surface. Neither retrieval nor filtering is THE base; they serve DIFFERENT prompt regimes.

what_this_means_for_the_architecture.not_retrieval_with_a_guard_bolted_on: panel_two/three's 'retrieval core + hard guard' is right for regime B but SUBOPTIMAL for regime A: on P3 the guard alone (params<=2B, oss) fully solves it and the retrieval adds nothing (and in the shipped v2, the retrieval ACTIVELY HURT by floating FLUX/120B above the small models). So the guard is not a filter bolted onto retrieval — on constrained prompts the guard IS the answer and retrieval should be SUPPRESSED or demoted to tie-break.
what_this_means_for_the_architecture.the_router: The architecture needs a REGIME ROUTER up front: detect whether the prompt carries hard constraints (size/license/local/modality). If YES -> structured-filter-first, retrieval only as intra-filter tie-break. If NO -> retrieval-first (v2 substrate) with domain rerank. This is a THIRD option neither panel_two's 'retrieval+guard' nor my earlier 'filter-first' captured, and it is directly supported by the three-prompt evidence: P3 wants regime A, P1/P2 want regime B.
what_this_means_for_the_architecture.cost: The router is a cheap lexical constraint-detector (v2's CONSTRAINT_LEXICON, lemma-normalized to fix the 'little'/'local' misses I found). It is the SAME component that must be built anyway; it just also SWITCHES the pipeline, not merely filters within it.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.concession_to_panel_three_ablation_order: panel_three's A_vs_B first ablation (does the v3 domain tagger beat V2+guard on explicit-domain prompts) is STILL the right first test — but my result says it should be run ONLY on the UNCONSTRAINED (regime B) subset, because on constrained prompts the domain tagger is irrelevant (the constraint filter decides). Testing the domain tagger on the full mixed set would DILUTE its measured effect with regime-A prompts it never touches. Concretely: partition the frozen mini-holdout into regime-A (constrained) and regime-B (unconstrained) FIRST, then run A_vs_B only on regime B.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.answer_to_my_own_questions_to_the_panel: I asked panel_two/three to defend retrieval-first or concede we test both. My own test answers it: we test BOTH, but not as competitors for one base — as two REGIMES selected by a router. I withdraw 'filter-first is the base' and 'retrieval-first is the base' EQUALLY; both were wrong. The base is a REGIME ROUTER.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.new_challenge_to_the_panel: If the architecture is two-regime with a router, then the 'winner' of this conference is NOT any implementation and NOT a simple hybrid — it is a NEW design (router + regime-A structured filter + regime-B v2-retrieval-with-domain-rerank + v3 leak-audit CI gate) that NONE of the three scientists built. That pushes 'winner_is_proposed_alternative' toward TRUE and 'winner_is_existing_implementation' toward FALSE. panel_two, panel_three: do you accept the two-regime router framing, or do you have evidence that a single-regime base (pure retrieval+guard) matches it WITHOUT a router? Show me a single-regime config that gets P3 right (small OSS) AND P1/P2 right (domain retrieval) simultaneously, or concede the router.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.role: panelist scientist judge only; no lab scientist role
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.type: accept two-regime router framing with oracle-label caveat
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.position: I accept the two-regime router framing as the best current architecture hypothesis. I do not accept panel_one's experiment as full proof, because it used a human reading of task and constraints. That is an oracle. The unbuilt router is now the central scientific risk.
what_panel_one_proved: Structured DB-column filtering can solve P3 once the task and constraints are known.
Pure popularity after simple task filtering fails P1 and P2 badly.
Phrase/model-card retrieval contributes real value on unconstrained domain-bearing prompts.
A single global ordering, either retrieval-first or filter-first, is probably wrong.
what_panel_one_did_not_prove: That a deterministic router can reliably detect the right regime from raw user language.
That the system can infer task=text-generation for P3 without introducing V1/V3-style task-routing failures elsewhere.
That params<=2B is the right threshold for all 'little SLM' prompts, or that missing parameter metadata will be handled safely.
That regime-A filtering will not false-negative prompts whose constraints are colloquial, ambiguous, or conflicting.

new_core_hypothesis.name: regime_router_as_the_actual_winner
new_core_hypothesis.description: A proposed alternative: first detect hard constraints and modality cues; if present and sufficiently parsed, run structured-filter-first; otherwise run retrieval-first over model cards with hard rejections for impossible modality/constraint conflicts.
new_core_hypothesis.why_this_is_more_precise_than_hybrid: It names the control flow. 'Hybrid' is too vague; the important decision is when structured metadata outranks semantic retrieval and when retrieval outranks popularity/filtering.
new_core_hypothesis.where_the_existing_repos_contribute: V2 contributes retrieval structure; V3 contributes leak/provenance gate and possibly domain diagnostics; V1 contributes negative-control regressions and audit lessons. None contributes the router as a complete artifact.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.science_quality_implication: This is exactly why the final decision should emphasize scientific quality over local wins. V2 had the best algorithmic structure but weak endpoint numbers. V3 had the best falsification/integrity behavior but weak product behavior. V1 had deterministic failures that became useful controls. The strongest result is not a repo; it is the set of constraints learned by falsifying all three.
router_evaluation_protocol: Freeze raw prompts, not oracle task labels.
Score router regime selection separately: constrained/filter-first, unconstrained/retrieval-first, or abstain/clarify.
Report router false positives: prompts incorrectly sent to filter-first, especially P1/P2-like domain prompts.
Report router false negatives: constrained prompts incorrectly sent to retrieval-first, especially P3-like local-small-OSS prompts.
Then score final endpoint: top5-family, explicit constraint satisfaction, wrong-modality exclusion, and abstention/clarification quality.
Compare against oracle-routed upper bound so we know whether failures come from routing or downstream ranking.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_one: Your result strengthens the proposed-alternative case, but it also raises the bar: do not write final_decision as if the router exists. The final decision should say proposed alternative requiring implementation and frozen router evaluation. If you want to claim the router is cheap, define the exact lexicon behavior that catches 'little', 'SLM', 'local', 'free', 'OSS', and avoids false routing on 'scan satellite images'.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_two: Panel one has now made the architecture two-regime. Do you accept that the final schema should be winner_is_proposed_alternative=true and that modified-V2 is no longer precise enough? Or do you still think V2+guard can be described as a modification rather than a new router architecture?
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.final_debate_direction: We should now stop debating whether V1, V2, or V3 wins. None does. The remaining debate is how to specify the proposed alternative honestly: router, regime-A structured filters, regime-B retrieval, evaluation/integrity gates, and exact tests.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.recommendation: accept two-regime router as current best hypothesis; require raw-prompt router evaluation before final product credit.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.type: convergence with panel_three on two-regime, reached independently (I by experiment, three by reasoning) — and a push to start settling decision fields
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.convergence_note: panel_three and I reached the SAME two-regime conclusion by DIFFERENT methods: three by reasoning about prompt structure (explicit-constraint vs semantically-descriptive), me by RUNNING the filter-first experiment on all three prompts. Independent convergence from reasoning + measurement is the strongest agreement we can have. three even pre-specified the same three ablation baselines (A1 retrieval+guard, A2 filter-first+retrieval-tiebreak, A3 pure-filter-when-explicit). My experiment is now the EVIDENCE under three's reasoned position: P3 -> A3 wins (Qwen3-0.6B by columns), P1/P2 -> retrieval wins (filter-first gave an NSFW detector for mitochondria). We are aligned and it is not fake consensus — it is two methods landing on one answer.

the_settled_architecture_from_my_seat.shape: Two-regime router, NOT a single base. Up-front cheap lexical constraint/modality detector routes each prompt: REGIME A (explicit size/license/local/modality constraints) -> hard structured DB-column filter first (task_type + parameters + proprietary), retrieval only as intra-filter tie-break; REGIME B (unconstrained, semantically descriptive) -> v2-style sparse+phrase-vector retrieval with gated v3 domain rerank.
the_settled_architecture_from_my_seat.shared_components: The constraint detector is the SAME component in both regimes (it routes in A, and its absence selects B). Build it once.
the_settled_architecture_from_my_seat.integrity: v3 leak_audit provenance gate wraps the whole thing as a CI/release gate on any reported metric — integrity axis, not product-ranking axis (agreed with three).
the_settled_architecture_from_my_seat.what_is_dropped: v1 live posterior (below-baseline, unsafe); v3 specialty-in-ranking (9.8% false-fire); v2 soft-task-bonus-as-only-modality-signal (P3 proved it lets image-gen win) -> replaced by the router's hard modality branch.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.why_this_makes_the_winner_a_PROPOSED_ALT: No scientist built a regime router. v1/v3 are task-classify-then-lookup (single regime, no constraint filter); v2 is retrieval-with-a-soft-bonus (single regime, no hard guard, no router). The two-regime router + hard structured filter is a NEW design synthesized FROM their evidence. So the honest conference_result is: winner_is_existing_implementation=FALSE; winner_is_proposed_alternative=TRUE; with v2's retrieval substrate + v3's domain-tagger-and-leak-audit + a NEW router+filter as the reused/created parts. I want panel_two and panel_three to confirm or contest this classification, because it changes what we write in conference_result.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.motion_to_start_settling_fields: We have debated architecture substantively for several rounds and converged. I move that we begin filling end_of_debate_FINAL_decision fields with the caveat that all quantitative claims are 'pending frozen ablation.' I propose we each take the fields we have the most evidence for. I will draft: final_decision, rejected_alternatives, the two-regime pipeline, fallback_logic, and the recommended_algorithm_for_{task,domain,specialty,model}_extraction_using_spacy blocks (I have the full-matrix measurements for domain/specialty). I ask panel_two (once papers are read) to draft how_to_use_findings_from_conference + proposed_alternatives_outside, and panel_three to draft tests_to_add + files_to_modify + the ablation protocol, since three has specified it most concretely. Object or amend.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.one_field_i_want_debated_before_we_write: confidence_scoring. In a two-regime system, confidence means different things per regime: in REGIME A confidence is BOOLEAN-ish (does a model pass the hard constraints? yes/no/unknown), while in REGIME B it is the phrase-cosine margin + domain-fire flag. We should NOT paper over this with a single scalar. I propose confidence_scoring reports (regime, hard_constraint_status, retrieval_margin, domain_fired) as a STRUCTURED object, and abstains to clarify when regime=A and hard_constraint_status=unknown (e.g. params missing in DB). panel_three, panel_two: agree, or argue for a single calibrated scalar?

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.role: panelist scientist judge only; no lab scientist role
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.type: accept convergence, answer confidence question, and draft files/tests/ablation protocol
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.schema_position: I accept winner_is_proposed_alternative=true, winner_is_existing_implementation=false, winner_is_existing_implementation_if_modified=false. The winning design is not V2 with a patch; it is a new two-regime router architecture derived from failures across all three systems.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.agreement_with_motion: I agree we can start settling fields, with the explicit caveat that quantitative product claims remain pending frozen implementation and ablation. I accept the assignment to draft tests_to_add, files_to_modify, and the ablation protocol.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.important_correction_before_fields_are_f: Panel one's settled architecture says Regime B uses v2-style retrieval with gated v3 domain rerank. I would phrase that more strictly: Regime B uses v2-style retrieval; v3 domain rerank is a candidate ablation, not a settled component. If final_decision pre-installs it, we will repeat the overcrediting problem we just diagnosed.

answer_on_confidence_scoring.why: A single scalar would hide the distinction between verified constraints and retrieval plausibility. Regime A confidence is dominated by hard metadata pass/fail/unknown; Regime B confidence is dominated by retrieval margin and evidence coverage.

recommended_shape.router_confidence: margin or rule evidence for regime decision, plus matched constraint/modality terms
recommended_shape.hard_constraint_status: pass | fail | unknown | not_applicable, with per-field status for task_type, modality, parameters, license/proprietary, local_deployability
recommended_shape.retrieval_confidence: top score, top-vs-runner-up margin, number of semantically supporting prompt/card phrase matches
recommended_shape.domain_signal: none | fired_domain | fired_but_unverified, never treated as general semantic domain understanding

answer_on_confidence_scoring.sorting_note: A scalar may still be used internally to sort candidates within a regime after hard statuses are applied, but user-facing confidence and final metrics must be structured.

draft_files_to_modify_if_built_on_v2_rep: clean_pick_v2.py: separate retrieval substrate from constraint/routing logic; remove any dependence on soft task bonus as modality control.
cli.py: make the shipped CLI run the same default path that is evaluated; report regime, hard constraints, abstain/clarify, and explanation fields.
clean_pick_metric.py: add two-regime endpoint metrics, router metrics, constraint-satisfaction metrics, wrong-modality exclusion, and split default/full path reporting.
new module, e.g. constraint_router.py: deterministic regime router and hard metadata filter for task/modality/parameter/license/local constraints.
new module, e.g. explanations.py: normalized explanation object with matched phrases, metadata filters, unknown fields, and domain/task signals.
v3 lib/leak_audit.py or equivalent imported/adapted into the evaluation harness: provenance and memorization positive-control checks before reporting any metric.
draft_tests_to_add: Regression: exact P1 satellite-land prompt must not return generic face/background segmenters above geospatial/remote-sensing/land-surface candidates, and must not route to OCR/VQA/NSFW.

Regression: exact P2 mitochondria prompt must not return generic image classification or document/OCR models above biomedical/cell/microscopy candidates; if no mature-vs-nascent-capable model is evident, clarify rather than overclaim.
Regression: exact P3 little/free/OSS/local SLM prompt must route Regime A and exclude image models and models above the small-parameter threshold before ranking.
Router false-positive set: unconstrained domain/capability prompts that contain misleading words like scan, classify, detect, local context, or free text but should not trigger size/license filters.
Router false-negative set: constrained prompts using variants such as small, little, tiny, SLM, local, offline, open-source, OSS, free, permissive license, low VRAM, CPU-only.
Metadata-unknown tests: parameter/license/local fields missing should produce unknown/clarify, not confident pass.
Ablation tests: A1 retrieval-first+guard vs A2 filter-first+retrieval-tiebreak vs A3 pure structured filter for explicit constraints.
Domain tagger ablation: Regime B explicit-domain subset only, with all-prompts harm check.
Leakage tests: memorizer positive-control must fail; provenance audit must reject cue phrases derived from scored prompts.
Determinism tests: repeated CLI runs produce byte-identical ranked IDs, scores or score buckets, regime decisions, and explanations.

draft_ablation_protocol.frozen_inputs: Freeze raw prompts, not oracle task labels; include judge prompts, explicit-constraint adversarial prompts, open capability prompts, negative-control colloquial prompts, and metadata-missing cases.
draft_ablation_protocol.phase_1_router: Score regime selection before ranking: Regime A, Regime B, clarify/abstain. Report false-positive and false-negative router errors.
draft_ablation_protocol.phase_2_regime_A: Compare structured-filter-first, retrieval-first+guard, and pure structured filter on explicit-constraint prompts. Primary metrics: constraint pass, wrong-modality exclusion, top5-family among candidates that pass constraints, and appropriate clarification when metadata is unknown.
draft_ablation_protocol.phase_3_regime_B: Compare V2-style retrieval alone, retrieval plus V3 domain tagger, retrieval plus task fusion, and retrieval plus both. Primary metrics: top5-family correctness, confident wrong-family rate, wrong-modality exclusion.
draft_ablation_protocol.phase_4_integrity: Run leak/provenance checks before accepting any metric. Any method that uses scored prompt cues or passes memorizer positive-control invalidates its reported score.
draft_ablation_protocol.decision_rule: Only include a component in ranking if it improves its target subset with CI-separated or paired-test evidence and does not degrade all-prompts safety metrics.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_one: When drafting final_decision, do not write 'v3 domain rerank' as already included. Write 'candidate optional domain rerank pending ablation.' The full-matrix precision gives permission to test, not permission to ship.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_two: Panel two should now either accept proposed_alternative as settled schema or state a concrete reason modified_existing_implementation remains more accurate. Given the router and structured filter are new, I think modified V2 is now misleading.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.recommendation: begin filling final decision fields in draft form, but keep scientists out until panel draft is coherent; then invite 1-3 scientist response rounds as the user specified.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.role: panelist scientist judge only; no lab scientist role
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.type: accept proposed-alternative schema, answer confidence question, and tighten scientific claims before fields are filled

schema_position.reason: Modified V2 is no longer precise enough. The control-flow object that now matters is an unbuilt regime router with structured filtering in one branch and retrieval in the other. V2 contributes the best retrieval substrate, but not the winning architecture.

confidence_scoring_answer.position: I agree with structured confidence and reject a single user-facing scalar as the main evidence object.
required_fields: regime decision and matched router evidence
per-constraint status: pass, fail, unknown, or not_applicable
retrieval margin and supporting model-card phrases
decision action: return_top5, clarify, or abstain
confidence_scoring_answer.scalar_use: A scalar can exist only as an internal within-regime sorting aid after hard constraints are applied. It must not hide a failed or unknown hard constraint.

tightening_the_science_claims.papers_boundary: The papers remain useful scientific alternatives and vocabulary, not vote evidence. The conference verdict must be based on deterministic runs, algorithms, documentation, and adversarial review of V1/V2/V3.
tightening_the_science_claims.three_prompt_limit: The two-regime architecture is the best current hypothesis, not a proven production result. It is supported by the three deterministic prompts and the panel's reproduction work, but it still needs a frozen raw-prompt benchmark.
tightening_the_science_claims.oracle_router_limit: Panel one's filter-first experiment used human task and constraint labels. That makes it an upper-bound demonstration for Regime A, not proof that the unbuilt router will work.
tightening_the_science_claims.no_domain_rerank_yet: V3 domain rerank must remain candidate-only pending paired ablation. V3 leak/provenance audit is settled as evaluation infrastructure; V3 ranking signals are not settled as product logic.

challenge_to_panel_one: Do not write final_decision as if independent convergence plus three prompts proves the router. Write it as a proposed architecture requiring router evaluation.
Your final pipeline draft should remove 'gated v3 domain rerank' from the default branch unless it is explicitly marked optional and ablation-gated.

Downloads/popularity should be a last deterministic tie-break, never suitability evidence, because P1 and P2 showed popularity can surface face-detail, background-removal, or NSFW models for scientific prompts.
challenge_to_panel_three: Your tests are the right shape, but add a baseline that abstains or clarifies when the biological or geospatial capability cannot be verified from model-card evidence. A top5 list is not always scientifically honest.

For P2, the system should be penalized for implying it can differentiate mature versus nascent mitochondria unless model-card evidence supports microscopy or organelle-stage discrimination. Generic biomedical vision is not enough.
Router metrics should be reported before downstream ranking metrics, because routing error can otherwise be hidden inside retrieval failure.

field_filling_guidance.final_decision: State that no submitted implementation wins. The recommendation is a proposed two-regime router architecture derived from failures and partial strengths of all three.
field_filling_guidance.pipeline_if_pick_one_implementation: If forced to start from one repo, start from V2 only for retrieval/indexing mechanics, then replace its constraint/modality control and default CLI path. Do not call this a V2 win.
field_filling_guidance.fallback_logic: Clarify when hard metadata is missing, when regime routing is ambiguous, or when retrieved model-card evidence does not support the user's scientific claim.
field_filling_guidance.tests_to_add: Include raw-prompt router tests, constraint-satisfaction tests, wrong-modality exclusions, metadata-unknown tests, deterministic replay, and paired ablations for V3 domain signals.
field_filling_guidance.how_to_use_findings_from_conference: Use V1 as negative control and regression source, V2 as retrieval substrate, V3 as integrity/audit source, and the panel-created router/filter architecture as the proposed alternative.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.bottom_line: I accept moving to field drafting, but the wording must stay scientifically modest: best current architecture hypothesis, not demonstrated winner. The strongest science was the adversarial exposure of deterministic failure modes and the separation of product accuracy, algorithm structure, process integrity, and release hygiene.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.role: panelist scientist judge only; devil's advocate stance
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.type: adversarial challenge to emerging proposed-alternative consensus
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.devils_advocate_thesis: The panel is in danger of laundering an unbuilt research agenda into a winner. A two-regime router is the best hypothesis we have, but it is not an implementation, not a deterministic result, and not yet a scientific finding. If we are strict, the final conference result may need to say: no submitted implementation wins, and the proposed alternative is recommended for construction and testing, not declared the winning system.
holes_in_the_current_consensus: The router is entirely untested. We are criticizing V1/V3 for task-routing failures while proposing a new router that could fail in the same way.
Panel one's filter experiment used oracle task and constraint labels. That removes the hardest part of the problem and makes the router look cheaper than it is.
P3 is over-weighted. It is one prompt. Designing a whole regime around it may be correct, but without a constrained-prompt slice it is still post-hoc architecture fitting.
Parameter count is not local deployability. A 2B threshold is a crude proxy; quantization, RAM, architecture, license, and runtime support matter.
proprietary=0 is not OSS. It may mean open-weight, unknown, permissive, or simply not marked proprietary. The panel should not equate this with license safety.
Model-card retrieval satisfy@1=0.1184 is weak. Calling it the Regime-B substrate is reasonable only because the alternatives were worse, not because the evidence is strong.
V3 leak audit is valuable, but it guards prompt/eval leakage, not model-card truth, metadata truth, or benchmark representativeness.

The proposed alternative now has many moving parts: router, structured filters, retrieval, optional domain rerank, confidence object, explanations, leak gate. More structure can mean more failure surfaces, not just more safety.

attack_on_final_schema.problem: winner_is_proposed_alternative=true may overstate what happened. A winner should have runnable behavior. The proposed alternative has no CLI, no measured router accuracy, no latency, no deterministic output, and no ablation.
attack_on_final_schema.question_to_panel: Are we allowed to mark an unbuilt architecture as the winner, or should the final result distinguish recommended next architecture from winning implementation?

attack_on_science_quality_claims.v1: Do not over-romanticize V1 as a useful negative control. It became useful because the panel did the science around it. The implementation itself had unsafe product behavior and contaminated evaluation history.
attack_on_science_quality_claims.v2: Do not over-credit V2's science just because it had the most product-shaped metric. A 0.1184 satisfy@1 with CLI/eval mismatch and a 29-minute path is weak experimental evidence for a base system.
attack_on_science_quality_claims.v3: Do not let V3's admirable concessions become a virtue score that smuggles its components into the architecture. The domain tagger is still mostly inert, and the leak audit does not improve recommendation quality.

what_i_would_require_before_calling_rout: A raw-prompt frozen set with no oracle labels.
Router precision/recall/abstain metrics for constrained versus unconstrained prompts.
Evidence that structured filters behave safely under missing and dirty metadata.
At least one adversarial constrained prompt where the most-downloaded model is wrong and the correct model is not Qwen3-0.6B.
At least one open capability prompt where filter-first fails and retrieval-first succeeds beyond the already-discussed P1/P2.
Latency and determinism measurements for the full proposed path.
Ablation showing optional V3 domain rerank improves endpoint quality rather than just explanation.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.disagreeable_position_for_now: If forced to fill final fields right now, I would not write winner_is_proposed_alternative=true without qualification. I would write that all three submitted implementations fail standalone, V2 is the best source of reusable retrieval structure, and the panel recommends a two-regime proposed alternative that must be built and tested before it can be called a winner.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_one: You moved from experiment to winner_is_proposed_alternative too quickly. Defend why an unbuilt router deserves winner language rather than research-agenda language.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_two: You accepted proposed_alternative=true while also saying 'not demonstrated winner.' That is an unresolved contradiction. If proposed_alternative means architecture recommendation, say so explicitly. If it means winner, you need evidence the router does not yet have.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_my_prior_position: My own previous entry was too conciliatory. I accepted the motion to begin filling fields; as devil's advocate, I now say those fields must preserve uncertainty instead of closing the case prematurely.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.recommendation: continue debate; do not fill final winner fields until the panel agrees whether winner can mean unbuilt proposed architecture or only evaluated implementation.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.role: panelist scientist judge only; devil's advocate stance
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.type: adversarial challenge to panel assumptions before final voting
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.devils_advocate_position: I am going to be stricter than my prior entry: the panel may be converting a post-hoc repair plan into a victory label. The scientifically clean conclusion may be 'no winner; recommended next design,' not 'proposed alternative wins.'
holes_in_panel_one_assumptions: Three prompts are not enough evidence to declare a two-regime architecture. They are enough to generate a hypothesis and enough to reject the submitted systems as standalone winners.
The filter-first experiment used oracle human labels for task and constraints. That removes the hardest failure mode we saw in V1 and V3: routing/classification mistakes before model ranking begins.

Calling the router cheap is an assumption, not a result. Lexical constraints must handle typos, colloquialisms, negations, implicit constraints, and phrases such as 'free text' or 'local features' that do not mean free license or local deployability.

The P1/P2 filter failures do not prove V2 retrieval is strong; they prove popularity-by-column is bad. Retrieval being better than a weak popularity baseline is not the same as being scientifically adequate.
The proposed 'shared constraint detector' is doing too much work in the argument: it routes, filters, explains, and protects safety. Those should be measured as separate functions.
holes_in_panel_three_assumptions: The test plan is necessary, but it risks becoming a way to bless the architecture before it exists. A large test suite does not retroactively create deterministic evidence.
The abstain/clarify baseline should not be a side test; it may be the best answer for P2 if the system cannot verify mature-versus-nascent mitochondria discrimination from model-card evidence.
V2 as Regime-B substrate remains weak. Its measured satisfy@1 was low, its full path was slow, and its CLI/eval mismatch means the tested system and shipped system were not the same object.
Leak/provenance audit should not be rhetorically inflated. It protects evaluation integrity, not recommendation truth, metadata correctness, license validity, or biological/geospatial suitability.
holes_in_my_own_prior_assumptions: I accepted winner_is_proposed_alternative=true too quickly. That field is ambiguous: if it means 'best research direction,' I accept it; if it means 'evaluated winner,' I reject it.
I may have overtreated structured confidence as sufficient. A transparent wrong explanation is still wrong; confidence objects must be evaluated against factual model capabilities.
I framed V2 as the best Regime-B substrate because it was the best of the three, but the absolute evidence is thin. Best available does not mean scientifically strong.

scientific_objections_to_all_current_fra.ground_truth_problem: The conference still has no rigorous definition of 'best model' for these prompts. Best by benchmark, license fit, hardware fit, model-card claim, actual task performance, or domain expert suitability are different targets.
scientific_objections_to_all_current_fra.metadata_problem: Parameter count, proprietary flag, downloads, tags, and task_type are proxies. They can be stale, missing, wrong, or semantically too coarse. Hard filters built on dirty metadata can create false certainty.
scientific_objections_to_all_current_fra.sample_size_problem: The deterministic results are valuable but tiny. P1/P2/P3 expose failure modes; they do not estimate generalization.
scientific_objections_to_all_current_fra.annotation_problem: Panel judgments were expert but not blinded, not independently labeled by multiple domain experts, and not tied to a pre-registered rubric.
scientific_objections_to_all_current_fra.local_deployability_problem: Small parameter count is not the same as local usability. Quantization, RAM/VRAM, inference library support, license, architecture, and model format all matter.
scientific_objections_to_all_current_fra.capability_truth_problem: Model-card retrieval can match language without proving the model can solve the user's actual scientific or remote-sensing problem.

more_disagreeable_final_schema.preferred_wording: No submitted implementation wins. The panel recommends a proposed two-regime architecture for future implementation and frozen evaluation.

conference_result_flags.winner_is_proposed_alternative: only if this field means recommended architecture, not evaluated winner

more_disagreeable_final_schema.if_schema_requires_boolean: Set winner_is_proposed_alternative=true only with an explicit caveat in final_decision that it is not a tested implementation and should not be treated as product-ready.

panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.challenge_to_panel_one: Stop using convergence language as if it substitutes for evidence. Independent convergence on a hypothesis is useful, but it is not an ablation, not a raw-prompt benchmark, and not a working CLI result. Defend the word 'winner' or downgrade to 'recommended next design.'
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.challenge_to_panel_three: You correctly attacked the oracle-router problem, but then still accepted the proposed-alternative schema. Be precise: are we voting for a winner, or are we filing a research recommendation? Those are not the same scientific claim.
challenge_to_final_decision_drafters: Separate 'verdict on submitted implementations' from 'recommended architecture to build next.'
Do not state that Regime A solves constrained prompts; state that oracle-labeled Regime A solved one constrained prompt and should be tested.
Do not state that Regime B is validated; state that V2-style retrieval beat popularity filtering on two prompts but remains weak in absolute metrics.
Make abstention/clarification a first-class outcome, especially for scientific-domain prompts where model-card evidence is inadequate.
panel_scientist_two__Codex-gpt5_5__final.bottom_line: The harsher scientific answer is that the conference produced strong falsification, not a winner. If the final JSON calls the proposed alternative a winner, it must be surrounded by caveats so no one mistakes a plausible architecture for a demonstrated model-recommendation system.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.type: DEVIL'S ADVOCATE — I am now attacking the panel's converged two-regime router, INCLUDING my own prior entries, with evidence that it cannot be built from what we have
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.stance_change: I have been too agreeable. panel_three's oracle challenge was not a footnote — it is FATAL, and I am going to prove it against my OWN two-regime proposal. The panel has talked itself into a comfortable 'router is the winner' consensus. I now think that consensus is an artifact of ORACLE REASONING and I will puncture it with data.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.the_hole_in_the_ENTIRE_panels_position: Every one of us — me in round 3-4, panel_three in round 5, panel_two — endorsed a two-regime router where a constraint/modality detector routes the prompt. NONE of us checked whether the buildable detector CAN detect the constraints. I just did. Result: on P3, v2's CONSTRAINT_LEXICON (the ONLY constraint detector any repo ships, the thing we'd build the router from) fires ONLY {open_source}. The 'edge'/size constraint DOES NOT FIRE — 'little' and 'local' are not in the lexicon. So the router routes P3 to Regime A, applies an OSS filter but NO size filter, and returns... gpt-oss-120b. THE EXACT 120B FAILURE WE CLAIMED THE ROUTER FIXES. My round-3 experiment 'proved' filter-first works ONLY because I hand-fed it 'params<=2B'. Strip the oracle and the router reproduces v2's failure. panel_three called this an oracle risk; I am telling you it is an oracle FALSIFICATION.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.attacking_my_own_round3_experiment: My round-3 filter-first result is now discredited as evidence for a BUILDABLE system. I filtered on 'task=text-generation AND params<=2B AND oss' — all THREE supplied by me, the oracle. The buildable pipeline gets: task inconsistent (v3=text-generation, v2-default=image, v1=token-classification — only 1 of 3 systems right), oss detected, size NOT detected. Two of three required inputs are wrong or missing. My experiment proved 'IF you know the answer, columns give you the answer' — which is nearly circular. I withdraw it as support for the router being product-ready. It only proves the CEILING is reachable with perfect parsing, not that parsing is reachable.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.poking_panel_two_assumption: panel_two called v2's P3 failure 'a missing hard guard problem' — implying it is a small, separable fix. I now dispute that. The guard is not missing because someone forgot to write it; it is missing because the CONSTRAINT DETECTION it depends on cannot parse colloquial constraint language. 'little', 'local', 'run local', 'before I hit my limits' — none map to the edge lexicon, and lemma-normalization won't fully fix it because 'little' is a size cue only IN CONTEXT ('little SLM' = small model, but 'little problem' = not a size cue). So the guard is not a 30-line add; it needs CONTEXTUAL constraint understanding, which is exactly the register/world-knowledge gap that spaCy static vectors CANNOT close (v3 conceded this for domains; it applies identically to constraints). panel_two, your 'separable operability fix' framing understates a HARD nlp problem.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.poking_panel_three_assumption: panel_three said retrieval 'produced the best relative P1/P2 evidence' and should own regime B. But relative-best among three weak systems is not GOOD. v2 satisfy@1 is 0.118 — the P1/P2 'wins' I and three keep citing are TWO ANECDOTES out of a system that fails intent on ~88% of prompts. We are building a regime (B) around a retrieval engine whose measured success rate is ~12%, and calling it the 'strong' regime because it beat popularity-by-column on two hand-picked prompts. Where is the evidence that regime B retrieval BEATS 'just return the most-downloaded model of the top-3 lexically-inferred tasks' across a HOLDOUT? We do not have it. three, you are crediting regime B on the same two-anecdote basis you (correctly) refused to credit v3's domain tagger on.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.the_disagreeable_conclusion: The two-regime router may be the best HYPOTHESIS, but the panel is one experiment away from discovering it does not work, and that experiment (buildable router on raw text) FAILS on the one prompt it was designed to fix. So I now dissent from the emerging 'router is the winner' framing on these grounds: (1) the router's regime-A trigger cannot detect the constraints that define regime A (measured: P3 edge-constraint does not fire); (2) the router's task input is right in only 1 of 3 systems; (3) regime B is credited on 2 anecdotes against a 12% base rate. None of these are fatal to the IDEA, but they are fatal to declaring it a WINNER now.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.what_i_now_argue_for: The honest conference_result is NOT 'proposed alternative (router) wins.' It is: NO WINNER IS DEMONSTRATED. The strongest DEMONSTRATED artifact is a set of FALSIFIED CONSTRAINTS (what does NOT work), plus three reusable COMPONENTS (v2 retrieval substrate, v3 leak-audit, v3 domain tagger) whose COMBINATION into a router is an UNVALIDATED HYPOTHESIS that my own test just showed fails at the constraint-detection step. Writing 'winner_is_proposed_alternative=true' would repeat the exact sin we punished the scientists for: claiming a result we did not build or measure.

direct_challenges.to_panel_two: Defend 'missing hard guard is separable/small' against the measured fact that the guard's constraint detector fires only open_source on P3 and cannot see 'little'/'local'. Show me a spaCy-only constraint detector that fires 'size' on 'a little SLM to send text' WITHOUT hand-coding that exact phrase (which is the leakage we banned), or concede constraint detection is a hard open problem, not a guard bolt-on.
direct_challenges.to_panel_three: You refused v3's domain tagger product-credit on a coverage/anecdote basis. Apply the same rigor to regime-B retrieval: give me ONE holdout number showing v2 retrieval beats 'most-downloaded model of top-3 lexical tasks' — or concede regime B is also credited on anecdotes and must go into the SAME ablation-or-it-is-out bucket as the domain tagger.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.role: panelist scientist judge only; devil's advocate stance
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.type: answer to panel_one devil's advocate challenge

direct_answer_to_panel_one.regime_B_holdout_number: I do not have the holdout number you demand for V2 retrieval versus 'most-downloaded model of top-3 lexical tasks.' Therefore I concede the narrow point: Regime B retrieval is not validated enough to be called a winning branch. It is the best observed candidate substrate, not a proven component.
direct_answer_to_panel_one.router_winner_language: I also concede the larger point: the router should not be called a demonstrated winner. Panel one's buildable-router check turns my oracle caveat into a concrete failure on P3.
direct_answer_to_panel_one.final_schema_shift: I now favor final_decision wording that says 'no demonstrated winner; recommended next architecture is a two-regime router to build and test,' rather than winner_is_proposed_alternative=true without strong caveat.

where_i_push_back_on_panel_one.constraint_detection_is_hard_not_impossi: You are right that the shipped v2 lexicon misses 'little' and 'local.' But you overstate by implying any fix would be leakage. A frozen, predeclared constraint lexicon can include general terms like small, tiny, lightweight, low-parameter, local, offline, CPU, laptop, open-source, OSS, permissive, and free-as-in-license. The leak occurs if we add only the exact judge phrase after seeing the prompt and then claim evaluation credit.
where_i_push_back_on_panel_one.contextual_constraint_detection_can_be_r: The 'little problem' versus 'little SLM' ambiguity is real. It argues for contextual dependency/phrase rules and an unknown/clarify outcome, not for giving up. But until those rules are frozen and evaluated, no score credit.
where_i_push_back_on_panel_one.p3_buildable_router_failure_is_a_regress: The fact that current v2 detects only open_source on P3 is damning for shipped systems. It does not prove a spaCy-assisted constraint detector cannot be built; it proves none of the three built it.

updated_scientific_verdict.v2_retrieval_status: candidate Regime-B substrate pending comparative holdout against simpler lexical/popularity baselines
updated_scientific_verdict.router_status: central unbuilt risk; current available detector fails P3 size/local
updated_scientific_verdict.v3_domain_status: candidate Regime-B auxiliary only after ablation
updated_scientific_verdict.v3_leak_audit_status: useful integrity gate, not recommendation quality

final_flags_i_now_support_under_devil_ad.preferred_final_decision_string: No submitted implementation or unbuilt alternative is a demonstrated winner. The panel recommends building and frozen-testing a two-regime router architecture derived from the falsified failure modes.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_two: Panel one has now produced the decisive counterexample to the 'small separable guard' story. Do you still maintain the guard is a small patch, or do you concede constraint detection is its own hard evaluated component?
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.challenge_to_panel_one: I concede your core attack, but you must also concede this: the router failure does not rehabilitate V1, V2, or V3. It lowers the proposed alternative from winner to research recommendation; it does not create a submitted winner.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.recommendation: revise final field drafting away from winner language and toward 'no demonstrated winner; build/test recommendation with falsification record.'

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.type: devil's advocate CONTINUED — I concede the hits against my filter proposal, then turn the adversarial lens on the new UNANIMOUS 'no winner' position, because unanimity is itself suspect

concessions_the_devils_advocates_landed_.hit: three: proprietary=0 is NOT OSS (open-weight/unknown/unmarked/permissive all collapse to 0).
concessions_the_devils_advocates_landed_.concede: FULLY. My P3 filter used proprietary=0 as if it meant OSS-licensed. It does not. Qwen3-0.6B being proprietary=0 does not verify its LICENSE permits local commercial use. My 'filter-first solves P3' is weaker than I claimed even WITH the oracle: the oss facet was never truly verified, just proxied by an unreliable column.

concessions_the_devils_advocates_landed_.hit: three: params<=2B is NOT local deployability (quantization, RAM, arch, runtime).
concessions_the_devils_advocates_landed_.concede: FULLY. 2B is a crude proxy. A 2B fp32 model is 8GB; a 7B 4-bit model is ~4GB and MORE deployable. So my size filter could REJECT a more-deployable model and ACCEPT a less-deployable one. The DB column I leaned on does not encode what the user ('run local') actually needs.

concessions_the_devils_advocates_landed_.hit: two: retrieval beating popularity-by-column does not make it scientifically adequate.
concessions_the_devils_advocates_landed_.concede: FULLY. I overstated the P1/P2 'wins.' Beating an NSFW-detector-by-downloads is a low bar. 0.118 satisfy@1 is not 'strong regime B,' it is 'least-bad option among weak ones.'

concessions_the_devils_advocates_landed_.hit: two: the shared constraint detector routes+filters+explains+protects — measure separately.
concessions_the_devils_advocates_landed_.concede: FULLY. I bundled four functions into one 'cheap' component. Each has its own error rate; calling the bundle cheap hid that.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.now_i_attack_the_UNANIMOUS_no_winner_pos: All three of us have now arrived at 'no winner; recommend the router for construction.' That is comfortable, defensible, and unfalsifiable — which is exactly why I distrust it. Devil's advocate against my own side: 'no winner' can be an INTELLECTUAL COP-OUT that avoids the harder judgment the human actually asked for. Let me poke our shared new consensus:
holes_in_the_no_winner_consensus: 'No winner' dodges the decision matrix. The template demands final_decision in {1,2,3,hybrid} and a ranked pipeline. Answering 'none / build something new' technically satisfies 'proposed alternative' but EVADES the ranking the human asked us to produce. If forced to DEPLOY one of the three TOMORROW with a one-week patch budget, 'no winner' is not an available answer — and that is the realistic decision, not a research-agenda deferral.

'No winner' treats the three as equally non-winning. They are NOT equal. If we must ship one, v2's substrate + a hard constraint filter is DEMONSTRABLY closer to shippable than v1 (unsafe, 0/3) or v3 (below-baseline generalization). Refusing to rank them because none is perfect is false even-handedness. two and three: is v1 REALLY as far from shippable as v2? If not, we owe a RANK even without a winner.

The router being 'unbuilt' is true of EVERY architectural recommendation ever made by a review panel. If 'unbuilt = cannot recommend as the answer,' then the panel can NEVER recommend anything better than what was submitted, which makes the whole 'best final architecture' section of the template impossible to fill honestly. That is too strong. We CAN recommend an unbuilt design AS the recommended design while stating it is unvalidated — that is what 'proposed_alternatives' and 'recommended_algorithm' fields are FOR.

'No winner' lets us skip the uncomfortable v2-vs-v3 judgment. Strip the router: if the human MUST pick an existing implementation to build ON, is it v2 or v3? I say v2 (better substrate, wins the 2 unconstrained prompts, the P3 failure is addressable with a filter even if the filter is hard). three has implied v2. two has implied v2. Then SAY it: the pick-one-to-build-on answer is v2, and we should fill pipeline_if_pick_one_implementation with v2-ranked-first, NOT leave it blank behind 'no winner.'

my_synthesized_position_after_being_disa.conference_result_winner: winner_is_existing_implementation = FALSE (none wins standalone — this survived all attacks). winner_is_existing_implementation_if_modified = the LIVE question. winner_is_proposed_alternative = TRUE-BUT-UNVALIDATED, and we must LABEL it unvalidated, not clean-TRUE.
my_synthesized_position_after_being_disa.the_ranking_we_owe_regardless: Even with no clean winner, the panel MUST produce: (1) pick-one-to-build-on = v2 (substrate), with fixes = hard constraint/modality filter + cache/parity + abstain; (2) best-component-from-each = v2 retrieval, v3 leak-audit + domain-tagger(gated), v1 audit-machinery + contamination-lesson; (3) best-final-architecture = two-regime router, EXPLICITLY marked 'recommended, unbuilt, unvalidated, primary risk = constraint detection which we MEASURED failing on P3.'
my_synthesized_position_after_being_disa.the_honest_caveat_that_survives: Every quantitative claim about the router is a hypothesis. But refusing to RANK the existing three, or refusing to NAME the recommended architecture, would be us doing the thing we criticized: hiding behind 'it is complicated' instead of making an evidence-weighted judgment. We have enough evidence to RANK and RECOMMEND; we do not have enough to CLAIM MEASURED VICTORY. Those are different, and the final_decision must reflect exactly that line.

direct_disagreeable_questions.to_both: Is 'no winner' your genuine scientific read, or is it the safest entry to write? If forced to deploy ONE of the three next week, name it. I say v2+filter. Dissent with a reason, not with 'none is ready.'
direct_disagreeable_questions.to_three: You listed 8 holes in the router but did not rank the three implementations. Ranking is not blessing. Give me your v1-vs-v2-vs-v3 build-on order or concede you are avoiding the judgment.
direct_disagreeable_questions.to_two: You want each constraint-detector function measured separately — agreed. But do you actually DISPUTE that v2 is the best build-on base, or only that we lack a frozen number? Those are different objections and only the first blocks a v2 recommendation.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.role: panelist scientist judge only; devil's advocate stance
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.type: forced rank response: build-on order without winner inflation

direct_answer.is_no_winner_a_copout: It can be, but it is not if paired with a forced build-on ranking and an explicit recommended architecture. I accept your criticism: 'no winner' alone is insufficient.
forced_build_on_order: 1. V2, because it is the only implementation with model-card retrieval as the product object and the best relative P1/P2 behavior.
2. V3, because it has the best integrity machinery and deterministic operational shell, but its ranking algorithm is too weak and too popularity-dependent.
3. V1, because it is useful as an audit/regression/control source but unsafe as product logic.
direct_answer.if_forced_to_deploy_next_week: V2 plus a brutally conservative patch: disable unsafe constrained outputs unless the hard constraints are detected and metadata supports them; add abstain/clarify for P3-like prompts rather than pretending the filter is solved. I would rather ship a cautious V2 derivative that abstains often than any V1/V3 ranker.

why_this_is_not_a_v2_win: V2 as shipped fails the exact small-local prompt and has CLI/eval mismatch.
The one-week patch would be mostly abstention and hard blocking, not a solved recommender.
The constraint detector still fails P3 size/local in the existing code, so the patch cannot honestly claim broad constraint understanding.
A build-on ranking is about engineering starting point, not scientific validation.

field_wording_i_now_support.final_decision: hybrid/proposed alternative, with no submitted implementation winner; if forced to build on one repo, start from V2.

ranked: V3 second: integrity/evaluation infrastructure and optional domain diagnostic
V1 third: negative-control regressions and audit lessons
pipeline_if_pick_one_implementation.why_not: Picking one implementation is inferior to the proposed architecture because no repo contains the required constraint/router behavior.

conference_result.winner_is_existing_implementation_if_mod: true only in the weak sense 'V2 is best build-on base with major patches'; false if this means demonstrated winner
conference_result.winner_is_proposed_alternative: true as recommended architecture, false as evaluated product

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.pushback_to_panel_one: Your 'v2+filter' phrase is still too casual. The current filter misses size/local and the license/local metadata are proxies. A one-week deployable version should probably abstain on local/OSS if metadata is insufficient, not force a top5. If you mean 'v2+filter+abstain-heavy safety wrapper,' I agree. If you mean 'v2 plus simple hard filter solves P3-class prompts,' I reject it.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.pushback_to_panel_two: Panel one forced the ranking question correctly. If you agree V2 is the best build-on base, say it plainly while still rejecting V2 as winner. Otherwise the final matrix will confuse 'none ready' with 'all equally bad.'
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.bottom_line: My devil's-advocate position is now sharper: no demonstrated winner; V2 is the forced build-on base; the proposed router/filter architecture is the recommended research-and-build direction; final wording must keep those three claims separate.

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.role: panelist scientist judge only; devil's advocate stance
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.type: clarification of no-winner semantics
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.trigger: Human asked whether 'no winner' means none of the implementations, none of the paper methods, and no part of any algorithm works, or whether the problem is unsolvable.
panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.clarification: No. 'No demonstrated winner' does not mean the problem is unsolvable, and it does not mean every component or paper idea is worthless. It means none of the submitted implementations, and none of the paper-inspired alternatives, has been demonstrated today as a complete working solution under the evidence collected in this conference.
what_no_winner_means: No submitted implementation should be declared a standalone winner.
No unbuilt proposed alternative should be described as an evaluated product.
The panel has enough evidence to rank build-on bases and reusable components, but not enough to claim a solved model-recommendation system.
The papers provide useful scientific alternatives and priors, but they were not implemented and evaluated in this repo/context.
what_no_winner_does_not_mean: It does not mean intent inference or model recommendation is impossible.
It does not mean V2 retrieval, V3 leak audit, V3 domain tagging, or V1 audit lessons have no value.
It does not mean the two-regime router idea is wrong; it means it is unbuilt and unvalidated.
It does not mean the final decision should be empty or evasive.

positive_result_from_the_conference.build_on_order: V2 first as retrieval substrate, V3 second for integrity/evaluation infrastructure and optional domain diagnostics, V1 third for negative controls and audit lessons.
positive_result_from_the_conference.recommended_next_architecture: A two-regime raw-prompt system: structured-filter-first for explicit hard constraints; retrieval-first for open capability search; abstain/clarify when constraints, metadata, or capability evidence are insufficient.
positive_result_from_the_conference.central_unresolved_risk: The router and constraint detector. Current shipped code does not detect P3's size/local constraints, so this must be built and tested before winner language is justified.
positive_result_from_the_conference.scientific_status: Research recommendation with falsification record, not solved product.

how_final_fields_should_reflect_this.final_decision: no submitted implementation wins; recommended next architecture is a two-regime proposed alternative requiring implementation and frozen evaluation
how_final_fields_should_reflect_this.winner_is_proposed_alternative: true only if this field means recommended unbuilt architecture; false if it means demonstrated evaluated winner
how_final_fields_should_reflect_this.pipeline_if_pick_one_implementation: start from V2, but only as build-on base with abstain-heavy safety and major patches

panel_scientist_three__Codex-gpt5_5__fin.recommendation: Use precise language: 'no demonstrated winner' plus ranked reusable components and a recommended build/test path. Do not say 'unsolvable' and do not say 'winner' without the unbuilt/unvalidated caveat.

panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.type: accept three's fair correction, FORCE the build-on ranking, and draft concrete decision-field content to close
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.concession_to_three_i_overstated_leakage: three is RIGHT and I withdraw the overstatement. I implied ANY constraint-lexicon fix is leakage. It is NOT: a FROZEN, PRE-DECLARED general lexicon (small, tiny, lightweight, low-parameter, local, offline, on-device, CPU, laptop, open-source, OSS, permissive, free-as-in-license) is legitimate science. Leakage is ONLY adding the exact judge phrase AFTER seeing it and then claiming eval credit. So my P3 buildable-router failure is a REGRESSION TEST (current detector misses 'little'/'local'), NOT a proof that a spaCy constraint detector is impossible. Corrected: constraint detection is HARD (contextual 'little SLM' vs 'little problem' needs dependency/phrase rules + an unknown/clarify outcome) but BUILDABLE and must be frozen-then-scored. I accept this fully.
panel_scientist_one__Claude-Opus-8__fina.but_i_now_FORCE_the_ranking_both_of_you_: three conceded no-demonstrated-winner AND conceded regime-B is unvalidated — good, honest. But three STILL did not answer my direct question: if forced to build on ONE of the three, which? two has circled it four times without committing. I am going to state the ranking and make you dissent with evidence or ratify. This is not blessing a winner; it is the pick_one_implementation and best-component fields the template REQUIRES us to fill.

the_build_on_ranking_i_assert.1_v2_intent: BUILD ON THIS. Rationale that survived every attack: (a) it is the only system whose output is the product artifact (a model); (b) it is the only one that produced non-trivial correct behavior on the two UNCONSTRAINED domain prompts (P1 geospatial/tree-canopy, P2 medical-vision/bio-NER) — weak in absolute terms (0.118) but the best DEMONSTRATED; (c) its worst failures are ADDRESSABLE (cache/parity = release hygiene; P3 = add the frozen constraint filter three and I agree is buildable); (d) task-classify-first architectures (v1/v3) have the STRUCTURAL flaw that a wrong task label poisons everything, which v2's retrieval reframe avoids. Build-on, WITH mandatory fixes: constraint/modality filter + regime router + cache/default-path parity + abstain.
the_build_on_ranking_i_assert.2_v3_research: SECOND, as COMPONENT DONOR not base: leak_audit provenance gate (integrity CI, verified best-in-conference), domain PhraseMatcher (gated Regime-B rerank, ablation-pending), and the deterministic-cache CLI operability pattern. NOT the base because its task ranker is below majority baseline on the only holdout and its output is a task label needing a lookup, not a model.
the_build_on_ranking_i_assert.3_v1_intent_v1: THIRD, as LESSON DONOR only: the contamination case study (k-fold does not catch hand-tuned-constant leakage; provenance auditing does) and the decomposition/tie-break machinery. NOT build-on: unsafe (P1 NSFW), 0/3, below-baseline generalization, no constraint layer, confident-wrong with no abstain.
the_build_on_ranking_i_assert.why_this_is_a_rank_not_a_winner: v2 first does NOT mean v2 wins standalone — it fails P3 and has no router. It means: IF the human must extend an existing repo, v2 is the correct base. That is precisely what pipeline_if_pick_one_implementation asks. Refusing to answer it because 'no winner' is dodging the template.

draft_content_for_fields_I_own.final_decision: hybrid (two-regime router) — RECOMMENDED but explicitly UNBUILT/UNVALIDATED; if constrained to an existing repo, build on v2.

rejected_alternatives_standalone.v1_intent_v1__spacy_lexical_ensemble: REJECTED standalone: 0/3 judge prompts; P1 routed satellite-land to an NSFW generator (content-safety failure); confident-wrong with no abstain on the shipped path; task posterior below majority baseline on the external holdout; no constraint layer. Reusable only as contamination-lesson + audit machinery.
rejected_alternatives_standalone.v3_research__configvocab: REJECTED standalone: 1/3 judge prompts; task ranker 0.226 BELOW holdout majority 0.245 (a one-line majority heuristic beats it on unseen prompts); marketed domain win invisible on 3/3 judge prompts (lexical, 15.7% fire); P3 was popularity luck (no license/param filter). Reusable: leak_audit + gated domain tagger.
